title: Vendor protoc-gen-go-grpc stub generator from grpc-go (Apache 2.0)
description:
## Why

wiresmith today delegates service-stub generation to protoc-gen-go-grpc, which forces every adopter to install and invoke two separate codegen binaries (see Makefile lines around `--go-grpc_out`). Bringing stub emission into wiresmith gives a single-command codegen experience.

The official protoc-gen-go-grpc generator cannot be imported as a library — `google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0/grpc.go` is `package main` (~635 LOC, Apache 2.0). Three real paths exist (subprocess the binary, vendor the source, write our own). Per direction from the wiresmith maintainer thread on 2026-06-05, this bead picks **vendor**: a single self-contained wiresmith binary owns the entire output.

The vendored file remains byte-identical to upstream where possible so future upstream syncs are a straight `cp` plus header reconciliation.

## What

1. Add `compiler/generator/grpc/` housing the vendored `grpc.go`. Preserve the Apache 2.0 licence header verbatim; add SPDX-License-Identifier. Update repo NOTICE / THIRD_PARTY (or create one mirroring `protohelpers/` precedent — that directory vendors vtprotobuf protohelpers under Apache 2.0, see how it documents attribution).
2. wiresmith uses `bufbuild/protocompile`, while the upstream generator expects `protogen.Plugin` / `protogen.File` / `protogen.GeneratedFile`. Bridge by either:
   - (a) Synthesising a `pluginpb.CodeGeneratorRequest` from wiresmith descriptors, running `protogen.Options{}.New(req)`, and calling the vendored `generateFileContent` unchanged. Minimal-edit path — preserves zero-cost upstream sync.
   - (b) Rewriting the vendored generator to talk to wiresmith descriptor types directly. Drops the protogen dep but loses sync ergonomics. Prefer (a) unless it hits a concrete obstacle.
3. Emit `*_grpc.pb.go` alongside existing wiresmith outputs, matching the split-file convention used by `_compare.pb.go` / `_equal.pb.go` / `_reflect.pb.go`. Trigger only when `len(file.Services) > 0`.
4. Wire into the wiresmith CLI / generator entry point and remove the standalone `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocation from the Makefile once the new path is verified.
5. Rerun `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` against the wiresmith-emitted stubs — every test must pass (existing live-roundtrip suite: unary / client-stream / server-stream / bidi-stream / GetServiceInfo).

## Acceptance

- `gen/basic/service/v1/service_grpc.pb.go` is produced by `make generate-ours` with no `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocation required.
- All five tests in `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` pass against the wiresmith-emitted stubs.
- Generated `service_grpc.pb.go` is byte-equivalent (or diff limited to file banner / package decoration) to what `protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0` emits today.
- `protoc-gen-go-grpc` dropped from Makefile and any install instructions / docs.
- Apache 2.0 licence header preserved verbatim in the vendored file; repo licence attribution updated.

## Out of scope

- Re-including `fdp.Service` in the embedded raw FileDescriptor — that is wiresmith-ea4 (descriptor-level reflection, separate concern). Note: see DESIGN below for the open question on whether ea4 must land first.
- Customising the gRPC codepath (mempool, Alloc-from-pool, custom codec à la vtprotobuf). Defer until a concrete user need exists.
- Tracking SupportPackageIsVersionN bumps. Stay pinned to v1.6.0 contract until forced to bump.

## Design open question

Once wiresmith emits its own `_grpc.pb.go`, the registration init() may need matching service entries in the embedded raw FileDescriptor — at minimum to avoid protoimpl.filetype.Builder.initDecls cardinality panics if the gRPC stubs reference protoreflect descriptors. If verification shows the panic triggers, this bead also un-strips `fdp.Service` (effectively subsuming wiresmith-ea4). If not, ea4 stays independent. Worth verifying early with a quick spike against the vendored generator output before committing to either dep direction.

## References

- `google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0/grpc.go` — source to vendor (package main, Apache 2.0, ~635 LOC).
- `compiler/generator/emit_registration.go::serializeFileDescriptor` — current `fdp.Service = nil` strip site (left alone here; see Design open question above).
- `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go` — live-roundtrip suite that validates new stubs.
- `Makefile` — `--go-grpc_out` invocation that this bead replaces.
- Competitor analysis: PR #105 (wiresmith-dei) and the wiresmith repo conversation on 2026-06-05 (vendor-vs-subprocess decision).
notes:
Design open question resolved: stripping fdp.Service in serializeFileDescriptor does NOT trigger a protoimpl.filetype.Builder.initDecls panic. Verified by TestGRPCProtocCompat_ServiceInfo (passes against wiresmith-emitted stubs). Reason: the gRPC stubs use grpc.ServiceDesc registration via Server.GetServiceInfo, independent of protoreflect — they reference message types whose ProtoReflect() lives in wiresmith but never walk service entries on the file descriptor. wiresmith-ea4 (un-strip fdp.Service for descriptor-level reflection) stays an independent bead.
Output diff vs upstream protoc-gen-go-grpc@v1.6.0 (3 lines, all in non-code regions): (1) banner protoc version 'v7.35.0' → '(unknown)' (no CompilerVersion synthesized in CodeGeneratorRequest), (2) banner 'source:' path 'service.proto' → 'basic/service/v1/service.proto', (3) Echo_ServiceDesc.Metadata field same path change. (2) and (3) reflect wiresmith's synthetic import-key path (proto-package-derived) vs protoc's invocation-relative path. Metadata is informational (used by grpc reflection responses); behavioral impact verified zero by the 5 test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go tests.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/116
close_reason: implemented

title: Loki migration
description:
## Goal

Replace `protoc-gen-gogoslick` (and `protoc-gen-go-grpc` for some packages) as the protobuf compiler for [grafana/loki](https://github.com/grafana/loki) with `wiresmith`. This enables Loki to benefit from wiresmith's value-type structs, lower allocations, and faster (de)serialization on the hot ingest/query paths — most critically `pkg/push/push.proto` (every `PushRequest`) and `pkg/engine/internal/proto/{physicalpb,wirepb}/*.proto` (engine scheduler/worker hot path).

## Why

Loki's hottest data paths are `PushRequest`/`StreamAdapter` (ingest) and the new engine's `Plan`/`Task`/`Frame` types (query execution). Both go through gogofast-generated marshaling today. Loki is also actively building the new engine layer (the recent `IndexMerge` node marshalling work, `chore(engine): Add stats for measuring data locality`, etc.) and adopting wiresmith there gives the largest perf headroom while the API surface is still internal.

## Scope of the migration

Counts from `grafana/loki` (main, 2026-06-05):

- **38 `.proto` files** under `pkg/**`, all `proto3`.
- **24 files** import `gogoproto/gogo.proto` and rely on at least one gogoproto extension.
- **15 files** declare gRPC `service` definitions covering every Loki transport: querier, ingester (push), index-gateway, bloom-gateway, scheduler, frontend v1/v2, compactor, ruler, bloombuild, limits, pattern.
- **7 files** use streaming RPCs (`returns (stream X)`): pattern, indexgateway, logproto (3 streams), ingester-rf1, scheduler, bloombuild, compactor, frontend v1.

### gogoproto extensions used by Loki's .proto files

Collected with `grep -h "gogoproto\.[a-z_]*" $(find pkg -name '*.proto')`:

| gogoproto option            | Count | Wiresmith status                                            |
| --------------------------- | -----:| ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `jsontag`                   |   237 | **native** — `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)`                  |
| `nullable`                  |   187 | native — wiresmith defaults to value-types; pointer via `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` |
| `customtype` on bytes/string |  ~5 | [[wiresmith-06z]] — shipped                                |
| `customtype` on message/scalar | ~75 | [[wiresmith-hnr]] — **OPEN, critical for `push.Stream`, `ulid.ULID`, `model.Time`, `LabelAdapter`** |
| `stdtime`                   |    54 | [[wiresmith-gqx]] — shipped                                |
| `marshaler_all` / `unmarshaler_all` / `sizer_all` | 31 | native — wiresmith always emits Marshal/Unmarshal/Size |
| `stdduration`               |     8 | [[wiresmith-b34]] — open                                   |
| `casttype`                  |     5 | [[wiresmith-cr0]] — open                                   |
| `customname`                |     1 | [[wiresmith-boe]] — shipped                                |

### Well-known types

- `google.protobuf.Timestamp` — 17 files. Covered by `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` [[wiresmith-gqx]] ✓
- `google.protobuf.Duration` — 5 files (`physicalpb.proto`, `ruler/base/ruler.proto`, `ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`, `querier/stats/stats.proto`, `querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto`). Needs `(wiresmith.options.stdduration)` [[wiresmith-b34]].
- `google.protobuf.Any` — 2 files. [[wiresmith-7m6]]:
  - `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto` (rule definitions)
  - `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/types.proto` (cached query response payload — hot path)
- `google.protobuf.Empty` — 1 file. `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto` (single use; could be replaced with empty message).

### gRPC services

15 Loki proto files declare RPCs. Covered by [[wiresmith-36b]] (closed via [[wiresmith-dei]]: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuses wiresmith messages directly). Streaming RPCs covered too.

[[wiresmith-ea4]] is needed if any consumer of Loki's gRPC services uses protoreflect-based reflection (e.g. `grpc_reflection_v1alpha`), since wiresmith strips `fdp.Service` and `Services().Len()` reports 0.

## Loki-specific structural blockers (not yet covered by any bead)

These are blockers unique to Loki's proto layout. Filed as sibling beads off this epic — see DEPENDS ON list.

### Same proto `package` ↔ multiple `go_package` (HARD blocker)

Wiresmith rejects compilation when one `.proto` `package` name maps to multiple Go packages (`compiler/generator/generator.go:559`). Loki violates this three times:

| proto `package`  | go_packages                                                                                                                                       |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `logproto`       | `github.com/grafana/loki/pkg/push` (no v3 — separate go-module) + `github.com/grafana/loki/v3/pkg/logproto` (8 files split across two directories) |
| `proto`          | `pkg/dataobj/compaction/proto`, `pkg/dataobj/compaction/v2/proto`, `pkg/limits/proto`                                                              |
| `stats`          | `pkg/logqlmodel/stats`, `pkg/querier/stats`                                                                                                        |

The `logproto` split is load-bearing: `pkg/push` is a separately-versioned Go module so external clients can depend on the push API without all of Loki. The other two are cosmetic.

**Options:** (a) Loki renames `package proto;` and `package stats;` to unique names per directory (cosmetic — only `(gogoproto.casttype)`-style FQN consumers care). (b) `logproto` requires either re-coupling `pkg/push` into the main module or accepting a proto-package rename on the wire (low risk — no Loki wire bytes embed the FQN, but external clients using `proto.MessageName` could break). (c) Wiresmith relaxes the rule to allow per-file `go_package` override matching protoc's `paths=source_relative`.

### Sibling-style proto imports (MINOR blocker)

`pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/test_types.proto` uses `import "types.proto";` to reference a sibling — works under protoc because Loki's Makefile passes `-I ./$(@D)`. Wiresmith resolves imports only against `--proto_path`. Either rewrite the import to its fully-qualified path, or use `--proto_path` per package. [[wiresmith-zxd]] addresses the multi-root case generally.

### Duplicate `service PusherRF1` (Loki bug surfaced by wiresmith)

`service PusherRF1 { ... }` is defined twice — `pkg/push/push-rf1.proto:11` and `pkg/logproto/ingester-rf1.proto:12`, both with `package logproto;`. Protoc tolerates this with per-file compilation; wiresmith errors with `symbol "logproto.PusherRF1" already defined`. This is a real Loki bug to fix during migration.

## Loki-side work not covered by wiresmith beads

After all wiresmith deps land, the Loki-side migration still needs:

1. **Resolve the `proto package` collisions** (see structural blockers above). Likely a rename pass on `package proto` / `package stats` and a careful decision on `package logproto`.
2. **Adapter types for customtype.** `push.Stream`, `ulid.ULID`, `model.Time` (`model.Fingerprint`), `syntax.LineFilter`, `plan.QueryPlan`, `pkg/engine/internal/util/dag.Graph`-related types all currently implement gogo-shaped `{Marshal,MarshalTo,MarshalToSizedBuffer,Unmarshal,Size}`. Wiresmith customtype expects `{Size,Marshal,Unmarshal,Equal,Compare}Wiresmith` (per [[wiresmith-06z]] notes). Needs new methods on those types or wrapper adapters.
3. **Rewriting `.proto` annotations.** Mechanical sweep over `(gogoproto.*)` → `(wiresmith.options.*)`. [[wiresmith-93t]] would eliminate this if it lands.
4. **Replace `google.protobuf.Empty` with a defined message** in `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto` (or wait on [[wiresmith-7m6]] for full WKT support).
5. **Drop legacy `pkg/ruler/base/`** if possible — it's gogofast-generated, uses `stdtime`, and is in maintenance mode. Less migration work if removed.
6. **Update `Makefile`'s `protos:` target** to invoke wiresmith instead of `protoc --gogoslick_out`. Drop the per-file `protoc` invocation; switch to per-package wiresmith calls with explicit `--proto_path` roots.
7. **`clean-protos:` glob update** — wiresmith emits 4 sibling files per `.proto` (`*.pb.go`, `*_compare.pb.go`, `*_equal.pb.go`, `*_reflect.pb.go`); current rule only removes `*.pb.go`.

## Hard blockers, in dependency order

1. **`customtype` on message and scalar fields** — [[wiresmith-hnr]] (open, P1) — used 80x across Loki:
   - `pkg/logproto/logproto.proto` (23): `push.Stream`, `model.Time` (`from`/`through`), `plan.QueryPlan`, `syntax.LineFilter`, `LabelAdapter`.
   - `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto` (24): same set.
   - `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb/wirepb.proto` (8): `ulid.ULID`.
   - `pkg/compactor/deletion/deletionproto/types.proto` (6), `pkg/logproto/bloomgateway.proto` (5), `pkg/logproto/metrics.proto` (3).
2. **`stdduration`** — [[wiresmith-b34]] (open, P2) — 5 files. Critical for `pkg/engine/internal/proto/physicalpb/physicalpb.proto` (`AggregateRange.Step/Range`, hot path).
3. **`casttype`** — [[wiresmith-cr0]] (open, P2) — 5 sites: `model.Fingerprint` in `indexgateway.proto`, `bloombuild/protos/types.proto`, `DetectedFieldType` in `logproto.proto`.
4. **`google.protobuf.Any`** — [[wiresmith-7m6]] (open, P2) — 2 files. `resultscache/types.proto` is in the query-result cache hot path; can't be reserved out.
5. **Multi `--proto_path` support** — [[wiresmith-zxd]] (open, P2) — to handle vendor/ + sibling-style imports without restructuring the Loki tree.
6. **(Loki-internal) Resolve the `proto package` ↔ `go_package` 1-to-1 collisions** — see structural blockers above.

## Per-file blocker matrix (38 files)

**Migratable today (no open blockers, only annotation rewriting):**
- `pkg/logqlmodel/stats/stats.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/compaction/proto/compaction.proto` (after `package` rename)
- `pkg/dataobj/compaction/v2/proto/compactionv2.proto` (after `package` rename)
- `pkg/dataobj/metastore/metastore.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/internal/metadata/datasetmd/datasetmd.proto`
- `pkg/dataobj/internal/metadata/filemd/filemd.proto`
- `pkg/xcap/internal/proto/xcap.proto`
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/ulid/ulid.proto`
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/expressionpb/expressionpb.proto`
- `pkg/limits/proto/limits.proto` (after `package` rename — needs `wiresmith-dei` recipe for service)
- `clients/cmd/docker-driver/logdriver/entry.proto` (already protoc-gen-go)

**Service-only (need protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse path, already proven via wiresmith-dei):**
- `pkg/lokifrontend/frontend/v2/frontendv2pb/frontend.proto`

**Blocked by hnr (customtype-on-messages) only:**
- `pkg/logproto/sketch.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/ingester-rf1.proto` (after fixing PusherRF1 duplicate)
- `pkg/push/push-rf1.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + service:**
- `pkg/push/push.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/bloomgateway.proto`
- `pkg/logproto/metrics.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + cr0:**
- `pkg/bloombuild/protos/types.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + cr0 + service:**
- `pkg/logproto/indexgateway.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + b34 + cr0 + service:**
- `pkg/logproto/logproto.proto` ← largest blocker concentration

**Blocked by hnr + b34 only:**
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/physicalpb/physicalpb.proto` ← engine hot path
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrange.proto`
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrangebase/queryrange.proto`

**Blocked by hnr only (engine):**
- `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb/wirepb.proto`

**Blocked by b34 only:**
- `pkg/querier/stats/stats.proto`
- `pkg/bloombuild/protos/service.proto` (also service)
- `pkg/querier/queryrange/queryrangebase/definitions/definitions.proto`

**Blocked by 7m6 (Any):**
- `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/types.proto` (also imported by everyone)
- `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`

**Blocked by 7m6 (Empty) + service:**
- `pkg/compactor/client/grpc/grpc.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + b34 + service (legacy):**
- `pkg/ruler/base/ruler.proto` ← consider deleting outright (legacy ruler)

**Blocked by hnr + service:**
- `pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto`
- `pkg/lokifrontend/frontend/v1/frontendv1pb/frontend.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + checkpoint customtype:**
- `pkg/ingester/checkpoint.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + service + Loki PusherRF1 dup fix:**
- `pkg/logproto/pattern.proto`

**Blocked by hnr + casttype + resultscache:**
- `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/test_types.proto` (also fix sibling import)

**Blocked by hnr + b34:**
- `pkg/compactor/deletion/deletionproto/types.proto`

## Out of scope (until enough is unblocked to pilot)

- Per-package migration sequencing — wait until at least `wiresmith-hnr` and `wiresmith-b34` land.
- Benchmark comparison against gogoslick on `PushRequest` / engine hot path — done as part of the migration PR.
- Any deletion of the `gogoproto` vendor directory.
- Migrating `clients/cmd/docker-driver/` (one-off, separate concern).

## Definition of done

- Every Loki `.pb.go` file currently produced by `gogoslick` can be regenerated by wiresmith with byte-identical wire compatibility on the `PushRequest` / `QueryResponse` / engine `Frame` paths.
- `make protos` in `grafana/loki` uses wiresmith.
- All Loki unit + integration tests pass against the wiresmith-generated code.
- A benchmark comparison vs. the gogoslick baseline on `pkg/push` and `pkg/engine/internal/proto/wirepb` is published in the PR.
- Backwards-compatible JSON encoding for everything the HTTP API touches (Volume/IndexStats/Stream/Sample/Series response shapes) — verified by golden tests in `pkg/util/marshal/`.

title: Tempo migration
description:
Replace gogofaster / protoc-gen-go-grpc with wiresmith as the protobuf compiler in [grafana/tempo](https://github.com/grafana/tempo).

## Scope

The Tempo repo has four proto trees that need wiresmith codegen:

| Proto | Generator today | Wiresmith-only blocker | Migration scope |
|-------|-----------------|------------------------|-----------------|
| `pkg/.patched-proto/{common,resource,trace}/v1/*.proto` (patched OTel) | gogofaster | none — pure proto3 messages | Migration-ready once wiresmith CLI is wired into Makefile. |
| `pkg/tempopb/tempo.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + streaming RPCs + `stdtime`/`customtype`/`nullable` | Full feature set required. |
| `pkg/tempopb/backendwork.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + per-message `equal`/`compare`/`marshaler_all`/`unmarshaler_all` | Full feature set required. |
| `modules/frontend/v1/frontendv1pb/frontend.proto` | gogofaster + grpc | gRPC services + bidi streaming (`stream X returns stream Y`) | Full feature set required. |
| `tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto` | gogofaster | `embed` + `customtype` + `customname` + `jsontag` + `stdtime` (heavy gogoproto, no gRPC) | Storage-layer; touches every block-read/write path. |

## Required wiresmith features (blockers below)

Confirmed by reading the Tempo proto files (`gogoproto.*` annotations + `service`/`rpc` blocks):

- gRPC service stub generation incl. streaming — `wiresmith-36b` (umbrella).
- `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` for `google.protobuf.Timestamp` → `time.Time` — `wiresmith-gqx`.
- `(wiresmith.options.customtype)` for `PreallocBytes`, `UUID`, `Encoding`, `DedicatedColumn` — `wiresmith-06z`.
- `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)` for the HTTP API tag stability in `tempodb/backend/v1` — `wiresmith-gd2` (new).
- `(wiresmith.options.customname)` for `BlockID`/`TenantID`-style initialism-preserving names — `wiresmith-boe` (new).
- `(wiresmith.options.embed)` for `CompactedBlockMeta.BlockMeta` inlining — `wiresmith-9ks` (new).
- `Compare()` method generation for `backendwork` scheduler ordering — `wiresmith-2xv` (new).

`nullable = false` is already the wiresmith default (value-type fields); `marshaler_all`/`unmarshaler_all` are functionally equivalent to wiresmith's always-on marshal/unmarshal; per-message `equal` is covered by the existing nil-safe `Equal()`.

## Out of scope for this epic

- Migrating Mimir / other Grafana consumers — separate epic per repo.
- Removing the gogo runtime dependency from Tempo entirely (some non-proto callers still pull `gogo/protobuf/types`). Treated as a follow-up.
- Performance evaluation versus the current gogofaster build — done as part of the migration PR, not a precondition.

## Plan (high level)

1. Land all blocker beads in wiresmith.
2. In Tempo: rewrite `(gogoproto.*)` annotations to their `(wiresmith.options.*)` equivalents (or rely on the alias-recognition each blocker bead defines).
3. Swap `buf/buf.gen.*.yaml` `protoc-gen-gogofaster` for `wiresmith` invocations; regenerate `*.pb.go` and `*_grpc.pb.go`.
4. Drop `vendor/github.com/gogo/protobuf` from the proto-codegen path.
5. Run `make test` + `make integration` + benchmarks; verify wire compatibility against canned blocks from prior versions.

## Acceptance criteria

- `make gen-proto` in Tempo runs wiresmith only — no `protoc-gen-gogofaster` or `protoc-gen-go-grpc` invocations.
- All existing unit, integration, and benchmark tests pass with the regenerated code.
- Backwards-compatible JSON encoding for `tempodb/backend/v1` block metadata (no regressions in stored block decoding).
- CHANGELOG entry documenting the swap and the floor wiresmith version pinned.
notes: All 9 wiresmith-side blockers closed as of 2026-06-09 (wiresmith-9qx was the last). Execution work now happens in the grafana/tempo repo (proto annotation rewrites + Makefile swap + regenerate). Out of scope for /bd-work loop iterations in this repo — needs explicit user direction to start cross-repo work.

title: Mimir migration
description:
## Goal

Replace `protoc-gen-gogoslick` (and `protoc-gen-go-grpc` for some packages) as the protobuf compiler for [grafana/mimir](https://github.com/grafana/mimir) with `wiresmith`. This enables Mimir to benefit from wiresmith's value-type structs, lower allocations, and faster (de)serialization on the hot write/query paths — most critically `pkg/mimirpb/mimir.proto`, which carries every `WriteRequest`.

## Why

Mimir's hottest data structures (`PreallocTimeseries`, label sets, samples, histograms, exemplars) flow through gogoproto-generated marshaling. The Mimir codebase is also the strategic Grafana consumer the wiresmith README implicitly targets: replacing gogoproto on a production multi-tenant ingester is the proof point that wiresmith can replace gogofaster outside the OTel collector.

## Scope of the migration

Counts from `grafana/mimir` (branch `wiresmith`, 2026-06-05):

- **33 `.proto` files** under `pkg/**`, all `proto3`.
- **29 files** import `gogoproto/gogo.proto` and rely on at least one gogoproto extension.
- **10 files** declare gRPC `service` definitions (ingester, ruler, scheduler, store-gateway, alertmanager, distributor, frontend v2, usage-tracker, block-builder scheduler, compactor scheduler).

### gogoproto extensions used by Mimir's .proto files

Collected with `grep -rh "gogoproto\.[a-z_]*" pkg/**/*.proto`:

| gogoproto option            | Wiresmith status                                            |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `customtype` on bytes/string | [[wiresmith-06z]] — shipped                                |
| `customtype` on message     | [[wiresmith-hnr]] — **OPEN, critical for PreallocTimeseries/LabelAdapter** |
| `casttype`                  | [[wiresmith-cr0]] — open                                   |
| `stdtime`                   | [[wiresmith-gqx]] — shipped                                |
| `stdduration`               | [[wiresmith-b34]] — open                                   |
| `goproto_enum_prefix`       | [[wiresmith-1dq]] — open (cosmetic — workaround is to rename enum values in proto) |
| `marshaler_all` / `unmarshaler_all` / `sizer_all` | native — wiresmith always emits Marshal/Unmarshal/Size |
| `nullable`                  | native — wiresmith defaults to value-types; pointer via `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` |
| `equal_all`                 | **native** — wiresmith emits `Equal()` in `<name>_equal.pb.go` |
| `jsontag`                   | **native** — `(wiresmith.options.jsontag)`                  |
| `messagename_all`           | **no callers** in Mimir non-generated code; safe to drop the option |
| `goproto_getters_all`       | **native** — wiresmith always emits `Get<Field>()`         |
| `goproto_sizecache_all`     | **no-op** — wiresmith has no `XXX_sizecache` field         |
| `goproto_unkeyed_all`       | **no-op** — wiresmith doesn't add `XXX_unrecognized`-style fields by default |
| `goproto_unrecognized_all`  | [[wiresmith-4je]] — relevant only if a consumer needs gogo `proto.Buffer` compat (Mimir does NOT — see `pkg/util/http.go:162` falls through to `Unmarshal` directly) |

### Well-known types

Mimir uses well-known types in:

- `google.protobuf.Timestamp` — covered by `(wiresmith.options.stdtime)` [[wiresmith-gqx]] ✓
- `google.protobuf.Duration` — needs `(wiresmith.options.stdduration)` [[wiresmith-b34]]
- `google.protobuf.Any` — **only [[wiresmith-7m6]]**. Used in 4 files:
  - `pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto`: `repeated google.protobuf.Any options` (ruler Prometheus Manager opts, cold path)
  - `pkg/frontend/querymiddleware/model.proto`: `google.protobuf.Any response` (query-result cache extent)
  - `pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto`: `google.protobuf.Any payload` (scheduler protocol)
  - `pkg/storegateway/storepb/rpc.proto`: 5 uses, **all on Deprecated fields** — could be reserved+removed instead of migrated

### gRPC services

10 Mimir proto files declare RPCs. Covered by [[wiresmith-36b]] (closed via [[wiresmith-dei]]: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuses wiresmith messages directly).

## Mimir-side work not covered by wiresmith beads

After all wiresmith deps land, the Mimir-side migration still needs:

1. **Adapter types for customtype.** `PreallocTimeseries` (`pkg/mimirpb/timeseries.go:143`) and `LabelAdapter` (`pkg/mimirpb/timeseries.go:520`) currently implement gogo-shaped `{Marshal,MarshalTo,MarshalToSizedBuffer,Unmarshal,Size}`. wiresmith customtype expects `{Size,Marshal,Unmarshal,Equal,Compare}Wiresmith` (per [[wiresmith-06z]] notes). Mimir needs either new methods on those types or wrapper adapter types.

2. **Rewriting `.proto` annotations.** `(gogoproto.customtype)` is not aliased to `(wiresmith.options.customtype)` ([[wiresmith-06z]] deferred this — see new bead for the alias). Every `.proto` file with gogoproto annotations must be rewritten by hand to use `wiresmith.options.*` equivalents.

3. **Updating the `apply-expected-diffs.sh` patches.** The `*.pb.go.expdiff` files in Mimir are gogoslick-output-specific and become invalid under wiresmith.

4. **Cross-package import paths.** `mimir.proto` uses `go_package = "mimirpb"` (bare name). Wiresmith honors `go_package` literally; needs to be either changed to the full path or overridden via `-M`.

## Hard blockers, in dependency order

1. **`customtype` on message fields** — [[wiresmith-hnr]] (open, P1) — `WriteRequest.timeseries` (`PreallocTimeseries`), `TimeSeries.labels`/`Metric.labels`/`Exemplar.labels` (`UnsafeMutableLabel`/`LabelAdapter`). Blocks `mimir.proto`, `ingester.proto`, `ruler.proto`, `querierpb`, `frontendv2pb`, `storegateway/storepb/{cache,types}.proto`, `planning/core/core.proto`, `rulespb/rules.proto`, `querymiddleware/model.proto`.
2. **`casttype`** — [[wiresmith-cr0]] (open, P2) — `FloatHistogram.counter_reset_hint`, `streamingpromql/planning/plan.proto` and `planning/core/core.proto`.
3. **`stdduration`** — [[wiresmith-b34]] (open, P2) — `ruler.proto`, `querier/stats/stats.proto`, several `streamingpromql/optimize/plan/*` files, `planning/{plan,core/core}.proto`, `rulespb/rules.proto`.
4. **`google.protobuf.Any`** — [[wiresmith-7m6]] (open, P2) — 4 files. Storegateway uses are all on deprecated fields and can be reserved-out instead.
5. **`goproto_enum_prefix`** (cosmetic only) — [[wiresmith-1dq]] (open, P3). Mimir can rename `Histogram.ResetHint` values to embed the prefix manually as a cheap workaround.

## Per-file blocker matrix (33 files)

**Migratable today (no open blockers, only annotation rewriting):**

title: SEC-3: 32-bit length-decode truncation in skipValue and inline helper
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:32-43` (skipValue case 2); `compiler/types/type.go:227-232` (`emitConsumeBytesLenAt`).

**What:** `byteLen` is decoded into `uint64` but immediately converted with `int(byteLen)` and checked only for `< 0`. On `GOARCH=386/arm/wasm` (32-bit `int`), values in `(MaxInt32, MaxInt64]` truncate to small positive ints, bypassing both the `< 0` check and the `postIndex > l` bound. CLAUDE.md "Common review caveats" lists this exact pitfall under "32-bit length safety".

**Fix:** Before `int(byteLen)`, add `if byteLen > uint64(math.MaxInt) { return io.ErrUnexpectedEOF }`. Apply in both `skipValue` and `emitConsumeBytesLenAt`. Add a test that runs under `GOARCH=386`.
close_reason: merged via #63 (commit 9e26682) — bead missed by earlier sweep

title: CR-4: Self-referencing message without optional emits infinite Go struct
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_struct.go:51` (no cycle detection); generator emits value-type message fields by default.

**What:** `message Tree { Tree child = 1; }` is valid proto3. wiresmith emits `Child Tree` (value type), which `go vet` and `go build` both reject as a recursively-sized struct. The pointer-option (`(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`) and `optional` keyword both fix it, but the generator silently emits the broken struct rather than detecting the cycle.

**Fix:** Add a SCC-based cycle check during planning, following only non-optional/non-repeated/non-pointer-option/non-oneof/non-map edges. On detection, return an actionable error pointing at the offending field and suggesting `optional` or `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true`.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: SEC-2: Pre-scan default: break only exits switch, not outer loop
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:138-141`.

**What:** When `preTyp ∈ {3,4,6,7}` (legacy groups + reserved), the `default: break` only terminates the inner `switch`; `preIdx` is unchanged, so the next iteration treats payload bytes as a new tag. This inflates field counts and feeds SEC-1. The post-switch guard at line 141 only catches genuinely out-of-bounds `preIdx`.

**Fix:** Either label the outer `for` and `break OUTER`, or set `preIdx = l` in the default branch, or simply abort the pre-scan entirely on unknown wire type (the main loop is the source of truth — pre-scan is an optimisation). The "abort the optimisation" option is safest.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-3: Empty .proto produces uncompilable Go
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:271-309` (`generateFile`); pertinent imports added unconditionally at `emit_unmarshal.go:163-165`.

**What:** A `syntax = "proto3"; package empty;` with no messages or enums still emits a `.pb.go` that imports `protohelpers`/`protowire` but never uses them. CLAUDE.md flags this as a known caveat; nothing in the generator guards it.

**Fix:** In `generateFile`, short-circuit if `messages == 0 && enums == 0` — either skip the file entirely or emit only the package clause. Add a generator test with an empty fixture proto.
close_reason: Closed

title: CR-2: Negative-zero floats stripped on marshal (silent corruption)
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/types/fixed64.go:42-43` (`EmitSize`, `EmitMarshal` use `if access != 0`); `compiler/types/fixed32.go:43` mirrors this for `float`.

**What:** In Go, `-0.0 != 0` is `false`, so a singular `double`/`float` set to `-0.0` is treated as a default value and elided from the wire. On round-trip it reads back as `+0.0`. `math.Signbit` is lost. The official `google.golang.org/protobuf` library guards this with `if v == 0 && !math.Signbit(v) { return }` (see its `codec_gen.go`).

**Fix:** Emit `if math.Float64bits(access) != 0` (or, equivalently, `Float32bits`) so any non-zero bit pattern — including negative zero — serialises. Add a unit test in `test/basic/numeric_test.go` that marshals `math.Copysign(0, -1)` and asserts `math.Signbit` survives the round-trip.
close_reason: Fix: marshal/size now use math.FloatNNbits(v) != 0 so -0.0 survives the wire round-trip; matches google.golang.org/protobuf. Includes test coverage in test/basic/numeric_test.go.

title: SEC-1: Pre-scan slice pre-allocation is attacker-amplifiable
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:144-156` (pre-scan size hint), `99-141` (pre-scan loop).

**What:** The pre-scan counts field-number occurrences then does `m.X = make([]Span, 0, count)`. A malicious 1 MB payload of tiny tag-for-field-1 + zero-length entries can produce ~500 000 occurrences, allocating ~125 MB of capacity for `[]Span` (struct size ≈ 250 B). Combined with SEC-2 below, the count itself becomes attacker-controlled.

**Fix:** Cap pre-allocated capacity by a sanity bound: `cap = min(count, (l-iNdEx)/minBytesPerElement)` where `minBytesPerElement` is the smallest legal wire encoding for the element type (e.g., `1` for varint scalar tag + minimum body). For message-typed repeated fields, the minimum is 2 bytes (tag + zero-length). Easy formula: `cap = min(count, l/2)`. Add a fuzz seed that exercises this exact shape.
close_reason: Already shipped in merged PRs #68, #72, #69, #67, #70 respectively

title: CR-1: Reset() panics on nil receiver
description:
**Severity:** Critical

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_reset.go:15`

**What:** Emits `func (m *T) Reset() { *m = T{} }` with no nil guard, while the same generator emits `String()` *with* a nil guard at line 17–19. `CLAUDE.md` "Common review caveats" lists `Reset()` as one of the methods that must handle nil gracefully — this regressed silently because `String()` got the treatment and `Reset()` did not.

**Fix:** `func (m *T) Reset() { if m == nil { return }; *m = T{} }`. One branch on a cold path; trivially testable.
close_reason: merged via #64 (commit 28613ea); bead never got closed at the time

title: wiresmith: file-level no_registration option to opt out of official global registry
description: By default a wiresmith-generated file registers its descriptor and message/enum types with the official global proto registries (protoregistry.GlobalFiles/GlobalTypes) at init, matching protoc-gen-go. That global registration panics at init when a wiresmith-migrated proto shares its proto package / import path with an official-runtime module also linked into the binary that registers the same package. The precedent is github.com/prometheus/client_model, which owns io.prometheus.client globally — a Cluster 3 blocker for Prometheus generating io.prometheus.client with wiresmith. Fixed with a file-level (wiresmith.options.no_registration) = true option (field 50044) that redirects the generated init into a package-local protoregistry.Files/Types (via protoimpl.DescBuilder.FileRegistry / TypeBuilder.TypeRegistry) instead of the globals: zero global mutation, no collision. Types stay valid proto.Messages (wire methods + local-descriptor reflection keep working); they are simply invisible to the global registry.
close_reason: Closed

title: fleet re-pin adaptation for flat-file keying flip (mimir/tempo/loki)
description:
BLOCKS bumping any fleet consumer's wiresmith pin past fc34fadb1d5661e1c22f5a40819cf221f026dc81 (the flat-file import-keying flip to protoc/buf path-parity).

Before fc34fad, wiresmith keyed a flat proto (one sitting directly at a --proto_path root) by its proto package, which the fleet exploited to map flat sources into package-nested Go output. After fc34fad, flat files key by bare filename and emit at bare source-relative paths. mimir/tempo/loki all have flat files under their proto_path and WILL break at their next re-pin until adapted. Their current f15959a pins are unaffected today.

Per-repo checklist:
- mimir: pkg/mimirpb/mimir.proto (package cortexpb) is flat under pkg/mimirpb; the Makefile (~404) emits to pkg/mimirpb/cortexpb/ then `mv`s the files up one dir + rmdir cortexpb. Under path-parity it emits directly at pkg/mimirpb/mimir.pb.go, so the `mv cortexpb/...` fails. Remove the mv-hack. Also re-verify ha_tracker.proto (~422) and querier/stats/stats.proto (~436): they rely on package-keying placing output under the package name to line up with --out one level up.
- tempo: tempo.proto (package tempopb) is staged flat and relies on the package-derived key placing output at pkg/tempopb/ (Makefile ~333/343; the Makefile comment documents this dependency). Stage it (and the patched OTLP tree) at their import paths so path-parity reproduces pkg/tempopb/.
- loki: staged trees + -M keys assume package-keying for flat files (Makefile ~168/611). Stage protos at their import paths and align -M source keys with the new path-parity keys.

Each repo's regen must reproduce its committed .pb.go byte-for-byte after adaptation (their existing verification gates). Do this at the same time as re-pinning each repo onto a post-fc34fad wiresmith.
notes:
2026-07-06 fleet-adaptation ACTUALS (checklist above unchanged; annotating loki+tempo DONE, mimir PENDING):

LOKI -- DONE, NO-OP. The checklist assumption ("staged trees + -M keys must match path-parity") was WRONG: loki stages every proto NESTED with full-path imports, so its files were keyed by relative path under BOTH the old and new schemes -- fc34fad only changed the flat-file branch of buildImportMapping, which loki never hits. Re-pinned to wiresmith v0.0.0-20260706094138-fc34fadb1d56 with ZERO codegen / Makefile / -M changes. Byte-identity verified twice (migrator agent + independently reproduced by orchestrator). Commit ccb8820ce2 on the loki fork's wiresmith branch (PR #22382).

TEMPO -- DONE, smaller than the checklist predicted. Only the flat tempo.proto + backendwork.proto invocation (both package tempopb, staged at the build-tree root) was affected. Fix: stage those two under a tempopb/ subdir so the path-relative key equals the old package-derived key tempopb/tempo.proto -- committed `// source:` headers and registration names preserved exactly. The OTLP protos and the other 3 gen-proto invocations were already staged in subdirs and needed NOTHING (checklist over-predicted scope). Re-pinned to the same v0.0.0-20260706094138-fc34fadb1d56 pseudo; commit a39a00e38 on the tempo fork's wiresmith branch (PR #7466). Byte-identity verified twice + independently reproduced by orchestrator.

MIMIR -- still PENDING; queued behind catch-up merge -> egvq fix -> cqa.4; adapts + re-pins last. Lesson carried from tempo: the invariant to preserve is the committed `// source:` header / registration key (mimir.proto's old package-derived key is cortexpb/mimir.proto), NOT literally "drop the mv cortexpb/ hack". The right fix may be staging mimir.proto under a cortexpb/ subdir (keeping the source header + registration names identical) rather than dropping the mv. The mimir agent must decide by checking the committed mimir.pb.go `// source:` header and the gogoproto_registry shims' name coupling. Also: mimir's expdiffs re-derive AFTER cqa.4.

Net: o70s stays OPEN until mimir adapts + re-pins.

title: flat-file import keying diverges from protoc; embedded options.proto collision blocks buf-vendoring consumers
description:
Flat .proto files (sitting directly at a --proto_path root) were keyed by their proto package (e.g. `package prometheus` + types.proto -> prometheus/types.proto) instead of their path relative to the root. That diverged from protoc/buf, which always key by root-relative path, and blocked buf-native consumers.

Concretely (Prometheus, the 4th migration target): prompb/ is a buf module root where remote.proto does `import "types.proto"` and `buf breaking --against ...#subdir=prompb` forces that flat topology. wiresmith could not compile it -- the bare import keyed as prometheus/types.proto and never resolved -- and the vendored prompb/wiresmith/options.proto (required on disk for buf, which can't read the compiler's embed) hard-collided with the embedded schema.

Fix (databases fc34fadb1d5661e1c22f5a40819cf221f026dc81):
- Import keying is now protoc/buf parity: every walked file keys by its path relative to the containing --proto_path root (root-level -> bare filename, nested -> relative path); the proto package no longer influences the key.
- Embedded-schema tolerance: an on-disk wiresmith/options.proto is accepted iff byte-identical to the compiler's embedded schema (wiresmith serves its own embed, emits nothing); a drifted copy is rejected with a byte-mismatch error.
- wiresmith's own OTel/basic/test/conformance protos were physically relocated to their import-path layout so each file's root-relative key equals its previous package-derived key -> `make generate-ours` is byte-for-byte identical (verified empty gen/ diff).

Verification: build/vet/full test suite green; new tests (compiler/generator/keying_parity_test.go + rewritten buildImportMapping tests) cover flat-file bare keying, bare-import between flat siblings, source-relative flat output, and the options.proto accept/reject paths. Prometheus probe (read-only, -proto_path=prompb) now succeeds; scratch vs committed prompb .pb.go differs only in path strings (// source:, rawDesc descriptor path, file_*_proto identifiers, banners) with zero semantic diffs, and now registers bare "types.proto" -- matching upstream gogo proto.RegisterFile("types.proto", ...) (correcting the prompb/-prefixed path the pre-migration wiresmith emitted).

BREAKING for existing flat-file consumers (mimir/tempo/loki): tracked by the fleet-adaptation follow-up bead.
close_reason: Fixed on databases fc34fadb1d5661e1c22f5a40819cf221f026dc81: import keying is now protoc/buf path-parity (flat files key by bare filename; proto package no longer influences the key) and a byte-identical vendored wiresmith/options.proto is tolerated. wiresmith's own protos were relocated to their import-path layout so gen/ stays byte-identical (verified). Prometheus probe green with zero semantic diffs and bare types.proto registration matching upstream gogo. Follow-up: fleet-adaptation bead.

title: wiresmith cannot resolve bare (no-directory-component) proto imports
description:
wiresmith fails to resolve an import whose path has no directory component (a bare sibling like import "types.proto"), even when the file sits at a --proto_path root and is also passed as a positional. protoc/buf resolve these fine against -I roots.

Minimal repro (wiresmith f15959a / v0.0.0-20260618160438-f15959a1e4e7):
  a.proto: package t; message A { int32 x = 1; }
  b.proto: package t; import "a.proto"; message B { A a = 1; }
  wiresmith --proto_path=. --out=. --module=example.com/m a.proto b.proto
  => error: compiling protos: t/b.proto:4:8: could not resolve path "a.proto": file does not exist
Changing the import to a path WITH a directory component (import "sub/a.proto") resolves fine. Tried: positional and walk modes, CWD at the root, absolute/real proto_path — all fail for the bare form.

Impact: consumers whose protos use bare same-package sibling imports (Prometheus prompb: remote.proto imports "types.proto") must rewrite them to include a directory prefix (prompb/types.proto). That repo-root-relative form is then incompatible with a buf module rooted at the package dir (buf wants the bare form), which BLOCKS the clean buf-workspace / BSR consumer integration (see wiresmith-o97m) — buf-lint cannot resolve the rewritten import. Expected: resolve bare imports against --proto_path roots like protoc/buf/gogofast do.

title: Publish wiresmith/options.proto as a Buf Schema Registry (BSR) module for buf-based consumers
description:
buf-based consumers that import "wiresmith/options.proto" (e.g. Prometheus) cannot resolve it: the options schema is embedded in the CLI and auto-injected only in CLI mode, but buf lint / buf breaking / buf generate need the file available either as a module file or a buf.yaml deps: entry. Today consumers must vendor a copy — mimir vendors proto-include/wiresmith/options.proto for protoc; the Prometheus wiresmith PoC vendors prompb/wiresmith/options.proto for buf (with a TODO pointing here).

Idiomatic fix, matching every other annotation-based compiler: publish a BSR module so consumers add ONE deps line. Precedent: gogoproto -> buf.build/gogo/protobuf; protovalidate -> buf.build/bufbuild/protovalidate; protoc-gen-validate -> buf.build/envoyproxy/protoc-gen-validate. Prometheus's prompb/buf.yaml already consumes gogo exactly this way.

Action: add a buf.yaml to the compiler repo and publish wiresmith/options.proto to the BSR as buf.build/grafana/wiresmith (name TBD), ideally pushed from CI on release. Consumers then replace the vendored copy with deps: [buf.build/grafana/wiresmith] (symmetric with their existing gogo dep). Retire the vendored stopgaps in mimir + prometheus once published. Optionally also keep a vendored -I include dir for protoc-only consumers (mimir already does).

title: wiresmith: unique-interned string fields to remove yoloString + gRPC buffer-retention dependency
description:
Discovered in mimir cqa.3 (frontendv2pb migration, 2026-06-22). Mimir gRPC zero-copy receive path relies on mimirpb yoloString: label Name/Value are unsafe aliases into the pooled gRPC frame buffer. Consumers must keep the frame alive (MessageWithBufferRef SetBuffer/FreeBuffer) while those strings live. wiresmith generates message structs with no provision to attach a buffer-ref field (the gogo BufferHolder pattern), so frontendv2pb QueryResultStreamRequest uses a global sync.Map side-channel for buffer retention. That side-channel has a bounded-but-permanent leak on a Close-vs-buffered-message race (<=1 entry per abandoned stream; key and mem.Buffer never GC-able). Accepted for now and documented in mimir WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md.

Proper fix: a wiresmith option to generate string fields as buffer-INDEPENDENT canonical strings via the stdlib unique package (unique.Make(string(b)).Value()), which removes the yoloString frame-aliasing (no buffer retention needed, no leak) and dedups repeated label names/values. CAVEAT per team Go rules: unique.Make(string(b)).Value() only keeps the dedup/interning benefit if the unique.Handle is preserved; a bare .Value() copies safely but loses long-term interning unless the Handle is kept. Design must account for Handle lifetime.

Prerequisite for completing the mimir migration (wiresmith-cqa): the buffer-retention leak must be properly resolved before the mimir PR merges to upstream.

title: PERF: pre-scan exact-fit grow is O(n^2) when unmarshalling into a reused (non-reset) message
description:
DB-9 tempo finding (2026-06-11, benchstat-grade, M4 Pro). pkg/ingest BenchmarkEncodeDecode reuses one Decoder and calls Decode (=> PushBytesRequest.Unmarshal) repeatedly WITHOUT Reset. Result vs gogo baseline 7c61b2b70b: +2196% time (165us -> 3.59ms) and +6788% bytes (1.25MiB -> 89MiB/op), allocs flat (2004 -> 2006). Isolation experiment (force pre-scan off via 'if false'): wiresmith collapses to 161us / 1.33MiB -- DEAD MATCH with gogo (165us / 1.30MiB). So the pre-scan is 100% the cause.

ROOT CAUSE: emitPreScan grows the target slice with EXACT-FIT capacity: 'grown := make([]T, len(m.X), len(m.X)+c); copy(...)'. With append-style unmarshal and no Reset, len(m.X) grows by c every call, so cap is always exceeded by exactly c => every Unmarshal reallocates and copies the ENTIRE (growing) backing array => O(n^2) total work and O(n^2) bytes. gogo's plain 'append' doubles capacity (amortized O(n)) so the identical contract-violating usage stays bounded per-op.

This is NOT the u4qg security angle and NOT the bobw scan-cost angle; it is a quadratic perf cliff that turns a benign gogo pattern into a blowup. Note u4qg's assumption 'append growth beyond c allocates fresh (fine)' is refuted -- the realloc is in the pre-scan grow, not the append. Real risk: ingest.Decoder.Decode is a public method that does not Reset; any loop reusing it in production hits this.

FIX OPTIONS: (a) only pre-scan-reserve when len(m.X)==0 (fresh message) -- keeps the fresh-decode win, falls back to amortized append on reuse; (b) grow with amortized headroom max(need, 2*cap) instead of exact-fit; (c) document + lint the Reset contract harder (weak). Prefer (a). Re-run BenchmarkEncodeDecode + mimir RW1/RW2 + tempo PushBytes/OTLP after fix. Cross-ref wiresmith-u4qg (DB-6 reuse), wiresmith-bobw (DB-18 scan cost).
notes:
Implemented FIX OPTION (a): pre-scan reserves count-sized capacity ONLY when the repeated (non-map) slice is empty (len==0); a populated slice is left to the main loop's amortized append. emit_unmarshal.go else-branch now emits: if len(m.X)==0 && cap(m.X)<c { m.X = make(goType,0,c) }. Map branch and preCapMax (l/2 SEC-1) clamp unchanged.

Invariants: len==0,cap<c (fresh) -> make(_,0,c) byte-identical to before; len==0,cap>=c (pooled reset) -> no make, reuse; len>0 (merge) -> NO reserve, append grows amortized (was the O(n^2) exact-fit realloc).

Benchstat (M4 Pro, count=20, benchtime=1s, GOLANG_PROTOBUF_REGISTRATION_CONFLICT=warn, clean paired runs old-vs-new): geomean +0.22% (noise floor). B/op and allocs/op byte-identical on every macro (UnmarshalMap/Traces/SingleSpan/Logs/Profiles, MarshalTraces) -- exactly as Option A predicts for fresh-message decode. Per-bench sec/op all within noise (Map ~, MarshalTraces -0.69%, Traces ~, SingleSpan +0.74%, Logs +2.27%, Profiles -0.46%); noise-control moves bracket the targeted ones. NO regression on macros. (Local microbench-only validation; user will re-run Tempo pkg/ingest BenchmarkEncodeDecode + mimir RW1/RW2 before closing.)

Tests: new test/basic/prescan_test.go::TestPreScanNoExactFitGrowOnMergeUnmarshal -- unmarshals the SAME MixedModifiers 5x WITHOUT reset using a >=256B repeated_string payload; asserts (a) len grows by perCallCount each call (merge/append parity) + element round-trip, (b) final cap>len (amortized headroom) and cap<4*len. The cap>len half FAILS on the old exact-fit path (which left cap==len after the final realloc) and PASSES with the fix; observed cap progression 8,16,32,32,71 for len 8,16,24,32,40. Updated compiler/generator/prescan_cap_test.go::TestPreScanEmitsCapClamp to assert the new make([]Item,0,c) form. make test PASS; go test ./compiler/... ./test/basic/... PASS. NOT closing -- pending Tempo re-bench.
VERIFIED on real Tempo path (M4 Pro, 20-round alternated, gogo 7c61b2b70b vs fixed wiresmith via new compiler binary @39ef729 regenerating pkg/tempopb). BenchmarkEncodeDecode: was +2197% time / 89 MiB/op -> now 178.1us / 1.247 MiB (p=0.10/0.14, parity with gogo 173.7us / 1.235 MiB), allocs 2.004k identical. ~21x faster, ~69x less memory; O(n^2) eliminated. OTLP decode -4.4%; PushBytes +3.8% (separate scan-cost class = wiresmith-bobw/DB-18, untouched). Regen diff confirmed to be ONLY the Option A grow-block swap, no drift; m.Traces fix present. Compiler fix commit 39ef729 in wiresmith-databases is LOCAL/unpushed (pending user review). Tempo pkg/tempopb regen is currently UNCOMMITTED in tempo-wiresmith working tree.
Rolled into grafana/wiresmith PR #134 (head pre-scan-tempo-fix -> main): zlce O(n²) fix + go.mod vtprotobuf-replace removal (makes module go-installable). Merged origin/main in to resolve a .beads/issues.jsonl conflict (union, no loss). PR conflict-free/MERGEABLE but BLOCKED by branch protection (needs review/CI).

title: DB-16: compile only positional files + transitive imports (stop eager whole-tree compile)
description: Mimir phase-2 blocker #1 (also hit by loki/tempo agents — every repo invented staging-dir recipes). 'wiresmith --proto_path=./pkg/distributor file.proto' fails when an unrelated sibling proto in the tree is gogo-annotated or has unresolvable imports, because Generate compiles the entire proto_path walk and only filters at emission. Fix: when positional files are given, pass only those as protocompile roots; imports resolve on demand via the resolver over the proto_path mapping; validation/dest computation operate on the compiled set (+reachable imports) as today. Eliminates staging dirs for staged migrations.
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch (7691012): positional files become the protocompile roots; imports resolve lazily via the resolver; unrelated broken siblings never parsed. Tests: TestGenerateFilesSkipsUnrelatedBrokenSiblings / TestGenerateFilesBrokenImportStillFails. Removes the staging-dir recipes in all three database repos.

title: DB-13: size-dependent repeated-field semantics: pre-scan (>=256B) replaces, small payloads append
description: Tempo blocker #4: unmarshal into a non-empty message replaces repeated fields when the pre-scan triggers but appends below preScanMinBytes — broke tempo blockbuilder's merge-by-unmarshal; arguably a wiresmith bug. Mimir/gogo expectations: gogo always appends (merge semantics). Proposed: make pre-scan prealloc len-preserving (grow to len+c, copy, keep elements) so semantics are uniformly append — composes with the DB-6 cap-guard (Reset/pooled messages have len 0, so the pooling win is unchanged). Needs benchstat + design sign-off; interacts with docs/design.md pooling-contract wording (currently says replace-on-decode).
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch: pre-scan prealloc is a length-preserving grow (len+c, copy, reuse when cap fits); map prealloc nil-guarded. Uniform append/merge semantics both sides of the 256B threshold, gogo parity, pooling unchanged. Tests: prealloc_reuse_test.go merge suite (both size paths, pooled-capacity, map merge). benchstat neutral (focused pair flat, p=0.98). Tempo blockbuilder pattern works again.

comments.author: Oleg Kozliuk
comments.text: User decision 2026-06-10: uniform APPEND semantics (gogo merge parity). Pre-scan prealloc becomes length-preserving grow (len+c, copy); maps get nil-guard instead of unconditional make. Pooling win unchanged (Reset/pooled messages have len 0).

title: DB-11: duplicate skipValue/maxUnmarshalDepth when multiple .proto files share one Go package
description: Tempo blocker #2 (compile failure; tempo ships tools/strip-wiresmith-dup-helpers.py as workaround) and gates Loki's pkg/logproto cluster (multi-proto dir). Each generated .pb.go emits identical package-level skipValue func + maxUnmarshalDepth const; two protos with the same go_package collide. Proposed fix: move both into protohelpers (SkipValue func + MaxUnmarshalDepth const) and emit qualified references — skipValue is the cold unknown-field/wire-mismatch path and is already an uninlined call, so no hot-path risk (verify with benchstat per repo rules); the const is zero-cost. Removes the strip script and unblocks per-file regeneration of multi-proto packages.
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch: SkipValue + MaxUnmarshalDepth moved to protohelpers, generated code references qualified. Fixture proto/basic/multifile_{a,b}.proto + test/basic/multifile_test.go. benchstat: neutral (net ~+0.1% vs control on focused pair). Tempo can drop tools/strip-wiresmith-dup-helpers.py; unblocks loki logproto.

title: DB-9: post-migration database benchmarks + regression investigation (mimir/tempo/loki)
description:
User-anchored expectation (2026-06-10): once the three database migrations land, the next phase is running each database's own benchmark suites comparing wiresmith vs gogo baselines, then investigating and identifying every regression.

Known data points already in hand:
- Mimir (single-run, needs benchstat-grade): marshal parity (+2-4%); unmarshal +12-28% wall-clock but -40-47% allocated bytes on RW1. Prime suspect: pre-scan pass (see also preScanMinBytes sensitivity question). Recorded in mimir-wiresmith WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md.
- Methodology: count>=20 benchstat, quiet machine, compare against the gogo baseline commit each branch forked from (mimir: cb6dac78a3).

Scope per database: identify the proto-heavy benchmark suites (mimir pkg/mimirpb + distributor/ingester benches; tempo tempopb + tempodb; loki logproto/push paths), run paired baselines, benchstat, then root-cause regressions (pre-scan threshold, cap-guard branch, value-type copying on slice growth, icache effects). Feed findings back as wiresmith beads.
notes:
DB-9 progress: MIMIR DONE (benchstat-grade). pkg/mimirpb BenchmarkUnMarshal vs baseline cb6dac78a3, M4 Pro, 20-round alternated binaries, p=0.000: Marshal -7/-9% (faster); Unmarshal RW1 net win (skip=false -4.5% wall, -47% bytes); Unmarshal RW2 +15% (the only regression). Root-caused to the always-on pre-scan pass wasting a full linear scan on RW2 (symbols counted then discarded) for ~0 alloc benefit; isolation experiment shows removing pre-scan -> RW2 dead parity with gogo. Recorded under DB-18/wiresmith-bobw + mimir-wiresmith/WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md. NEXT: tempo + loki sweeps.
DB-9 COMPLETE across all 3 DBs (benchstat-grade, M4 Pro, 20-round alternated go test -c binaries, baselines = each branch's merge-base). Summary:

MIMIR (pkg/mimirpb BenchmarkUnMarshal): Marshal -7/-9% (FASTER); Unmarshal RW1 net win (skip=false -4.5% wall, -47% bytes/-40% allocs); Unmarshal RW2 +15% (only regression) = pre-scan SCAN cost (symbols counted then discarded). Isolation proves removing pre-scan -> RW2 parity. -> DB-18/wiresmith-bobw.

TEMPO (pkg/ingest decoders): OTLP decode parity; PushBytes decode +6% (pre-scan scan cost, decoder Resets); EncodeDecode +2197% time / +6788% bytes (1.25MiB->89MiB) = pre-scan EXACT-FIT grow is O(n^2) when unmarshalling into a reused/non-reset message. Isolation proves removing pre-scan -> dead match with gogo. Real production path: blockbuilder/live_traces_iter merges batches by repeated Unmarshal (O(B^2) per trace). -> NEW P1 wiresmith-zlce.

LOKI (queryrange DecodeMergeEncodeCycle + 6x logproto Merge*): straight PARITY, no regression no win (proto path masked by JSON re-encode / ns-scale merge logic; decodes into fresh messages so no pre-scan accumulation).

NET: wiresmith is at-or-better than gogo EXCEPT two pre-scan issues, both isolated and root-caused: (1) scan cost on payloads with large counted-but-not-preallocated sections (mimir RW2, tempo PushBytes) -> DB-18; (2) O(n^2) exact-fit grow on reused-message merge/decode (tempo) -> zlce P1. Both fixable in emitPreScan. Marshal is uniformly >= gogo. Full tables in each repo's WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md. Pointer-drop optimization (compat-flag headroom) analyzed and deferred by user.
Verified complete 2026-07-06: DB-9 benchstat-grade sweeps done across all three DBs (see NOTES). Regressions root-caused and spun into follow-ups: pre-scan scan-cost (DB-18 / wiresmith-bobw) and O(n^2) exact-fit grow on reused-message merge/decode (wiresmith-zlce, P1). Marshal is uniformly at-or-better than gogo; full tables in each repo's WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md. Remaining fix work lives in bobw/zlce; this benchmarking + investigation bead is complete.
close_reason: DB-9 sweeps complete across mimir/tempo/loki; regressions root-caused into bobw (DB-18) + zlce (P1). Done.

title: DB-5: presence-bitmap opt-out — (wiresmith.options.no_presence) per message/file
description: Found by Mimir migration (blocker #1, ~90% of their expdiff). fieldsPresent changes struct layout, which breaks Mimir's unsafe slice casts to Prometheus types ([]Sample<->[]promql.FPoint, FloatHistogram<->histogram.FloatHistogram — corrupting memory if layout differs) and every require.Equal(literal, unmarshalled) test. Loki hit the related W4 trap (Get<MsgField>/deep-equal observe the bitmap). Proposal: message-level (and file-level *_all) option to omit fieldsPresent; Has<F>() then falls back to zero-value comparison for message fields (gogo semantics) or is not emitted; Get<MsgField> returns &m.F unconditionally. Must benchmark: presence checks may be load-bearing in unmarshal/equal hot paths.
close_reason: Implemented on databases branch: message-level no_presence + file-level no_presence_all. Tests: option_no_presence_test.go (generator), test/basic/no_presence_test.go (layout/roundtrip/empty-drop/accessors). Docs: extensions.md + AGENTS.md. Default codegen byte-identical.

title: DB-1: -M override must exempt files from go_package/package-dir consistency checks
description: Found by Loki migration (loki-wiresmith WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md W1). collectGoPackages (generator.go:595) and computeDests' spans-multiple-directories check read literal go_package over the whole walk and ignore -M overrides, so a proto package split across Go packages (loki 'package logproto' in pkg/push + pkg/logproto, 'package proto' x3 dirs, 'package stats' x2 dirs) cannot compile even with -M pins. destForPath already prefers overrides; isSelfDest already compares resolved import paths, so emission handles split packages. Fix: files with an -M override skip the agreement checks (collectGoPackages seen-map + computeDests seen-map), get destinations registered directly; strengthen validateDestinations to reject two emitted files in one dir with different resolved import paths (override misuse). Hard gate for ~28 remaining loki protos.
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch (2a07c33): -M-pinned files exempt from collectGoPackages + computeDests agreement checks; validateDestinations rejects overrides splitting one dir. Tests: TestGenerateSplitPackageWithOverrides/WithoutOverridesRejected/OverrideSplittingDirRejected.

title: CI bench: PR soft gate + main trend artifacts
description:
No CI bench regression signal exists; the project's whole value proposition is performance, and CLAUDE.md mandates manual benchstat runs that nothing enforces.

Design (user decision: soft gate on PRs + trend artifacts on main):
- PR job: same-runner paired run — build and bench merge-base, then head, with -bench='_Ours$' -count=10 -benchtime=0.5s; compare with benchstat -alpha 0.01. Warn (continue-on-error: true + PR comment) when a statistically significant geomean regression >5% appears. Harden the threshold only after the false-positive rate on real PRs is known.
- scripts/bench-gate.sh parses benchstat -format csv.
- Main job: run the full suite and upload output as a SHA-named artifact for offline trend analysis.
- NO absolute-baseline gating across different runners — cross-runner deltas are pure noise.

Depends on B1/B2/B4/B5 landing first: gate the final benchmark set, not a moving target.
acceptance_criteria: PR bench job comments benchstat summary and warns on >5% significant geomean regression without failing the build; main uploads SHA-named full-suite artifacts; no cross-runner absolute thresholds anywhere.
notes: Audit item B3 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e. Blocked on B1, B2, B4, B5.

title: Unknown-field skip-overhead benchmark
description:
wiresmith intentionally discards unknown fields for performance (docs/design.md, 2 pinned conformance failures), but the cost/benefit has never been measured. Turn the documented design decision into a measured trade-off.

Design:
- buildCanonicalTracesDataWithUnknown(nSpans, nUnknown) in the bench inputs: inject unknown fields via span.ProtoReflect().SetUnknown(protowire-encoded high-tag fields) on the official-runtime message; the official runtime serializes unknowns verbatim, so all libraries unmarshal identical bytes.
- New shape TracesUnknown, all 4 libraries, Unmarshal only — a marshal comparison is structurally unfair (wiresmith re-serializes fewer bytes after discard); document that reasoning in bench/AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria: BenchmarkUnmarshalTracesUnknown_{Ours,Official,VTProto,GogoProto} exist; bench/AGENTS.md documents why marshal is excluded; docs/comparison.md mentions the measured skip overhead.
notes: Audit item B2 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e.

title: vtproto pool + unsafe benchmark variants
description:
The benchmark comparison uses vtproto's default config (features=marshal+unmarshal+size, Makefile generate-vtproto) — but performance-sensitive vtproto users deploy pool + unmarshal_unsafe. This is the most likely external criticism of docs/comparison.md. Verified against /Users/oleg-kozlyuk/Projects/vtprotobuf (features dir + README).

Design:
- Unsafe is additive: add unmarshal_unsafe+equal to the existing gen/vtpb features. It emits a separate UnmarshalVTUnsafe method; baseline _VTProto numbers are untouched.
- Pool must be isolated: new generate-vtproto-pool target -> gen/vtpb_pool/, scoped to trace+logs+common+resource protos, features=marshal+unmarshal+size+pool plus per-message --go-vtproto_opt=pool=<import>.<msg> for everything reachable (TracesData...Span.Event, KeyValue, AnyValue, LogsData...LogRecord).
- New bench/vtproto_variants_bench_test.go: BenchmarkUnmarshal{Traces,Logs,Histogram,SingleSpan}_VTProto{Unsafe,Pool}; pool loop = FromVTPool -> UnmarshalVT -> ReturnToVTPool.
- Wiresmith analog for fairness: _OursReuse variants (Reset() + reuse the same message).
- The anchored bench-compare regex (.*_$lib$) keeps these out of headline tables by construction.
- docs/comparison.md gets a separate 'vtproto advanced modes' table noting the semantic trades: unsafe = strings alias the input buffer; pool = manual lifecycle, use-after-return hazards.

Cross-ref wiresmith-sb8 (PERF-8 unsafe.String opt-in) as wiresmith's own unsafe analog — do NOT build it here.
acceptance_criteria: UnmarshalVTUnsafe + pooled variants benched on Traces/Logs/Histogram/SingleSpan; _OursReuse analog exists; headline bench-compare tables unchanged; docs/comparison.md advanced-modes table with semantic caveats.
notes: Audit item B1 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e. Largest review surface in the epic — land before the CI bench gate (B3).

title: Add -race test target + CI step
description:
No -race run anywhere in CI or Makefile, despite TestConcurrentMarshalSafety existing — that test proves nothing without the race detector.

Fix: add a test-race Makefile target running go test -race over ./test/peer/ ./test/basic/ ./test/differential/ with GOLANG_PROTOBUF_REGISTRATION_CONFLICT=warn, plus a CI step. Do NOT race the fuzz or bench packages (runtime cost, no concurrency to find).
acceptance_criteria: make test-race exists and passes; CI runs it on every PR.
notes: Audit item T2 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-hgl.

title: Run compiler unit tests in CI
description:
CI never runs the compiler test tier. make test (Makefile:56) runs only ./test/... ./cmd/...; ci.yml runs make build / make test / make fuzz. The 200+ tests under compiler/... only run via make coverage, which CI never invokes — generator regressions caught by those tests are invisible to CI.

Fix: add ./compiler/... to make test, or add a test-compiler target wired into the ci.yml build-test job. Update docs/testing.md to reflect what CI actually runs.
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml runs the compiler/... tests on every PR; docs/testing.md updated; CI green.
