title: Unify pointer + no_presence into one scoped `presence` tri-state option
description:
`(wiresmith.options.pointer)` and `(wiresmith.options.no_presence)` look like overlapping knobs but are actually two of **three** points on a single presence/shape axis for message fields. The redundant-feeling surface (one field-level bool, one message/file-level bool) should be consolidated into a single `presence` enum option settable at field / message / file scope.

This is a Go-shape change only — on-wire format is unaffected, like every wiresmith option.

## The three states

| State | Storage | Presence | Getter shape | gogo equiv | Current option |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| BITMAP (default) | `T` value | `XXX_fieldsPresent` bit | `*T` (`&m.F`, nil when bit clear) | *(none — wiresmith-specific)* | — |
| POINTER | `*T` | nil-check | `*T` (stored pointer, nil when unset) | `nullable=true` | `pointer` |
| FLAT | `T` value | none (present-empty doesn't round-trip) | `*T` (`&m.F`, non-nil when receiver non-nil) | `nullable=false` | `no_presence` / `no_presence_all` |

`pointer` and `no_presence` are **opposite ends** of the axis (`*T` vs value storage), not duplicates — they only superficially overlap because both drop the field's bitmap bit (`fieldsForPresence` skips pointer fields), but for opposite reasons: POINTER moves presence into the nil; FLAT discards it.

## Getter shape is uniformly `*T` — NOT part of the axis (decided 2026-06-18)

Singular message-field getters return `*T` in **all three** states. Getter shape is a constant, not a per-state/per-field decision, and there is **no** option to make a getter return value `T`.

History: `no_presence` originally also flipped message getters from `*T` to value `T` (gogo `nullable=false` getter parity), on the assumption that consumers needed gogo-shaped value getters. An audit of the three consumers (tempo/loki/mimir-wiresmith) on 2026-06-18 disproved this:

- **Zero** `return &m.`-style BITMAP getters and **zero** `XXX_fieldsPresent` fields exist in any consumer's generated code — none of them use BITMAP presence at all; every singular message field is either `pointer` (storage `*T`, getter `return m.F`) or `no_presence`/FLAT.
- Therefore **zero** consumer call sites rely on the pointer-vs-value getter shape produced by the old `no_presence` behavior.

Decision: keep getters **uniformly pointer-shape** for singular message fields, including under FLAT. Rationale — pointer getters preserve **chained-call** ergonomics (`x.GetY().GetW()`, `x.GetY().Marshal()`): the result of a value-returning getter is not addressable, so chained pointer-receiver method calls fail to compile. This deliberately **diverges from gogo `nullable=false` getter shape** (which returns value); the divergence is safe because the audit shows no reliance on value getters and it is strictly better for chaining. mimir's unsafe-cast layer keys off struct field layout, not getter return shape, so it is unaffected.

Consequence: `no_presence`/FLAT narrows to a **bitmap-only** concern — it drops the `XXX_fieldsPresent` bitmap, drops bitmap-backed `Has<F>()`, and makes present-but-empty nested messages not round-trip. It no longer touches getter shape or storage shape (storage was already value `T` in both BITMAP and FLAT; only `pointer` produces `*T`).

Implementation note: in `emit_getter.go` the `if fg.hasNoPresence(md)` value-return block is removed; FLAT message getters then fall through to the existing `if m != nil { return &m.F }` fallback (since `presenceMap` returns nil → `hasBit` false). That previously-defensive fallback becomes the live FLAT path.

## Why a single `pointer` boolean can NOT subsume `no_presence`
1. Opposite storage: FLAT yields value `T` (required by unsafe-cast consumers — Mimir `[]Sample↔[]promql.FPoint`, `UnsafeMutableLabel`); POINTER yields `*T`, which breaks exactly those consumers. (Getter shape is no longer a differentiator — both surface `*T` getters — but storage still differs.)
2. Different blast radius: `pointer` is field-level, rejected on scalars; `no_presence` is message-wide (governs scalar presence + the declared-fields-only struct layout). A `pointer_all` would leave the scalar bitmap in place → does NOT reproduce `no_presence_all`.
3. Three states, one bit: a boolean drops the BITMAP state, which is wiresmith's allocation-free proto3 message-presence model for OTLP.

## Target design
Replace both booleans with one option:
```proto
enum FieldPresence { BITMAP = 0; POINTER = 1; FLAT = 2; }
extend google.protobuf.FieldOptions   { optional FieldPresence presence = 500xx; }
extend google.protobuf.MessageOptions { optional FieldPresence presence = 500xx; }
extend google.protobuf.FileOptions    { optional FieldPresence presence_all = 500xx; }
```
The enum governs **storage + presence only**; getter shape is the `*T` constant above and is not encoded in the enum. Scope precedence: field > message > file (`presence_all`). `pointer`/`no_presence`/`no_presence_all` become deprecated aliases mapping to POINTER/FLAT.

Out of scope (separate decision, see notes): dropping the BITMAP default entirely to collapse the axis to binary — trades away the alloc-free presence model, not pursued here.
close_reason: Wontfix/superseded: the unification premise (pointer + no_presence are points on one axis needing a tri-state enum) is moot. Audit (2026-06-18) showed getter shape is not relied upon by any consumer, so getters become uniformly *T and no_presence narrows to bitmap-only — a single fix (wiresmith-der5), no new option. See epic description for the recorded decision + audit. Regen follow-up: wiresmith-h2ka.

title: Scoped (-M) override dropped for transitive imports
description: In scoped mode (positional Files set), computeDests resolves a transitively-imported file via destForReachable, which reads only the file's own go_package and ignores g.Overrides. A -M override on such a dep is silently dropped, so the emitted cross-file import uses the dep's own go_package instead of the pinned override. Fix: in the !inResults branch, route -M-pinned deps through destFor (override-aware), keep destForReachable for everything else (preserving the well-known-type special case).

notes:
DB-9 benchstat-grade evidence (mimir pkg/mimirpb BenchmarkUnMarshal, M4 Pro, 20-round alternated, p=0.000): RW2 unmarshal regresses +15.4%/+15.5% vs gogo baseline cb6dac78a3 while RW1 is a net win (skip=false -4.5% wall, -47% bytes/-40% allocs) and Marshal is FASTER (-7 to -9%). Isolation experiment (pre-scan forced off): removing the pre-scan recovers -13.7%/-13.1% on RW2 -> DEAD PARITY with gogo (10.09m/10.94m vs 10.10m/10.90m), but COSTS RW1 ~3% wall and ~2x allocations (+83-89% bytes). Root cause: WriteRequest.unmarshal pre-scan (gated len>=256) does a full linear pass counting fields 1/3/4/5; on RW2 the bulk (field 4 symbols) is counted then DISCARDED (_ = field4count, symbols go to paged storage), so RW2 pays the scan for ~0 alloc benefit. CPU profile attributes ~180ms (~5% total) to the pre-scan loop on RW2. Recommended fix: gate pre-scan off in unmarshalFromRW2 mode (or when counted fields don't drive a prealloc), or fold counting into the main decode loop. Full tables in mimir-wiresmith/WIRESMITH_MIGRATION.md.
FIX LANDED + VERIFIED (mimir-side) for the mimir RW2 symptom. Commit becca9c7 in mimir-wiresmith @wiresmith: WriteRequest.unmarshal pre-scan guard changed 'if l>=256' -> 'if l>=256 && !m.unmarshalFromRW2' (expdiff regenerated 1087->1094 lines, round-trip byte-identical, suites green). Re-bench (M4 Pro, 20-round alternated, gogo cb6dac78a3 vs fixed): RW2 unmarshal +15.4%/+15.5% -> -0.81%/-0.81% (parity), RW1 win preserved (skip=false -4.89% wall, -47% bytes, -40% allocs), Marshal -7.6%/-9.3%, RW2 bytes/allocs unchanged (confirms dropped field5 prealloc was ~0 benefit). geomean +1.86% -> -3.47%. NOTE: this is the mimir-specific runtime-flag fix; the GENERIC compiler scan-cost knob this bead tracks (e.g. tempo PushBytes +6%, or an opt-out option) is still OPEN -- WriteRequest.unmarshal serves both RW1/RW2 so the compiler can't fix it generically. Keep this bead open for the compiler-side knob.
DB-18 MECHANISM LANDED on 'databases' (compiler-side generic knob): new generated method UnmarshalNoPrescan(dAtA []byte) error. Decodes byte-identically to Unmarshal but skips the TOP-LEVEL pre-scan; nested/recursive pre-scans preserved (keeps tempo OTLP fresh-nested-slice win). Design = depth sentinel: UnmarshalNoPrescan calls unmarshal(dAtA,-1); generated pre-scan guard changed 'if l>=256' -> 'if l>=256 && depth>=0'; nested calls do depth+1 (same-pkg unmarshal, cross-pkg UnmarshalWithDepth which never re-clamps a non-negative) so -1 lifts to 0 at first hop. Emitted ONLY for messages that actually have a pre-scan (repeated msg/string/bytes or map field). Minimal regen churn: only pre-scan-bearing messages change (guard line + one new method); non-pre-scan messages byte-identical. Tests: compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal_test.go (guard form + method emitted/omitted), test/basic/no_prescan_test.go (deep-equal + no top-level exact-prealloc on fresh; pooled-cap reuse; nested events slice still exact-fit cap==count under UnmarshalNoPrescan). make build/test + test-race green. This is the generic compiler escape hatch callers (tempo PushBytesRequest reuse path, mimir RW2) can adopt instead of hand-patching. Bead stays OPEN: still need DB-9 benchstat on a quiet machine + downstream DB regen/adoption.

title: DB-12: String() is %v-based (nondeterministic for oneofs/maps) and not overridable
description: Tempo blocker #3: gogo's gostring/String was deterministic-ish and tempo overrode String() on some types (load-bearing for sort keys / span-set IDs — silent failure mode when %v changed). Options: emit String() into a separate file only when the message doesn't have a hand-written one (can't know at codegen); or emit a stable, gogo-like String(); or document StableString() pattern (tempo's workaround). Needs design.

title: DB-10: gogo-interop name for presence bitmap (XXX_fieldsPresent) or doc'd exclusion story
description: Tempo blocker #1: unexported fieldsPresent breaks gogo proto.Equal/Clone/Merge, cmp.Diff, testify on mixed gogo/wiresmith trees (~40 call sites changed during staged migration). gogo ignores XXX_-prefixed fields; renaming the bitmap XXX_fieldsPresent would interoperate. Alternatives: rely on (wiresmith.options.no_presence) (landed) for migrating messages; or document cmpopts.IgnoreFields recipe. Decide + implement.
close_reason: User decided: rename to XXX_fieldsPresent. Implemented on databases branch — gogo proto.Equal/Clone/Merge now skip the bitmap (mixed-tree interop restored); trade-off (exported field now visible to DeepEqual/cmp/testify) documented in design.md with ignoreBitmaps cmp recipe. Repo test helpers migrated to cmp.Diff.

title: Harden pre-scan backing-array reuse: cross-decode state review (DB-6)
description:
Follow-up to the DB-6 change (emit cap-guard in emitPreScan: reuse caller-provided slice capacity instead of unconditional make). User-approved design decision (2026-06-10): default-on reuse, documented contract 'Unmarshal requires Reset/fresh message unless pooling deliberately'.

Analyze adversarial and misuse scenarios:
- Cross-tenant/cross-request data exposure: stale elements from decode N-1 remain in the reused backing array beyond len; confirm no generated path (getters, Marshal, Equal, Compare, reflect) can observe beyond len; consider elements partially overwritten when decode errors mid-way.
- Error-path state: Unmarshal returns error after truncating to [:0] and appending k of n elements — message left with partial data over a pooled array; verify callers (gRPC codec, Mimir pool Put/Get) cannot leak that to another tenant.
- Interaction with preCapMax l/2 clamp: reuse path with c clamped below true count — append growth beyond c allocates fresh (fine), but confirm no path keeps both old and new arrays alive longer than before (memory retention).
- Pooled arrays retain large caps indefinitely (unbounded pool growth is the caller's problem, but document it).
- Compare against vtprotobuf's pool mode and gogo's append-merge semantics for prior art on CVEs/issues.

Output: notes in docs/design.md + fixes/tests if issues found.

title: DB-7: goproto_enum_prefix=false equivalent (unprefixed enum value constants)
description: Mimir blocker #3 (worked around with hand-written const aliases: UNKNOWN = MetricMetadata_UNKNOWN etc.). gogo's goproto_enum_prefix=false emits enum value constants without the EnumName_ prefix. Low priority: alias workaround is cheap and local. Implement only if more consumers hit it.
close_reason: Implemented on databases branch: enum-level enum_no_prefix + file-level enum_no_prefix_all with per-enum override. Tests: option_enum_no_prefix_test.go (generator), test/basic/enum_no_prefix_test.go (runtime). Docs: extensions.md + AGENTS.md. Mimir can drop its alias shims.

title: DB-6: pre-count prealloc clobbers caller-provided pooled slices — emit cap-guard
description: Found by Mimir migration (blocker #2). Generated unmarshal pre-counts repeated elements then unconditionally m.X = make([]T, 0, c), discarding caller-provided pooled backing arrays (Mimir's PreallocTimeseries pooling). Mimir patched via expdiff to 'if cap(m.X) < c { make } else { m.X = m.X[:0] }'. Also prime suspect for their +12-28% unmarshal wall-clock vs gogo (single-run, needs benchstat). Decide: emit cap-guard by default (bench it — extra branch on hot path) or behind an option. Note aliasing semantics: reusing caller backing array is what pooling wants but changes behavior for callers that kept references.
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch: cap-guard in emitPreScan (reuse via [:0] when cap fits, exact make otherwise). Tests: test/basic/prealloc_reuse_test.go. benchstat warmed-pair: geomean -0.25%, no regression (first-run pair showed +2.2% pure thermal drift — documented in commit). Docs: README + design.md pooling contract. Security follow-up: wiresmith-u4qg.

title: DB-2: cross-runtime interop — generated code calls wiresmith methods on imported gogo-generated message types
description: Found by Loki migration (W2). When a wiresmith proto imports a message whose Go type is gogo-generated (e.g. httpgrpc.Header via -M), the emitted code calls UnmarshalWithDepth/EqualWiresmith/Compare on it -> compile failure. Forces leaf-first migration and bridge types (loki worked around with wire-identical local Header + HeaderAdapter customtype). Options: (a) document leaf-first as the contract, (b) emit gogo-compatible fallback calls (Unmarshal/Size/MarshalToSizedBuffer are signature-compatible; depth tracking and Equal/Compare are not) behind a per-import opt-in, (c) nothing — close as wontfix with docs. Needs design decision.
notes: DECISION 2026-06-18 (user): NOT a migration gate; kept OPEN (do not close). Rationale: a big-bang migration of each repo's OWN proto graph eliminates the INTERNAL wiresmith→gogo import edge entirely (no leaf-first ordering needed), so 56c never fires internally. The only surviving cross-runtime boundary is EXTERNAL gogo imports from other modules (e.g. dskit httpgrpc.Header/status) that cannot be migrated at any scale — those use the established wire-identical local-copy + customtype/adapter bridge (loki util/httpgrpcpb + HeaderAdapter; mimir cqa.3 dskit-httpgrpc bridge). 56c's own option (b) fallback can't fully cover those anyway (Unmarshal/Size/MarshalToSizedBuffer are signature-compatible but depth-tracking + Equal/Compare are NOT), so the bridge is cleaner. 56c therefore stays as the optional ergonomic generalization (option b) / eventual wontfix+docs (option c) — no migration bead depends on it.

title: DB-3: unused Go import emitted when customtype replaces the only cross-file reference
description: Found by Loki migration (W3). When a field's type is replaced by (wiresmith.options.customtype) and that field was the only reference to an imported proto's Go package, the generated file still imports the now-unreferenced package -> compile error. Workaround exists (keep a dummy reference); fix: don't register the natural type's import when customtype overrides the field type, or prune unused imports before write.
close_reason: Fixed on databases branch: customtype added to suppressMessageType, mirroring stdtime. Test: TestGenerateCustomtypeSuppressesUnusedCrossFileImport.

title: Equal/Compare benchmarks
description:
Equal() and Compare() are generated for every message but never benchmarked — despite the documented +9% icache regression incident that forced Compare into a companion _compare.pb.go, and the Equal-split experiment (bd memory: equal-split-experiment-wiresmith-6ci). There is no canary that would catch a recurrence.

Design:
- New bench/equal_bench_test.go: BenchmarkEqualTraces_{Ours,Official,VTProto} — official = proto.Equal; vtproto = EqualVT (depends on B1 adding the equal feature to gen/vtpb). Skip gogo: it needs the equal_all file option threaded through the sed pipeline; document the gap in bench/AGENTS.md.
- BenchmarkCompareTraces_Ours as a regression canary for the icache-sensitive _compare.pb.go emission.
- Inputs: two independently unmarshaled copies of the same bytes — no pointer-identity shortcut.
acceptance_criteria: Equal benched across Ours/Official/VTProto on Traces; CompareTraces_Ours exists; inputs are independently allocated; gogo gap documented.
notes: Audit item B5 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e. Blocked on B1 (equal feature in gen/vtpb).

title: Size() benchmarks on all shapes
description:
Size() is the largest published lead (+1076% on Traces in docs/comparison.md) yet is measured on only 2 shapes (Traces, Maps). A claim that big resting on 2 data points is fragile under scrutiny.

Fix: add BenchmarkSize{Histogram,Logs,Gauge,Sum,ExpHistogram,Summary,Profiles,SingleSpan}_<lib> to the 4 per-library bench files (official runtime via proto.Size). Mechanical copy of the existing SizeTraces pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Size benchmarks exist for every shape x all 4 libraries; docs/comparison.md Size table covers all shapes.
notes: Audit item B4 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e.

title: Nil-receiver uniform sweep across all generated methods
description:
CLAUDE.md pins the contract 'every generated pointer-receiver method must handle nil receivers gracefully', but there is no systematic test — only ad-hoc spot checks. A new generated method (or a new field kind in an existing one) can silently break the contract.

Fix: new test/basic/nil_receiver_test.go driven by testutil constructors (add testutil.AllPanicSafeConstructors() if needed): for each message type, build a typed-nil pointer via reflect, invoke every exported method with zero-value args, assert no panic and sane results (Size()==0, Equal(nil)==true, String()=="<nil>" or equivalent). Maintain an explicit skip-list as the pinned contract for documented ProtoReflect panics — cross-reference wiresmith-q1s, which tests those documented limitations. Keep getter_reset_test.go for richer per-method semantics; this sweep is breadth, not depth.
acceptance_criteria: Sweep covers every constructor-registered message and every exported generated method; skip-list entries each cite the documenting issue/doc.
notes: Audit item T3 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-hgl. New types registered by other issues (edge fixtures wiresmith-05h) get swept for free.

title: EPIC: comparative benchmarking gap closure
description:
Umbrella for closing comparative-benchmarking gaps found in the 2026-06-10 test/bench strategy audit. wiresmith's value proposition is 'faster than vtprotobuf/gogoproto/official protobuf-go on OTel workloads' — the bench suite must survive external scrutiny.

Headline gaps: bench comparison uses vtproto's default config (features=marshal+unmarshal+size) while performance-sensitive vtproto users deploy pool + unmarshal_unsafe — the most likely external criticism of docs/comparison.md; Equal()/Compare() generated but never benchmarked (despite documented icache-regression history around Compare emission); Size() benched on only 2 shapes despite being the largest published lead (+1076% on Traces); no quantification of the unknown-field-discard trade-off; no adversarial/skewed payloads; no parallel benches; no CI bench regression signal.

User decisions: include vtproto pooled AND unsafe variants; CI bench gating = soft gate on PRs + trend artifacts on main.

Explicitly NOT in scope (do not relitigate):
- Cold-start benches (Go is AOT, no JIT warmup); compile-time benches (size report is the adequate proxy); separate GC-pressure harness (allocs/B-op + parallel benches cover where it manifests); real captured OTel data (PII/reproducibility friction; skewed-payload builders cover the legitimate criticism).
- Absolute-baseline gating across CI runners (pure noise) — only same-runner paired runs.

Suggested order: vtproto variants first (largest review surface), then unknown-field/Size/Equal benches (independent), then CI gate last (gates the final bench set).
acceptance_criteria: All child issues closed (or explicitly deferred with reasons); docs/comparison.md covers vtproto advanced modes, all-shape Size numbers, Equal benches, skewed payloads, and the measured unknown-field skip overhead; PR bench soft gate live with main trend artifacts.
notes:
Children linked via parent-child. Sibling epic: wiresmith-hgl (testing).
Suggested cross-epic implementation order (2026-06-10 audit): wiresmith-2b5 + wiresmith-jfe (CI safety net first) -> wiresmith-3mu (largest review surface) -> wiresmith-fdv/5fx/ioo (independent) -> wiresmith-64q (gates the final bench set) -> wiresmith-6el, wiresmith-0kq, wiresmith-66m (correctness properties) -> remainder by priority.

title: EPIC: testing gap closure
description:
Umbrella for closing testing gaps found in the 2026-06-10 test/bench strategy audit (3 Explore + 2 Plan agents; claims verified against the repo and local checkouts of vtprotobuf/gogoproto/protobuf).

Headline gaps: CI never runs the compiler test tier (make test runs only ./test/... ./cmd/...); no -race run despite TestConcurrentMarshalSafety; no 32-bit pass despite dedicated 32-bit safety code; no systematic nil-receiver sweep; no merge-semantics differential table (duplicate-message-field merge is the classic divergence spot); OTel-only fuzz seeds; untested option combos; no cross-package recursion depth test.

Explicitly NOT in scope (do not relitigate):
- proto2/groups/extensions/editions/JSON/text/WKT/deterministic-marshal/unknown-preservation tests — intentional non-features per docs/design.md; conformance failure list already pins the unknown-field stance.
- Wholesale port of gogoproto thetest.proto (proto2/extension-heavy); the Populate() idea captures the useful part.
- More Compare() unit tests — test/basic/compare_test.go is already thorough (360 lines incl. nil-receiver); only the cross-method consistency property remains (folded into wiresmith-6el).
- Conformance-suite expansion — 700/702 with pinned expected failures is already the strongest oracle.

Suggested order: T1/T2 CI safety net first, then correctness properties (wiresmith-6el, wiresmith-0kq, nil-receiver sweep), remainder by priority.
acceptance_criteria: All child issues closed (or explicitly deferred with reasons); CI runs compiler tests, -race, and a 386 pass; merge-semantics differential table and nil-receiver sweep exist; docs/testing.md reflects the actual CI matrix.
