Children linked via parent-child. Sibling epic: wiresmith-d0e (benchmarking).
Suggested cross-epic implementation order (2026-06-10 audit): wiresmith-2b5 + wiresmith-jfe (CI safety net first) -> wiresmith-3mu (largest review surface) -> wiresmith-fdv/5fx/ioo (independent) -> wiresmith-64q (gates the final bench set) -> wiresmith-6el, wiresmith-0kq, wiresmith-66m (correctness properties) -> remainder by priority.

title: Service-only .proto files: no _grpc.pb.go emitted
description:
## Why

PR #116 (wiresmith-df6) moved gRPC stub generation in-tree, but the file selection that gates whether wiresmith emits anything for a file (`Generator.shouldEmit` → `shouldGenerateFile` at `compiler/generator/generator.go:496`) still requires `fd.Messages().Len() > 0 || fd.Enums().Len() > 0`. A .proto that declares only services — referencing request/response messages from imports — slips through both:

- the collision-reservation loop at `compiler/generator/generator.go:437-449` skips it, so a `<name>_grpc.proto` neighbour could silently clobber the output later
- the emission loop at `compiler/generator/generator.go:451-458` skips `generateFile`, so `emitGRPC` is never invoked and no `_grpc.pb.go` is written

Before this PR, the external `protoc-gen-go-grpc` Makefile call covered this case independently. After vendoring, that path is gone, so service-only protos lose their stubs — a real regression flagged on PR #116 by Copilot at 2026-06-08T09:27:57Z.

The current test corpus (`proto/basic/service.proto`) co-locates a `Payload` message with the `BasicService` definition, so the bug doesn't manifest in CI. A split-file user (e.g. `messages.proto` + `service.proto`) would hit it immediately.

## What

1. Add a "should emit gRPC for this file" predicate parallel to `shouldEmit` that honours `g.emitFilter` and `isInternalSchemaFile` but only requires `fd.Services().Len() > 0`.
2. Update the collision-reservation loop and the emission dispatch in `Generator.Generate` so a service-only file in the emit set reserves and writes `_grpc.pb.go` even when the main/companion paths are skipped.
3. Cover the path with a regression: a service-only proto under `proto/basic/` (or a new `proto/grpc/`) that imports messages from a sibling file and is verified end-to-end via the existing checked-in-outputs test (`TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn`) plus a peer-style round-trip if cheap.

## Acceptance

- A new service-only `.proto` fixture emits `<name>_grpc.pb.go` via `make generate-ours` and nothing else.
- `TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn` covers the new fixture so future regressions fail loudly.
- `compiler/generator/generator.go::shouldGenerateFile` documentation matches behavior (current comment says "emit when messages/enums exist", new behavior also emits for services).

## Out of scope

- Reworking `shouldGenerateFile` semantics or main-file emission for service-only protos. The fix should leave the main `.pb.go` skipped (it would be header-only and misleading) and only add the gRPC path.

## References

- PR #116 review thread `PRRT_kwDOSENRc86Hxr-r` (Copilot, 2026-06-08T09:27:57Z) — `compiler/generator/generator.go:442`.
- `compiler/generator/generator.go:437-458` — collision/emit loops.
- `compiler/generator/emit_grpc.go:25-55` — the gRPC emitter that's currently unreachable for service-only files.
close_reason: Service-only .proto files now emit _grpc.pb.go (and _reflect.pb.go). shouldGenerateFile admits files with Services().Len()>0; main .pb.go write gated on fg.body.Len()>0; emitAllUnmarshalMethods skips its file-level helpers when no messages exist; emitRegistration allows service-only files through so the FileDescriptor still registers. emit_grpc.go synthesizes a sentinel Dest for the embedded wiresmith/options.proto so user files that transitively import it don't trip buildParameter's strict missing-dest check. New fixture under proto/basic/serviceonly/v1/ (split messages.proto + service.proto) + checked-in gen/serviceonly/v1/* outputs + test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_serviceonly_test.go. Note: outputs land at gen/serviceonly/v1/ rather than the plan's nominal gen/basic/serviceonly/v1/ — the source-relative output rule keys outputs off fd.Path() (= relative path under --proto_path), and getting the plan's layout would have required nesting protos at proto/basic/basic/serviceonly/v1/. The go_package option was adjusted to match the resulting disk path so Go's directory-equals-import-path stays consistent.

title: Extend (wiresmith.options.customtype) to repeated bytes/string fields
description:
**Source:** Tempo migration ([[wiresmith-sj5]]) — `pkg/tempopb/tempo.proto` declares `PushBytesRequest.traces` as `repeated bytes [(gogoproto.customtype) = "PreallocBytes"]`. This is the ingest hot path: every span push request goes through it. The customtype wraps a pooled `[]byte` so the per-trace allocations are reused across requests.

[[wiresmith-06z]] shipped customtype for **singular** bytes/string only — repeated is explicitly rejected (`Reject customtype on map/oneof/repeated with a clear error`). [[wiresmith-hnr]] covers customtype on **message** fields, which is a fundamentally different wire-encoding contract (the user type owns sub-tags) and is not the case we need here.

This bead extends customtype to the `repeated bytes` (and naturally `repeated string`) case so adopters with gogoproto-style `repeated bytes [(gogoproto.customtype) = ...]` annotations can migrate the Go-side pooling type unchanged.

**The specific shape:**

- Proto:  `repeated bytes traces = 2 [(wiresmith.options.customtype) = "X"];`
- Go:     `Traces []X` — slice of the user type, **one user struct per repeated element**.
- Each user-type element owns exactly **one length-delimited bytes payload** (just the bytes, no inner sub-tags). Same Marshal/Unmarshal contract as the singular bytes customtype from [[wiresmith-06z]], just emitted inside the repeated loop.
- This is NOT the `repeated <MessageType> [customtype=X]` case (where X owns a full submessage including sub-tags). That's tracked by [[wiresmith-hnr]] and uses different plumbing.

**Scope:**
- Allow `(wiresmith.options.customtype)` on `repeated bytes` and `repeated string` fields.
- Generated field becomes `[]X` with per-element `SizeWiresmith` / `MarshalWiresmith` / `UnmarshalWiresmith` / `EqualWiresmith` / `CompareWiresmith` calls.
- Pre-scan pre-allocation still applies (count elements, allocate exact slice capacity).
- Reject the `repeated <message>` case — defer to [[wiresmith-hnr]]. Reject `repeated <scalar numeric>` — no use case.

**Acceptance:**
- Fixture proto with `repeated bytes` + customtype and a Go type implementing the wiresmith customtype interface.
- Round-trip test, wire-compat against the equivalent non-customtype proto compiled by gogofaster.
- Update `docs/extensions.md` to drop the singular-only caveat for bytes/string customtype.

**Out of scope:**
- Customtype on `repeated <message>` — [[wiresmith-hnr]].
- Customtype on map keys/values.
- `(gogoproto.customtype)` alias recognition (same deferral as [[wiresmith-06z]]).
notes: Already implemented by wiresmith-hnr (PR #117). That bead extended customtype to singular and repeated bytes/string/message in one pass — the per-element wire envelope is length-delimited regardless of source kind, so the repeated loop is kind-agnostic. proto/basic/customtype.proto declares RepeatedCustomTypeHolder with repeated bytes+UUID and repeated string+Tag; test/basic/customtype_repeated_test.go covers field-type swap, round-trip, wire-compat against a plain repeated control field, empty round-trip, size-0 element preservation, and nil-receiver getter; docs/extensions.md no longer carries the singular-only caveat.
close_reason: implemented by wiresmith-hnr (PR #117)

title: Renovate: group gomod 'go' + dockerfile 'golang' into single PR
description: Renovate raises separate, time-skewed PRs for the gomod 'go' directive and the 'golang' base image in test/conformance/Dockerfile. The gomod PR is fast-tracked (0-day) while the dockerfile digest update waits on the default 7-day minimum, so the conformance build breaks on every Go patch release until someone manually bumps the SHA pin (fd42a82). Group both deps under one fast-tracked rule so a single PR carries the matched updates.
close_reason: Closed

title: PERF: Split Equal() methods into separate _equal.pb.go (icache hypothesis)
description:
Mirror the _reflect.pb.go split for Equal() methods. Equal is never on the Marshal/Unmarshal hot path; co-locating ~30-50 lines per message in the main .pb.go may push hot Marshal/Unmarshal code into different cache sets. Hypothesis: splitting Equal into a companion _equal.pb.go gives a similar (smaller-magnitude than reflect's 7-14%) throughput win.

Method:
1. Baseline: GOLANG_PROTOBUF_REGISTRATION_CONFLICT=warn go test ./bench -bench='Marshal|Unmarshal.*_Ours' -benchmem -count=20 -benchtime=1s -run='^$' > /tmp/before.txt
2. Add equalBody/equalImports buffers to FileGenerator (mirroring reflectBody/reflectImports). Route emitAllEqualMethods + bytes/math lazy imports to the new buffer.
3. Add outputEqualPathFor + write the companion file with a documentation banner similar to emitReflectFileBanner.
4. Regenerate everything: make generate-ours
5. After: capture same filter with -count=20 -benchtime=1s
6. benchstat /tmp/before.txt /tmp/after.txt and document outcome.

Decision criteria: keep the split only if geomean improves with no targeted bench regressing > noise floor (use SizeTraces/SizeMap as noise controls — Equal isn't on Size path either).
notes: Result (clean serial run, count=20, benchtime=1s, Apple M4 Pro, 2026-06-01T22:51Z): geomean -1.75%, no alloc changes. Biggest hot-path wins: UnmarshalSingleSpan -6.23%, MarshalProfiles -5.45%, UnmarshalLogs -5.13%, MarshalSingleSpan -4.40%, UnmarshalHistogram -4.38%, MarshalMap -3.76%, UnmarshalTraces -3.73%. Tiny regressions: MarshalSummary +0.92%, UnmarshalProfiles +0.73%. Caveat: Size benchmarks (Equal can't touch them) also moved -1.5% to -3.3%, so part of the gain is generic code-layout shift after removing Equal from the main .pb.go; the largest Marshal/Unmarshal gains still exceed that noise floor. Net: keep the split — clear win, mirrors the documented _reflect.pb.go rationale. Full numbers in /tmp/equal_split_summary.txt during the session.
close_reason: Shipped via PR. Geomean -1.75% on hot Marshal/Unmarshal, zero alloc change, mirrors the documented _reflect.pb.go split rationale.

title: Emit Compare() method per generated message (gogo compare equivalent)
description:
**Source:** Tempo `pkg/tempopb/backendwork.proto` — several messages opt in with `option (gogoproto.compare) = true;`. The generated `Compare(other) int` returns -1/0/1 ordering, used by Tempo to deterministically order jobs in the scheduler.

**What gogo does:** When `(gogoproto.compare)` is set on a message, generates `func (this *M) Compare(that interface{}) int` that walks fields in tag order, compares each (numeric < / > / ==, byte slices via `bytes.Compare`, nested messages via their own `Compare`, strings via lexicographic order), and returns the first non-zero comparison.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Always emit `Compare(other interface{}) int` per generated message (or gate it on `option (wiresmith.options.compare) = true` if always-emit grows binary too much).
- Field order: ascending wire tag.
- Repeated: compare by length first, then by element pairwise.
- Map: not orderable — either skip in compare or sort entries by key.
- Oneof: compare by variant index first, then by payload if same variant.
- Float/double: bit-exact comparison via `math.Float{32,64}bits` (same convention as the existing nil-safe `Equal`).
- Nil-safety: `nil.Compare(nil) == 0`; `nil.Compare(non-nil) == -1` (matches gogo).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Tests for ordering: ascending/descending field order, mixed types, repeated, oneof, nested message recursion.
- Tests for tiebreaks: nil vs zero vs non-zero, NaN handling matches Equal's bit-exact rule.
- Bench addition if always-emitted: confirm `bench` numbers don't regress materially.

**Why this blocks Tempo migration:** Tempo's `backendwork` scheduler relies on `Compare()` for canonical job ordering. Without it, the migration would require hand-writing comparisons across all scheduler-tracked message types.
notes:
Implemented as message-level (wiresmith.options.compare) + file-level (wiresmith.options.compare_all). Always-emit was tried first but added +9% geomean to OTel Marshal/Unmarshal/Size hot paths (UnmarshalMap +14%, MarshalSingleSpan +13%) via icache pressure on the linked binary, even though Compare is never called on those paths. Gating reduces the visible regression to within run-to-run noise (~2%). Generator computes the transitive closure over message-typed field references so referenced inner messages auto-emit Compare; users only annotate the root.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/100
Re-implemented: Compare goes into a sibling <name>_compare.pb.go (parallel to _reflect.pb.go) instead of the main .pb.go. Always-emit, no opt-in. benchstat (M4 Pro, count=20): geomean -0.06% on OTel hot benchmarks — separate compilation unit lets the linker keep Compare's bytes away from Marshal/Unmarshal so the icache pressure that motivated the original gate disappears.
close_reason: implemented: Compare(other) int emitted on every message into a sibling <name>_compare.pb.go (same split as _reflect.pb.go) — separate compilation unit avoids icache pressure on the marshal hot path. benchstat geomean -0.06% on OTel benchmarks.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.embed) field option (gogo embed equivalent)
description:
**Source:** Tempo `tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto`, field `CompactedBlockMeta.block_meta`:
```proto
BlockMeta block_meta = 1[(gogoproto.embed) = true, (gogoproto.nullable) = false, (gogoproto.jsontag) = ""];
```
The Go API exposes `CompactedBlockMeta` as an inline struct of `BlockMeta`, so callers can do `c.TenantID` rather than `c.BlockMeta.TenantID`.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.embed) = true` on a message field produces a Go-side embedded struct (`BlockMeta` instead of `BlockMeta BlockMeta`). Wire format is unchanged (still field number 1, still a length-delimited message); only the Go representation is affected.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.embed) = true` field option to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits `BlockMeta` (type only, no field name) inside the parent struct. Marshal/unmarshal target the embedded struct via promoted field access.
- Reject combinations that don't make sense: embedded scalar/enum/bytes/string, embedded repeated, embedded oneof variant, embedded map, embedded optional.
- Equality and marshal/unmarshal must continue to work transparently — the field still has its tag and wire number.

**Migration ergonomics:** accept `(gogoproto.embed)` as alias.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Fixture proto in `proto/basic/embed.proto` matching the Tempo pattern (parent with one embedded message + own scalar fields).
- Round-trip test verifies wire compatibility with an equivalent non-embedded proto compiled by gogofaster.
- Tests verify both `parent.FieldFromEmbedded` (promoted) and `parent.BlockMeta.FieldFromEmbedded` (explicit) reads compile.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.

**Why this blocks Tempo migration:** `CompactedBlockMeta` callers in `tempodb/` read `c.TenantID`, `c.BlockID` directly. Without embed support, those would need `c.BlockMeta.TenantID` everywhere — large invasive refactor.
close_reason: cancelled by user

title: Add (wiresmith.options.customname) field option (gogo customname equivalent)
description:
**Source:** Tempo proto files (`tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto`) — used to keep Go-side names consistent with hand-coded Go API (e.g. `(gogoproto.customname) = "BlockID"` keeps the field `BlockID` even though the proto field is `block_id`; `(gogoproto.customname) = "TenantID"` for `tenant_id`).

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.customname) = "BlockID"` overrides the Go-side field/getter name. By default protoc converts `block_id` -> `BlockId`; this option forces it to `BlockID` (initialisms preserved). Critical when matching existing Go API surface.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.customname) = "BlockID"` field option to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator uses the supplied identifier verbatim for the field name and all generated method names (`GetBlockID()`, `HasBlockID()` etc).
- Validate the value is a legal Go exported identifier.

**Migration ergonomics:** accept `(gogoproto.customname)` as alias so Tempo can migrate annotations gradually.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Fixture proto in `proto/basic/customname.proto` with field-name overrides on scalar, message, repeated, oneof variant fields.
- Test that emitted Go API matches `customname` value exactly across `Get*`, `Has*`, `Equal`, marshal/unmarshal.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.

**Why this blocks Tempo migration:** Tempo callers reference `BlockMeta.BlockID`, `BackendBlock.TenantID`, etc. across thousands of call sites in `tempodb/`, `modules/`, `pkg/`. Auto-generated `BlockId`/`TenantId` would force a sweeping rename or break every consumer.
notes:
Shipped: (wiresmith.options.customname) at extension 50033. Single goFieldName(fd) helper centralizes the override — 17 snakeToPascal(fd.Name()) call sites across emit_*.go now route through it. The oneof variant wrapper TYPE name (Foo_TenantId) deliberately stays anchored to the proto field name; only the inner field and its accessors take the rename. Validation: must be a valid exported Go identifier (uppercase-first letter, rest [letter|digit|_]). gogoproto.customname alias deferred (no gogo.proto infra — same call as jsontag/customtype).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/104
close_reason: implemented

title: Add (wiresmith.options.jsontag) field option (gogo jsontag equivalent)
description:
**Source:** Tempo proto files (`pkg/tempopb/*.proto`, `tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto`) — used pervasively, especially in `tempodb/backend/v1/v1.proto` where every field carries a custom JSON tag for HTTP API stability (e.g. `(gogoproto.jsontag) = "blockID"`, `(gogoproto.jsontag) = "totalObjects"`).

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.jsontag) = "name"` overrides the Go struct tag `json:"..."` value on the generated field. Critical when proto field names use snake_case but the JSON wire contract expects camelCase or a legacy name.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.jsontag) = "blockID"` field option to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits the supplied string verbatim as the `json:"..."` tag on the field, replacing the default (which is the proto field name lowerCamelCased).
- Allowed values must be valid Go struct tag content (no embedded quote characters). Reject empty strings unless `=""` is the explicit intent (matches gogo).

**Migration ergonomics:** consider also accepting `(gogoproto.jsontag)` as a recognized alias so Tempo doesn't need to rewrite every annotation in a single PR.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Add fixture proto to `proto/basic/` covering jsontag on scalar, message, repeated, and map fields.
- Round-trip test that the emitted struct tag matches the annotation literal.
- Update `docs/extensions.md` to document the new option.

**Why this blocks Tempo migration:** Tempo's HTTP API (`tempodb/backend/v1`) serializes block metadata to JSON with specific tag names — `blockID`, `dataEncoding`, `totalObjects`, etc. Without the option, Go's default tag generation would change every wire-visible JSON field name, breaking backwards compatibility with existing clients.
notes:
Implementation summary: jsontag extension at 50031 (string), per-field override consulted in tag.go (fieldTag + mapFieldTag converted to *FileGenerator methods). Empty string is a legal explicit value (gogo passthrough — emits json:"", which encoding/json treats as "no tag given", NOT an opt-out; use "-" for the encoding/json opt-out idiom). Only validation is backtick rejection (would terminate the raw-string struct tag); quotes are safe via %q. Default unchanged: <proto_name>,omitempty. Custom values are verbatim — caller supplies any ,omitempty themselves. Fixture proto/basic/jsontag.proto covers scalar, message, repeated, map, oneof variant (added 2026-06-02 to address Copilot review), and the "-" opt-out. Tests in test/basic/jsontag_test.go (reflection-based struct-tag assertions + roundTrip + oneof) and compiler/generator/option_jsontag_test.go (backtick rejection + empty-string acceptance). gogoproto.jsontag alias deferred — would require embedding gogo.proto and a separate-extension-number infrastructure; not justified by this single option, file separately if Tempo migration needs it.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/102
close_reason: implemented

title: Emit XXX_* methods + XXX_unrecognized field (gogo proto.Buffer compat)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Gogo method set". User confirmed Mimir uses this 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** emits a set of deprecated-but-used internal methods that satisfy the older `proto.Marshaler`/`proto.Buffer` interface shape. Required by older gogo/Mimir code that hasn't been updated to the modern `proto.Marshal` API.

**Methods to emit (per message):**
- `XXX_Marshal(b []byte, deterministic bool) ([]byte, error)` — wraps `m.MarshalToSizedBuffer`.
- `XXX_Unmarshal(b []byte) error` — wraps `m.Unmarshal`.
- `XXX_Size() int` — wraps `m.Size`.
- `XXX_Merge(src proto.Message)` — merge src into m via marshal+unmarshal (or via a smarter per-field merge).
- `XXX_DiscardUnknown()` — no-op (wiresmith already discards unknown fields on unmarshal per design).
- `XXX_unrecognized []byte` field — this is the tricky one: it's a struct field, not a method. Used by older gogo to roundtrip unknown fields. **Decision needed:** either emit the field as always-nil (preserving the API shape but not the behavior, since wiresmith discards unknown fields by design — `docs/design.md:34`), or gate this whole bead behind a flag `--gogo-compat-xxx` so users opting in accept the surface without the behavior.

**Recommendation:** gate behind a flag (`--emit-gogo-xxx` or option). XXX_* are deprecated; consumers who need them should opt in explicitly. Default off.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All XXX_* methods compile and pass through to existing implementations.
- `XXX_unrecognized` field is always nil after Unmarshal (consistent with the "unknown fields discarded" design decision).
- Test that an older gogo-style consumer (`proto.Buffer{}`) successfully Marshal/Unmarshal a wiresmith message via XXX_* methods.
- Document the deprecation status and the unknown-field caveat in `docs/generated-api.md` and `docs/extensions.md`.

**Note for future deletion:** XXX_* are deprecated in gogo. Once Mimir modernizes its proto integration, this bead's output can be removed. Consider scoping the emission behind a flag from day one to make removal painless.
notes:
**File layout decision:** XXX_* methods (and the XXX_unrecognized field) MUST be emitted to a separate file per source proto: `<name>_gogo_compat.pb.go` (sibling to `<name>.pb.go`). Rationale: (1) keeps the deprecated gogo-compat surface clearly separated and auditable; (2) makes removal trivial when Mimir modernizes — delete the file, regenerate, done; (3) lets us gate emission on the `--emit-gogo-xxx` flag (or per-file option) by simply not writing the second file when off; (4) matches the protoc-gen-go-grpc precedent of `<name>_grpc.pb.go` co-existing in the same Go package.

**XXX_unrecognized caveat:** since it's a struct field, it cannot live in a separate file — fields must be declared with the struct in `<name>.pb.go`. Options: (a) drop the field entirely (XXX_unrecognized is always-nil in wiresmith anyway because unknown fields are discarded per design); (b) emit the field unconditionally in `<name>.pb.go` but only emit XXX_* methods in `<name>_gogo_compat.pb.go`. Recommend (a) — the field has no behavior anyway, and emitting it forces every consumer to see the stub. Document that gogo consumers checking `m.XXX_unrecognized != nil` to detect unknown-field presence will need to migrate; that check is meaningless on wiresmith messages.
close_reason: Cancelled: XXX_* methods are deprecated and only useful for gogo's unknown-field preservation, which wiresmith intentionally does not support (docs/design.md). Mimir consumers that rely on XXX_unrecognized to detect unknown fields will need to migrate off that pattern rather than have wiresmith emit a stub field that's always nil.

title: Emit GoString() method per generated message (gogo-style)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Gogo method set". User confirmed Mimir uses this 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** emits `func (m *Foo) GoString() string` returning a Go-syntax representation that round-trips through `fmt.Sprintf("%#v", m)`. Distinct from `String()` (text proto format). Used by Mimir for debugging output and golden-file test fixtures.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- New generator emit phase: `emit_gostring.go`.
- For each generated message, emit:
  ```go
  func (m *Foo) GoString() string {
      if m == nil { return "(*Foo)(nil)" }
      s := make([]string, 0, N)
      s = append(s, "&pkg.Foo{")
      s = append(s, fmt.Sprintf("FieldA: %#v,", m.FieldA))
      ...
      s = append(s, "}")
      return strings.Join(s, " ")
  }
  ```
- Handle oneofs by GoString-ing the active variant struct.
- Handle maps and repeated by GoString-ing the slice/map literal.
- Skip unset fields (matches gogo behavior — only emit fields that are present per the bitmap).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Round-trip test: `fmt.Sprintf("%#v", &foo)` is parseable Go (compile-check the output as a string in a test).
- Nil-safe (returns `"(*Foo)(nil)"`).
- Unit test in `compiler/generator/emit_gostring_test.go`.
- Update `AGENTS.md` "Generated API surface" and `docs/generated-api.md` to list `GoString()` alongside `String()`.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add (wiresmith.options.stdtime) — google.protobuf.Timestamp as time.Time
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers". Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.stdtime) = true` on a `google.protobuf.Timestamp` field emits a `time.Time` field directly (instead of `*timestamppb.Timestamp`), with helpers that convert seconds + nanos to/from `time.Time`. Mimir uses this on sample timestamps, query time ranges, etc.

**Distinct from BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`):** parallel to stdduration — that issue gives structural `Timestamp` support; this gives the ergonomic `time.Time` substitution.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.stdtime) = true` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Detect on Timestamp-typed fields; reject elsewhere.
- Emit `Foo time.Time`.
- Marshal: `t.Unix()` → seconds, `t.Nanosecond()` → nanos, encode as length-delimited Timestamp sub-message.
- Unmarshal: read seconds + nanos, `time.Unix(seconds, int64(nanos)).UTC()`. UTC is the protobuf canonical timezone; document.
- Be careful about the zero value: `time.Time{}` is January 1, year 1 UTC. proto3 default semantics treat unset Timestamp as Unix epoch zero. Pick one and document — gogo treats zero `time.Time` as "not set" via separate presence; wiresmith should follow.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/stdtime.proto` fixture (shared with stdduration) with a Timestamp field.
- Round-trip: now-ish times, zero, far-past (year 1), far-future (year 9999). Cross-library check vs official `timestamppb.Timestamp`.
- Decide and document the zero-value/presence semantics; lock it down with a test.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add (wiresmith.options.stdduration) — google.protobuf.Duration as time.Duration
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers". Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.stdduration) = true` on a `google.protobuf.Duration` field emits a `time.Duration` field directly in the struct (instead of `*durationpb.Duration`), with marshal/unmarshal helpers that convert seconds + nanos to/from `time.Duration`. Mimir uses this for retention windows, lookback durations, query timeouts.

**Distinct from BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`):** that issue covers structural WKT support — making `google.protobuf.Duration` work as a regular nested message. This issue is the *ergonomic* alternative: when the user opts in, the field becomes `time.Duration` directly, bypassing the `Duration` struct entirely. Both should be available.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.stdduration) = true` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator detects the option on a field whose type is `google.protobuf.Duration` (reject elsewhere) and emits `Foo time.Duration` instead.
- Marshal: encode `time.Duration` → seconds + nanos as a length-delimited Duration sub-message.
- Unmarshal: decode the sub-message and convert seconds + nanos back to `time.Duration` (saturating on overflow — `time.Duration` is int64 nanos, while proto allows up to ~10000 years; document the truncation behavior).
- Import `time` only when the option appears in the file.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/stdtime.proto` (shared fixture with stdtime bead) with a Duration field annotated.
- Round-trip test covers normal values, zero, negative, and the truncation boundary.
- Cross-library check: encode with wiresmith, decode with official protobuf into a `*durationpb.Duration` — values match.
- Document migration ergonomics (gogoproto alias) under `docs/extensions.md`.
notes:
Implemented as a sibling of stdtime: protohelpers.{Size,Encode,Decode}StdDuration handle the Duration envelope inline (no dependency on WKT support / wiresmith-7m6). Presence sentinel is d == 0 (proto3 default-suppression); decode saturates to math.{Max,Min}Int64 on overflow. Acceptance criteria covered except (gogoproto.stdduration) alias recognition — same deferral as wiresmith-06z left for the customtype alias. Worth a follow-up bead if a real migration needs it.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/118
close_reason: implemented

title: Add (wiresmith.options.casttype) field option (gogo casttype equivalent)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Pointer semantics" section. Confirmed needed by user 2026-05-29.

**What gogo does:** `(gogoproto.casttype) = "MyAlias"` casts the generated field to a different Go type name *without* changing the wire encoding. Used for type-safe int aliases like `type MetricType int32`, distinguishing IDs by domain (`type UserID int64`, `type AccountID int64`), etc. Unlike customtype, the wire-level kind stays exactly what proto says — only the Go-side name changes.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.casttype) = "MyAlias"` to `compiler/generator/embed/wiresmith/options.proto`.
- Generator emits `Foo MyAlias` instead of `Foo int64` (or whatever the proto kind would imply). All marshal/unmarshal/size code wraps the underlying kind in a cast (`int64(m.Foo)`, `MyAlias(v)` on read).
- The aliased type must be a Go `type X = Underlying` (alias) or `type X Underlying` (defined type). Both work because the wire encoding stays unchanged.
- Restrict to scalar kinds (int32/int64/uint32/uint64/sint32/sint64/fixed*/sfixed*/float/double/bool/string/bytes); reject on message/map/oneof/enum.

**Migration ergonomics:** same as customtype — consider accepting `(gogoproto.casttype)` as an alias.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `proto/basic/casttype.proto` fixture: cast int64 → custom alias, cast string → custom alias, cast bytes → custom alias.
- Test in `test/basic/casttype_test.go` covering round-trip + Equal + cross-library (wiresmith ↔ official protobuf, since wire format is unchanged).
- Reject on disallowed kinds with a clear error.
- Update `docs/extensions.md`.
notes:
Implemented as a wrapper FieldType (types.CastType) that delegates EmitSize/EmitMarshal to the underlying scalar — Go's type conversion bridges the alias-to-underlying gap at uint64/len/etc. call sites. EmitUnmarshal wraps Inner.CastExpr with the alias name; EmitEqual/EmitCompare delegate except for bytes, where the wrapper inserts explicit []byte() casts so bytes.Equal/Compare keep working. v1 scope: integer scalars, sint*, fixed*/sfixed*, bool, string, bytes. Float/double deferred — they emit math.Float*bits, which does not accept a defined-type argument; supporting it cleanly needs every emit site touched, not just a FieldType wrapper. Enum deferred — open question on whether casttype should enforce declared enum constants. Acceptance criteria met (int64/string/bytes fixture + round-trip + Equal/Compare + cross-library wire compat via control comparison). (gogoproto.casttype) alias recognition deferred (same deferral as wiresmith-06z for customtype).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/119
close_reason: implemented

title: Verify protoc-gen-go-grpc compatibility with wiresmith messages
description:
**Goal:** Empirically validate that `protoc-gen-go-grpc`-generated client/server stubs interoperate end-to-end with wiresmith-generated message types. If verification passes, native gRPC stub generation (wiresmith-36b umbrella + GRPC-1..4 children) becomes unnecessary.

**Hypothesis (informs the test plan):**

1. wiresmith messages register a `protoimpl.MessageInfo` fast-path methods table (`docs/design.md:29`).
2. `google.golang.org/grpc/encoding/proto` calls `proto.Marshal(msg)` which dispatches via the methods table → wiresmith's hand-rolled Marshal. No reflection slow-path on the gRPC hot path.
3. `protoc-gen-go-grpc` honors `option go_package`, so wiresmith and protoc-gen-go-grpc can emit into the same Go package (`<name>.pb.go` and `<name>_grpc.pb.go` — different files, no symbol collision).

**Test plan:**

1. Add `proto/basic/service.proto` with: 1 unary RPC, 1 client-stream, 1 server-stream, 1 bidi RPC. Each carrying real OTLP-ish payloads (not empty messages).
2. Wire `make generate-ours` to invoke wiresmith for messages AND `protoc-gen-go-grpc --go-grpc_out=gen --go-grpc_opt=paths=source_relative` for stubs, targeting the same output root.
3. Add `test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go`:
   - In-process `grpc.NewServer` registered via the protoc-gen-go-grpc-emitted `RegisterFooServer`.
   - Client via the emitted `NewFooClient(conn)`.
   - Round-trip all 4 streaming modes; assert wire-level equality with the wiresmith Equal method.
   - Sanity-check `grpc.NewServer().GetServiceInfo()` lists the wiresmith services (uses GlobalFiles registry).
4. Benchmark: compare per-RPC latency of (wiresmith + protoc-gen-go-grpc) vs (protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc) on the same .proto. Expect wiresmith side to be faster on Marshal/Unmarshal (fast-path methods table), parity-or-slightly-slower on call setup.

**Pass criteria (ALL must hold):**

- Compile cleanly with both code generators emitting into the same package.
- All 4 streaming modes round-trip without error.
- Server reflection (`grpc.reflection.v1.ServerReflection`) lists the wiresmith services.
- Bench shows no regression vs the protoc-gen-go baseline; Marshal/Unmarshal columns should show wiresmith's usual wins.

**Outcome paths:**

- **PASS** → close `wiresmith-36b` + GRPC-1..4 (`1w4`, `nyd`, `dxi`, `3q1`) as superseded with reason "protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified — see <this issue>". Add a "Using wiresmith with gRPC" section to README/docs explaining the recipe. Net effort: ~200 LOC test + docs vs ~1500 LOC of new emit code.
- **PARTIAL FAIL** → identify the specific gap (e.g. a gRPC interceptor that calls field-level reflection and panics). If the gap is fixable in wiresmith in <1 day, file a follow-up bead and fix it; then this verification re-runs. The umbrella stays blocked.
- **HARD FAIL** → keep `wiresmith-36b` umbrella scope; this bead closes as "investigated, native gen required". Document the blocker in the umbrella so future contributors know why we chose the heavier path.

**Why P2:** Same priority as the umbrella it gates — a fast verification is on the critical path for Mimir integration whichever way it resolves.
notes: Dependents (wiresmith-36b umbrella + GRPC-1..4 children: 1w4, nyd, dxi, 3q1) MUST be closed as superseded if this verification passes. The umbrella + children represent ~1500 LOC of new emit code; verification + docs is ~200 LOC. Reuse-first is the strict win unless a concrete gap surfaces during testing. Whoever closes this bead with PASS should run `bd close wiresmith-36b wiresmith-1w4 wiresmith-nyd wiresmith-dxi wiresmith-3q1 --reason="superseded by <this-id>: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified"` in one command.
close_reason: PASS: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end. wiresmith messages register a protoimpl.MessageInfo fast-path Methods table; gRPC's proto codec dispatches Marshal/Unmarshal through it (no slow-path field reflection). All 4 streaming modes round-trip rich payloads; Server.GetServiceInfo lists the wiresmith Echo service (powers reflection v1 ListServices). Implementation: 1-line strip of fdp.Service in serializeFileDescriptor (no hot-path impact — service-less protos produce byte-identical rawDesc), plus test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go (5 tests), proto/basic/service.proto, Makefile + CI wiring. Superseded wiresmith-36b/1w4/nyd/dxi/3q1.

title: Multi --proto_path support (compile across multiple proto roots)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Multi-path proto resolution" (lines 43-44) — Mimir integration needs `ProtoPaths []string` and `ProtoFiles []string` on `Generator` plus `multiPathResolver` for cross-directory proto imports.

**Why:** Today wiresmith's CLI accepts a single `--proto_path` directory and walks it recursively (`compiler/generator/generator.go:buildImportMapping`). Mimir's proto layout uses multiple roots (vendor/, internal/, etc.) — needs `-I=root1 -I=root2` style like `protoc`. The current single-root walker forces consumers to either symlink/copy or use Tempo's `.patched-proto` workaround. Mimir integration is on the near-term roadmap (confirmed 2026-05-29) so this is unblocking work.

**Approach:**
- Allow repeated `--proto_path=` flags (matches `protoc -I=path1 -I=path2`); colon-separated alternative also acceptable.
- In `buildImportMapping`, walk each root and union the results, keying by the unresolved import path (e.g. `common/v1/foo.proto`), not the absolute path.
- When an `import "foo.proto"` is resolved, check roots in order; first match wins (matches `protoc`). Document the precedence rule.
- Collision policy: if the same import path resolves to different absolute paths across roots, fail loudly with both candidate paths in the error — silent shadowing is the bug class we want to avoid.

**Interaction with existing closed work:**
- `wiresmith-7eh` (positional `.proto` paths) already lets users scope emission to specific files — keep that. After this bead, the positional paths still must live under *some* `--proto_path` root (any of them).
- `wiresmith-j0h` umbrella (source-relative output paths) is orthogonal — source-relative computation needs to pick the *matching* root per file, which falls out naturally if `buildImportMapping` retains the root each file came from.

**Out of scope:**
- Virtual filesystem (reading from tarball / module cache).
- Buf-style module resolution (covered by BUF-EPIC `wiresmith-c4r`).
- Auto-resolving `google/protobuf/*` from a built-in well-known types root (covered by `wiresmith-7m6` BUF-2.2).

**Validation:**
- Add `proto/basic/multi_root_a/foo.proto` + `proto/basic/multi_root_b/bar.proto` where `bar.proto` imports `foo.proto`.
- CLI test invoking with `--proto_path=proto/basic/multi_root_a --proto_path=proto/basic/multi_root_b` and asserting both files generate.
- Negative test: same import path present in both roots → expect explicit collision error.
notes:
Implemented as Generator.ProtoDirs []string (rename from ProtoDir; internal API, no deprecation shim). buildImportMapping now walks each root and union-keys by canonical import path. Strict collision policy: same key from two different absolute paths => 'duplicate import key' error naming both paths, instead of protoc's first-wins shadowing. Passing the same root twice (e.g. colon-split typo) is de-duped silently via keyToPath identity check. CLI: --proto_path and -I are repeatable; a single occurrence also accepts ':'-separated entries; default ['proto'] preserves historical no-flag behavior. Source-relative output paths fall out naturally from fd.Path() — no per-file root tracking needed downstream.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/122
Correction (review feedback on PR #122): the single-occurrence shorthand splits on os.PathListSeparator — ':' on Unix, ';' on Windows — not a hard-coded ':'. This keeps Windows drive-letter paths like 'C:\proto' from being mangled into 'C' and '\proto'.
close_reason: implemented

title: GRPC-4: Support streaming RPCs (client-stream, server-stream, bidi)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).
**Depends on:** GRPC-2 (client), GRPC-3 (server).

**What:** Extend client and server emission to handle all three streaming modes. Without this, the umbrella is unusable for Mimir's push API and any other streaming consumer.

**Client side, per streaming method:**
```go
// server-stream
type Foo_ListServer = grpc.ServerStreamingClient[ListResponse]
func (c *fooClient) List(ctx context.Context, in *ListRequest, opts ...grpc.CallOption) (Foo_ListClient, error)

// client-stream / bidi: similar with grpc.ClientStreamingClient / grpc.BidiStreamingClient
```
Use the typed `grpc.ClientStream` helpers (Go 1.21+ generic gRPC, available in `google.golang.org/grpc` ≥ 1.65).

**Server side, per streaming method:**
- Emit a stream handler thunk that wraps the `grpc.ServerStream` in the typed iterator.
- Append `grpc.StreamDesc{StreamName, Handler, ServerStreams, ClientStreams}` entries to the service's `Streams` slice (populated empty in GRPC-3).

**Key requirements:**
- All three streaming kinds in one bead — they share infrastructure.
- Method path format unchanged (`/pkg.Svc/Method`).
- ServiceDesc.Streams populated correctly: server-stream → `ServerStreams: true`, client-stream → `ClientStreams: true`, bidi → both true.

**Validation:** Per-stream-kind integration tests, all three in `test/peer/grpc_streaming_test.go`. Drive a 10-message round-trip per direction.
close_reason: superseded by wiresmith-dei: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end (4 streaming modes round-trip + reflection ListServices); no native gRPC stub generation needed

title: GRPC-3: Generate gRPC server interface + ServiceDesc + handler thunks
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).
**Depends on:** GRPC-1 (descriptor parsing).

**What:** Emit, per service:
```go
type FooServer interface {
    Bar(context.Context, *BarRequest) (*BarResponse, error)
}
func RegisterFooServer(s grpc.ServiceRegistrar, srv FooServer) {
    s.RegisterService(&Foo_ServiceDesc, srv)
}
var Foo_ServiceDesc = grpc.ServiceDesc{
    ServiceName: "pkg.Foo",
    HandlerType: (*FooServer)(nil),
    Methods: []grpc.MethodDesc{{ MethodName: "Bar", Handler: _Foo_Bar_Handler }},
    Streams: []grpc.StreamDesc{},  // populated in GRPC-4
    Metadata: "...source filename...",
}
func _Foo_Bar_Handler(srv interface{}, ctx context.Context, dec func(interface{}) error, interceptor grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor) (interface{}, error) { /* dec into *BarRequest, call srv.Bar, return */ }
```

Match `protoc-gen-go-grpc`'s shape. The handler thunk is the bridge between gRPC's generic dispatch and the typed server interface.

**Key requirements:**
- Unary methods only in this bead (streaming in GRPC-4).
- ServiceDesc.Metadata = path of the source .proto file (gRPC reflection consumes this).
- Emit `RegisterFooServer` as the public registration API.
- Optional: emit `UnimplementedFooServer` with default-method stubs that return `codes.Unimplemented` (matches `protoc-gen-go-grpc` since v1.32 — useful for forward compatibility).

**Validation:** Integration test in `test/peer/grpc_server_test.go`: register a wiresmith-generated server with `grpc.NewServer`, call via the generated client from GRPC-2, assert round-trip.
close_reason: superseded by wiresmith-dei: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end (4 streaming modes round-trip + reflection ListServices); no native gRPC stub generation needed

title: GRPC-2: Generate gRPC client interface + implementation
close_reason: superseded by wiresmith-dei: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end (4 streaming modes round-trip + reflection ListServices); no native gRPC stub generation needed

title: GRPC-1: Parse service / rpc definitions from descriptors
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-36b (gRPC umbrella).

**What:** Today the generator ignores `service` declarations in `.proto` files. Extend `compiler/generator/` to read `protoreflect.FileDescriptor.Services()` and surface the data needed by GRPC-2/3/4: service name, fully-qualified method paths, input/output types, streaming kind (unary, client-stream, server-stream, bidi).

**Plumbing only — no code emission in this bead.** Produce an internal model (`ServiceModel`, `MethodModel`) the emit phases consume. Wire it into `generateFile` phase ordering (likely after enums/messages so method input/output type names are already resolved).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A `proto/basic/service.proto` fixture with one unary, one client-stream, one server-stream, one bidi RPC.
- Unit test asserts the generator parses all four with correct streaming kinds and input/output type names.
- No `.pb.go` change yet — emission is deferred to GRPC-2/3.

**Out of scope:** code emission (GRPC-2/3), streaming runtime behavior (GRPC-4).
close_reason: superseded by wiresmith-dei: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end (4 streaming modes round-trip + reflection ListServices); no native gRPC stub generation needed

title: JSON-3: Oneof JSON encoding/decoding (variant tag handling)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Extend JSON-1 and JSON-2 to handle `oneof` fields per proto3 JSON spec: serialize only the active variant using its proto field name as the JSON key, deserialize by detecting which variant key is present.

**Why this needs its own bead:** Oneofs are the single trickiest part of proto JSON. wiresmith's oneof representation (interface + per-variant wrapper struct) maps cleanly to JSON but requires special-case emit in both marshal and unmarshal. TEMPO.md:69-75 calls out `AnyValue` (oneof-based) as a primary motivator — `encoding/json` cannot handle wiresmith oneofs natively, which is why Tempo hand-rolls a custom marshaler today.

**Key requirements:**
- Marshal: type-switch on the oneof interface, emit the variant's JSON key + payload.
- Unmarshal: tentatively parse to map[string]json.RawMessage, detect which oneof key is present, dispatch to the variant's decoder. Reject if multiple variants of the same oneof are set.
- `AnyValue` round-trip via wiresmith JSON → Tempo, side-by-side with the hand-rolled implementation, until parity is verified.

**Validation:** Reuse `proto/basic/oneof.proto` fixtures. Add a `test/basic/json_oneof_test.go`. Cross-check against `protojson` (treat its output as canonical).

title: JSON-2: Generate UnmarshalJSON per message
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Emit `UnmarshalJSON([]byte) error` per generated message. Inverse of JSON-1.

**Key requirements:**
- Accept both `lowerCamelCase` (proto3 JSON canonical) and the original proto field name (`protojson` default).
- int64/uint64/fixed64/sfixed64/sint64 accept **JSON strings OR numbers** (spec mandates either is valid input even though we emit strings).
- bytes accept base64 (standard padded; URL-safe is a follow-up).
- Enums accept name (string) or number (int). Unknown enum names → error; unknown numbers → preserve as int.
- `null` for singular fields → leave default (or nil for `optional`).
- Map JSON objects → map values.
- Maintain wiresmith's no-unknown-field-preservation contract: unknown JSON keys are silently ignored (matches `protojson.UnmarshalOptions{DiscardUnknown: true}`).

**Out of scope:** oneofs (see JSON-3), text format (see JSON-4).

**Validation:** Round-trip `MarshalJSON → UnmarshalJSON → Equal` fuzz target in `test/fuzz/`. Differential against `protojson.Unmarshal` for the OTel macros.

title: JSON-1: Generate MarshalJSON per message (proto3 JSON spec)
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Emit a `MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)` method per generated message that produces proto3 JSON spec-compliant output. Handles scalars, nested messages, repeated, maps, and presence (omit unset singular non-optional fields per spec).

**Key requirements:**
- Field names: `lowerCamelCase` JSON keys derived from proto names (matches official `protojson`).
- int64/uint64/fixed64/sfixed64/sint64 encode as **JSON strings** per OTLP JSON spec (TEMPO.md:52 calls this out specifically).
- bytes → base64.
- `optional` fields omit when nil; non-optional singular omit when at default (zero).
- Maps → JSON objects (key stringified per scalar key type).
- Enums emit by name (string), with int fallback for unknown values matching the official runtime's `EmitUnpopulated`/`UseEnumNumbers` knobs (start with name-emit; add knobs later if needed).

**Out of scope:** oneofs (see JSON-3), text format (see JSON-4), WKT JSON encoding (covered by BUF-2.2 `wiresmith-7m6` whenever WKT lands).

**Validation:** Differential test against `google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/protojson` for the OTel macros (Traces, Metrics, Logs) and `proto/basic/*`. Expect bit-identical except where field iteration order is undefined.

title: Add gRPC service stub generation (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "gRPC service stubs" (lines 36-37). BUF.md is silent on services.

**Why:** `AGENTS.md:91` and `docs/design.md:39` both list "services/RPCs" as Not Supported because OTel collector hot paths only need message serialization. Mimir, Tempo, and other Grafana consumers also need wiresmith to generate gRPC server/client code so it can fully replace gogofaster/protoc-gen-go-grpc. Without service stubs, wiresmith remains an unmarshal-only library for consumers with internal gRPC APIs.

**Approach:** Parse `service`/`rpc` definitions via `bufbuild/protocompile` (already used for messages). Emit:
- Client interface + Client impl (`grpc.ClientConnInterface`-backed)
- Server interface + ServiceDesc + handler thunks
- Streaming RPC support (client-stream, server-stream, bidi)

Match `protoc-gen-go-grpc` shape so consumers can swap in wiresmith without touching call sites. Runtime dependency: `google.golang.org/grpc` (standard).

**Design decisions to revisit when this lands:**
- `docs/design.md:39` "No proto2, services/RPCs, extensions..." — services clause must be revised.
- `AGENTS.md:91` "Not supported (not needed for OTel protos): services/RPCs..." — services clause must be revised.

**Decomposition:** see linked children — descriptor parsing, client gen, server gen, streaming. Update docs as part of umbrella close.

**Out of scope:** non-gRPC service exports (Twirp, Connect-Go, etc.). Reflection-service registration (since field-level reflection still panics).
close_reason: superseded by wiresmith-dei: protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse verified end-to-end (4 streaming modes round-trip + reflection ListServices); no native gRPC stub generation needed

title: Add protojson / prototext serialization support (umbrella)
description:
**Source:** TEMPO.md "AnyValue JSON handling" + MIMIR.md "Well-known type helpers" + IMPROVEMENTS.md gap.

**Why:** `docs/design.md` "Limitations" currently states `protojson`/`prototext` are unsupported because they depend on field-level reflection (Range/Get/Set/Mutable), which panics on wiresmith types — value-type message fields are incompatible with `protoimpl`'s field converters. Tempo and Mimir both need JSON serialization for OTLP attribute values (`AnyValue`), jsonpb interop, and OTLP JSON spec compliance (int64/uint64 encoded as JSON strings). The current consumer workaround in TEMPO.md:69-88 is hand-rolled `UnmarshalJSON`/`MarshalJSON` per-type, which does not scale.

**Approach:** Skip the protoreflect.Message route entirely — generate type-specific `MarshalJSON`/`UnmarshalJSON` (and `MarshalText`/`UnmarshalText`) directly, gogo-style. Matches `gogoproto.jsonpb` integration shape but emitted unconditionally per generated message. Avoids the field-level reflection gap.

**Design decisions to revisit when this lands:**
- `docs/design.md:38` "No `protojson`/`prototext`" — must be revised.
- `AGENTS.md:91` "well-known types" caveat is orthogonal; WKT JSON encoding is tracked separately under BUF-2.2 (`wiresmith-7m6`).

**Decomposition:** see linked children — Marshal, Unmarshal, oneof handling, text-proto format. Update docs as part of umbrella close.

**Out of scope:** field-level reflection (`Range`/`Get`/`Set`/`Mutable`) — those panics stay; this umbrella sidesteps them entirely.

title: map<K, Msg> duplicate-key wire entries should REPLACE not MERGE
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go::EmitUnmarshal` (post-loop merge branch, ~line 123).

**What:** When the same map key appears more than once on the wire, wiresmith MERGES the two message values via `existing.unmarshal(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` (or `existing.UnmarshalWithDepth` cross-package). The proto3 spec and the reference `google.golang.org/protobuf` runtime use REPLACE semantics — the second wire entry's value REPLACES the first entirely. See protobuf-go's `internal/impl/codec_map.go::consumeMapOfMessage`: each entry allocates a fresh `val`, unmarshals into it, and then `SetMapIndex` overwrites whatever was there.

**Discovered via:** wiresmith-sb1 re-enabled `corecursive`/`recursive_message` in the conformance proto. Two of the three previously expected recursive failures pass cleanly. The third — `Required.Proto3.ProtobufInput.ValidDataMap.STRING.MESSAGE.MergeValue.ProtobufOutput` — now exposes the merge-vs-replace divergence. Conformance output shows our merged corecursive carries fields from BOTH wire entries; the reference carries only the second.

**Fix:** Drop the merge branch in MapField.EmitUnmarshal for message-valued maps. Allocate a fresh value, decode the entry's bytes into it, then unconditionally `m.X[mapkey] = decoded_value`. Three observations to preserve:
  1. Same-package vs cross-package decode (the depth-threading from wiresmith-1c0) is still required for the value's per-field decode — only the outer merge call goes away.
  2. The wiresmith-1c0 generator tests pin the merge call's shape; they need to be replaced with tests that assert `SetMapIndex`-style replace.
  3. The CLAUDE.md 'Map field correctness → Merge semantics' note about `mapValueBytes != nil` vs `len(mapValueBytes) > 0` is about WITHIN a single map entry (the value field being absent vs empty-but-present) — that part stays correct.

**Validate:** After the fix, `Required.Proto3.ProtobufInput.ValidDataMap.STRING.MESSAGE.MergeValue.ProtobufOutput` should pass and can be removed from `test/conformance/failure_list.txt`. Conformance should go to 700 passing, 2 expected failures.
notes: Fix already shipped in a prior commit. compiler/types/map.go::EmitUnmarshal line 118 now emits unconditional 'access[mapkey] = mapvalue' (REPLACE semantics) — the inline comment cites wiresmith-05d. test/conformance/failure_list.txt no longer lists Required.Proto3.ProtobufInput.ValidDataMap.STRING.MESSAGE.MergeValue.ProtobufOutput. Bead was open as a tracking placeholder; closing for bookkeeping.
close_reason: fix already shipped, bead tracking placeholder

title: SEC-5 regression: cross-package map merge calls public Unmarshal (depth-reset)
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go:127`.

**What:** When a map\<K, Msg\> field has a value message in a different package, duplicate-key merge emits `existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes)` — the public entry point that resets the unmarshal recursion-depth counter to 0. This re-opens the SEC-5 hole that `UnmarshalWithDepth` closes everywhere else for cross-package message access:

- Same-package map merge: `existing.unmarshal(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` ✓
- Cross-package map merge: `existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes)` ✗ (depth-reset)
- Cross-package initial-value-decode (`MessageType.EmitMapEntryUnmarshal`, message.go:88): `varName.UnmarshalWithDepth(dAtA[iNdEx:postIndex], depth+1)` ✓ — the fix that 4a547a4 / SEC-5 was specifically about, but the merge path was not updated.

**Discovered via:** TEST-2 (wiresmith-thc) coverage. `TestMapField_EmitUnmarshal_CrossPackageMessageMerge` deliberately does NOT pin the exact public-call symbol (`Unmarshal` vs `UnmarshalWithDepth`) — that decision was deferred to this bead so the unit test doesn't guard the bug. It does assert the forward-compatible invariants (nil-based merge predicate, private `existing.unmarshal()` not reachable cross-package).

**Fix:** Emit `existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1)` for the cross-package merge branch. Add a generator-level integration test analogous to TestGenerateMapEntryThreadsDepth (generator_test.go:~1062) targeting the duplicate-key merge wire-form, not the initial decode.

**Validate:** add a regression test that crafts a wire input with N+1 duplicate map keys, each carrying a deeply-recursive payload, and asserts that the total decoded depth equals the cumulative depth (i.e. depth tracking survives the merge boundary).
notes:
Fix: changed compiler/types/map.go:127 from existing.Unmarshal(mapValueBytes) to existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1) so cross-package duplicate-key merge threads the recursion-depth counter, matching the rest of the codegen.

Tests added/updated:
- compiler/generator/generator_test.go: TestGenerateCrossPackageMapDuplicateKeyMergeThreadsDepth (new). Pins the codegen string match exactly the way TestGenerateCrossPackageMapValueThreadsDepth pins the *initial* decode site — the merge branch is now under string-match coverage too. Verified the test fails on pre-fix code.
- compiler/types/map_test.go: TestMapField_EmitUnmarshal_CrossPackageMessageMerge updated to drop the previously deliberate 'don't pin the call symbol' carve-out and assert UnmarshalWithDepth(...,depth+1) explicitly.

New proto: proto/basic/crossmap.proto defines basic.crossmap.v1.CrossMapOuter with a map<string, basic.recursive.v1.LinkedList> field, giving us a generator-test fixture without needing t.TempDir scaffolding.

Runtime test the bead recommended was investigated and dropped. The setup the bead suggests (N+1 duplicate keys with deeply-recursive payloads) cannot differentiate pre/post-fix at runtime: MessageType.EmitMapEntryUnmarshal already routes the *initial* per-entry value decode through depth+1, so any payload that would trip the post-fix merge also trips the initial decode of that same entry, before the merge runs. The pre-fix merge starts at depth=0 (strictly under the budget), so the only way to expose the exploit at the wire level is via a cross-package CYCLE (Outer -> map<K, Leaf>; Leaf -> Outer) so depth accumulates across the package boundary before reaching the merge — that's the SEC-5 attack model. The current proto suite doesn't have such a cycle and adding one purely for this test would be scope creep. The generator-level + unit-level string-match coverage is the practical guard; matches how the original SEC-5 fix is locked in (no runtime test there either).
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/94
close_reason: implemented: cross-package map merge now threads depth via existing.UnmarshalWithDepth(mapValueBytes, depth+1)

title: Use --out to compose the Go import-path base (drop /gen/ hardcode)
description:
**Source:** Highlighted by review on PR #79 (DOC-2). The README now invites users to set --module to their own module path, but the import-path base in compiler/generator/names.go:destForPath is hardcoded as `joinImport(module, "gen")`. Cross-file imports emitted in generated code therefore always reference `<module>/gen/<source-rel>`, regardless of --out. A user picking --out=pkg/api gets files on disk at pkg/api/foo/v1/foo.pb.go with imports pointing at <module>/gen/foo/v1 — Go's directory-equals-import-path rule rejects this at build time.

**Fix:** Replace the hardcode with `joinImport(module, outDir)` so --out and the import base agree. Tests using `OutDir: t.TempDir()` (absolute path) need adjustment — the formula doesn't work when outDir is absolute, since you'd get `module/<TMPDIR>` as an import path. Either chdir to tmp and use a relative --out, or refactor tests to scope --out as relative-under-tmp.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- destForPath uses outDir (relative) for the import base
- --out=gen continues to behave identically (wiresmith's own gen tree byte-identical after regen)
- A new test pins that --out=pkg/api with a matching go.mod produces buildable code (importPath equals on-disk-rel under module root)
- README example doesn't need a footnote saying 'only --out=gen works'

**Why:** This is the last hardcoded assumption from the pre-source-relative era. The full FLAGS.md migration (wiresmith-j0h) requires this so external consumers can pick their own --out without breakage.
notes:
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/80
Review follow-up: normalize bare '.' / './.' to empty (module root) in validateOutDir; previously composed to 'module/./<pkg>'. Test added.
close_reason: implemented: destForPath now uses module + outDir as the import base; --out=gen behavior unchanged (gen/ tree byte-identical post-regen); added test pinning that --out=pkg/api produces buildable cross-file imports; integration tests refactored to use chdir + relative outDir.

title: PERF: Investigate unrolled varint decoder (protobuf-go style)
description:
**Motivation:** The 10-byte overflow check added in wiresmith-j0d costs ~1% geomean / +7.7% on map-heavy unmarshal because it sits inside the per-byte loop. gogoproto and vtprotobuf skip the check entirely (and are vulnerable to silent overflow). protobuf-go (`encoding/protowire/wire.go::ConsumeVarint`) avoids the cost by fully unrolling the varint decode into ten flat branches — the 10th-byte `if y < 2` check exists in exactly one branch the CPU only enters on rare 10-byte values, and the 1-byte fast path is a single conditional return.

**Current shape** (`compiler/types/type.go::emitConsumeVarintAt` / `emitConsumeBytesLenAt` + `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go::emitSkipValueHelper` case 2): generic for-loop with per-iteration guards. The post-fix break-path move (PR #70) brings the overflow check down to once per varint; this ticket is the bigger structural change.

**Experiment:** Generate an unrolled decoder per call site — ten inline branches, each returning early when `b < 0x80`. Compare against the current loop on the bench harness with the same `Unmarshal*_Ours` filter and benchstat count=20. Hypothesis: large speedup on tag/length-heavy benchmarks (Maps, Traces) at the cost of generated code size.

**Acceptance:**
- Benchstat shows no statistically significant regression vs current baseline; ideally Maps recovers the +7.7% (or better) and other Unmarshal_Ours benches stay within noise.
- Generated code size delta documented in PR.
- Conformance still 696/5/0.
- Decision recorded: adopt, defer, or reject (with measurement).
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/95
close_reason: PR #95 merged. Resyncing bd state on sb1 branch after divergence noted in PR #96 review.

title: Address PR review comments for protobuf-registry
description: Inspect the current branch PR status and unresolved review comments, implement valid review fixes, resolve addressed threads, and push the branch.
close_reason: PR review comments addressed, pushed, and resolved

title: BUF-2.2: Support google.protobuf.Any (migration blocker for Loki + Mimir)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md Layer 2; Loki ([[wiresmith-bg7]]) + Mimir ([[wiresmith-cqa]]) migrations.

**Narrowed 2026-06-18:** a WKT-usage audit of the three consumer proto trees shows the only remaining well-known-type blocker is `google.protobuf.Any`.

WKT coverage / consumer usage (non-vendor `.proto`, import counts, 2026-06-18):

| WKT | wiresmith support | tempo | loki | mimir |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Timestamp | ✅ `stdtime` ([[wiresmith-gqx]]) | 1 | 14 | 2 |
| Duration | ✅ `stdduration` ([[wiresmith-b34]]) | – | 5 | 7 |
| **Any** | ❌ **this bead** | – | 2 | 4 |
| Empty / Struct / FieldMask | ❌ deferred → [[wiresmith-g25s]] | – | – | – |

`Any` is used as real message fields, not just service/error details:
- Mimir: `pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto:63` (`payload`), `pkg/frontend/querymiddleware/model.proto:81` (`response`), `pkg/storegateway/storepb/rpc.proto` (`hints` ×6).
- Loki: `pkg/storage/chunk/cache/resultscache/types.proto:36` (`response`).

Empty/Struct/FieldMask are imported by **none** of tempo/loki/mimir → split to [[wiresmith-g25s]] (P4, BUF.md general goal), off the migration critical path.

### Design note
`google.protobuf.Any` is structurally a plain proto3 message (`string type_url = 1; bytes value = 2;`) — unlike Timestamp/Duration it needs no value-type substitution. Support likely reduces to resolving/embedding the WKT descriptor and generating an ordinary wiresmith struct (+ Marshal/Unmarshal/Size/Equal/Compare/getter) under the usual pointer/no_presence shape rules. Confirm whether consumers need the typed `Any` helpers (`MarshalFrom`/`UnmarshalNew`/`TypeName`) or only the raw `type_url`/`value` struct.

### Acceptance
- A proto importing `google/protobuf/any.proto` and using `google.protobuf.Any` as a singular and a repeated field compiles via wiresmith.
- Roundtrip + Equal + Compare + Size pass for Any fields; getter shape follows the message-field rules.
- The Mimir (×7) and Loki (×1) Any field sites above generate and build.
notes: Mimir blocker context (per [[wiresmith-cqa]]): only google.protobuf.Any matters for Mimir migration. 4 .proto files use it — pkg/storegateway/storepb/rpc.proto (5 sites, all on Deprecated fields and could be reserved-out), pkg/ruler/rulespb/rules.proto (Prometheus Manager options, cold path), pkg/frontend/querymiddleware/model.proto (cached query response payload), pkg/scheduler/schedulerpb/scheduler.proto (scheduler protocol payload). Timestamp/Duration are already covered by stdtime/stdduration. If splitting this bead, Any-only would unblock Mimir alone.
close_reason: Closed

title: DOC-10: README benchmark numbers lack date/commit anchor
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `README.md:17`; `benchstat.txt` dated 2026-04-16.

**What:** "+19% vs vtproto on sec/op (geomean)" is a load-bearing claim. There's no date stamp or commit reference. Reader has no way to know if numbers reflect current code.

**Fix:** Add "as of <date>, commit <SHA>" header to the benchmark section. Optionally CI job to regenerate `benchstat.txt`.

title: TEST-10: Re-enable corecursive fields to remove 3 expected conformance failures
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `proto/conformance/test_messages_proto3.proto:28,63` (deliberately removed `corecursive` and `recursive_message`); `test/conformance/failure_list.txt`.

**What:** The 3 recursive-merge entries exist because the conformance proto deliberately omits self-referencing fields. But wiresmith already supports recursive messages via `optional` (see `proto/basic/recursive.proto`), and the merge path in `emit_unmarshal.go:300-309` exists. Re-introducing the fields with `optional` should fix all three failures.

**Fix:** Re-add `optional NestedMessage corecursive = 2;` to `NestedMessage` and `optional TestAllTypesProto3 recursive_message = 27;` to `TestAllTypesProto3`. Run conformance, prune `failure_list.txt`. The 2 unknown-field-preservation failures are by-design.
close_reason: PR #96 (this PR). Resyncing the snapshot to match the interactions log.

title: DOC-9: CLI error for missing proto_path leaks syscall name
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `cmd/wiresmith/main.go:51`; resulting error: `building import mapping: lstat /nonexistent: no such file or directory`.

**What:** "lstat" is a filesystem syscall — leaks implementation detail to end users.

**Fix:** Detect `errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist)` in `Generate` and return `fmt.Errorf("proto_path %q: directory does not exist", g.ProtoDir)`. Same treatment for `out` creation failures.
notes:
Fix: in compileSources, errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist) gates a clean '--proto_path "%s": directory does not exist' error before the fmt.Errorf wrap that would otherwise leak the lstat syscall. Out-creation failures (mentioned in the bead) happen lazily during file emit at line 467 (os.MkdirAll) and are less commonly triggered; left for a follow-up if a real user report surfaces.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/120
close_reason: implemented

title: TEST-9: Add pdata/cross-library differential test for profiles proto
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** missing — `test/differential/pdata_profiles_test.go`.

**What:** Traces/Metrics/Logs each have differential coverage (`pdata_roundtrip_test.go`). Profiles has only `test/basic/roundtrip_test.go:TestProfilesDataRoundTrip` plus field-survival. `FuzzCrossLibrary` excludes ProfilesData.

**Fix:** Mirror `TestPdataTracesRoundTrip` for Profiles, against `otlpprofiles` from `go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp`. If pdata lacks profile support, use only the official library for comparison.

title: DOC-8: AGENTS.md Generated API surface implicit/incomplete
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `AGENTS.md` "Design decisions".

**What:** Mentions `Has`, `Get`, `Reset`, `ProtoMessage`, `String` but not `Equal`, `Marshal`, `MarshalTo`, `MarshalToSizedBuffer`, `Unmarshal`, `Size`. The full surface is only knowable by reading the generated `.pb.go`.

**Fix:** Dedicated "Generated API surface" subsection listing every method with a one-line semantics + nil-safety statement.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: TEST-8: cmd/wiresmith unit + CLI integration tests
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**What:** cmd/wiresmith (~112 lines: flag parsing, --version/--help, buildVersion() fallback to debug.ReadBuildInfo, --proto_path/--out/-M wiring) has no tests — the most likely silent-regression surface. Consolidated scope of wiresmith-1fh (unit) + wiresmith-4hy (integration; superseded by this issue).

**Fix:**
- Unit: table-driven TestBuildVersion.
- Integration, in test/cli/: build cmd/wiresmith once per test binary (pattern in test/plugin/integration_test.go:42), then drive the binary in temp dirs. Happy paths: -M mapping, --module, multi --proto_path, positional proto files, --version exit 0 + version-string format, --help snapshot. Error paths: missing import, file outside the proto path, bad flag, output collision.
notes: 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): consolidated with wiresmith-4hy (superseded — same surface, P4). Earlier note about the stale 66-line anchor is resolved by the rewritten description.

title: DOC-7: MIMIR.md references nonexistent GogoCompat flag
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `MIMIR.md:13`.

**What:** "These... require GogoCompat flag" — no such flag exists. Most listed items (`Reset`, `ProtoMessage`, `String`, `Get*`, `Register*`, `Equal`, struct tags) ship unconditionally today.

**Fix:** Either delete `MIMIR.md` (most of it is now obsolete) or rewrite to say "Currently emitted unconditionally; previously contemplated as gated".
close_reason: resolved by deleting TEMPO.md/MIMIR.md — content was either stale (flags that don't exist; GogoCompat flag that doesn't exist) or captured in beads (gogo features: 06z/cr0/b34/gqx/nra/4je/1dq; flag elimination: j0h; multi-proto_path: zxd; JSON: 4kx; gRPC: 36b)

title: DOC-6: Generated files emit no generator version
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:313-314` (`emitHeader`).

**What:** Header is just `// Code generated by wiresmith. DO NOT EDIT.` + `// source: <path>`. No generator version. When a bug report arrives ("wiresmith generated wrong code"), there's no way to tell *which* wiresmith. `protoc-gen-go` emits a `// versions:` block for this exact reason.

**Fix:** Thread `buildVersion()` from `cmd/wiresmith` into the generator and emit a `// wiresmith: v0.0.0-...` line between source and package clause.

title: TEST-7: Add missing edge-case proto fixtures
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** suggested new `proto/basic/edge_cases.proto`.

**What:**
  - Single-variant oneof: no proto in fixtures exercises `oneof X { string only = 1; }`. Exercises a different branch in `isRealOneof` / `emit_oneof.go`.
  - Messages with only optional fields: presence bitmap should be size 0; not tested.
  - Same message used both singular and repeated in one file: `Foo singular = 1; repeated Foo many = 2; map<string,Foo> by_key = 3;` — not tested.
  - Cross-package import with same-name types (e.g. `pkgA.Foo` + `pkgB.Foo`) — not tested.

**Fix:** Add the proto, plus a single roundtrip test in `test/basic/edge_cases_test.go` confirming Marshal → Unmarshal cleanly.
notes:
Scope extended by 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): add to the fixture list:
- max field number 536870911 (tag-encoding boundary)
- >64 message-typed fields — exercises the multi-word presence bitmap end-to-end (existing WideFields has 82 fields but they are strings, which don't hit the bitmap path the same way)
- 10+-level nesting fixture
- cross-package recursion: proto/basic/xrecur_a.proto + xrecur_b.proto with A -> B -> A, plus a depth-limit test across the package boundary
Register all new types in testutil so the nil-receiver sweep (wiresmith-66m) and Populate property test (wiresmith-jgg) cover them for free; make generate-ours + the CI freshness check gives compile coverage automatically.

title: ARCH-6: De-mangle protohelpers import path so consumers don't see broken refs
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_registration.go:14,28`; `compiler/types/varint.go:47`; ~19 emit sites.

**What:** Every call site emits `fg.module+"/gen/protohelpers"` as the import. Consumers running `wiresmith --module=tempo --out=tempopb` get `tempo/gen/protohelpers` imports pointing at a non-existent directory. The "stable runtime helpers at a fixed path" model that `FLAGS.md` proposes is currently unimplementable.

**Fix:** Move helpers to a standalone module (`github.com/wiresmith/protohelpers` or under the wiresmith module root but at a fixed `wiresmith/protohelpers` path). Replace all `fg.module+"/gen/protohelpers"` with a single constant. Link the resolved design from `FLAGS.md` once committed.

title: DOC-5: BUF.md and FLAGS.md are untracked
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** repo root (git status `??`).

**What:** Both are substantive (4.7 KB and 8.6 KB respectively). Off-tree means they don't show in `git log`, can't be referenced from PR descriptions, and the "Keep documentation in sync" rule cannot enforce them.

**Fix:** Commit both with clear status headers: BUF.md → "Future work / not started"; FLAGS.md → "Accepted; TEMPO.md superseded" (or "Proposal; open"). Add references in `AGENTS.md` "Design proposals" subsection.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: TEST-6: Detect entirely-deleted package directories in regen smoke test
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator_test.go:238-261`.

**What:** The reverse check iterates `genDirs` and flags `.pb.go` files in those dirs that weren't produced. If the generator drops an entire directory (e.g. `gen/basic/oneof/v1`), it never appears in `genDirs` and the test passes silently.

**Fix:** After the loop, walk each proto-package root (`gen/otlp`, `gen/basic`, `gen/test`) and assert every `.pb.go` belongs to *some* `genDirs` entry. Equivalent: take the union of all `.pb.go` paths under each root and assert it's a subset of `generatedFiles`.

title: ARCH-5: Merge PointerField/OptionalField and RepeatedPointer/RepeatedField
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/optional.go:7`, `pointer.go:12`, `repeated_pointer.go:24`.

**What:** `OptionalField` handles `proto3 optional` (struct field is `*T`). `PointerField` handles `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` on messages only. The split exists because `OptionalField` calls into `MessageType.CastExpr` which panics. Once ARCH-2 lands (Message stops panicking), `OptionalField` can subsume `PointerField` and `RepeatedField` can subsume `RepeatedPointer`. Two narrow composites disappear.

**Fix:** Sequenced after ARCH-2. Merge composites; update `compiler/types/AGENTS.md` enumeration. `compiler/generator/option_pointer.go:172-187` "field-shape selector" also collapses.

title: SEC-7: collectGoPackages ..-segment guard misses unparented paths
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:222-240`; `compiler/generator/names.go:141-155`.

**What:** The `..`-segment check only fires when the parsed `go_package` import path is *inside* `<module>/gen`. A `go_package = "external/path/with/../etc"` falls through the in-base early-out at line 231-233 and the segment check is never run. The fallback path uses the validated proto-package name so this is not currently exploitable, but the guard is misleading — a future change could trust the unvalidated string.

**Fix:** Move the `..` segment check before the in-base early-out so every `go_package` value is validated regardless of where it points. One extra string scan per file; negligible.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: PERF-6: Avoid heap allocation in optional scalar unmarshal
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/optional.go:42-47`; observable in `gen/otlp/metrics/v1/metrics.pb.go` (HistogramDataPoint has 3 optional floats — 3 allocs × N points).

**What:** `tmp := math.Float64frombits(v); m.Sum = &tmp` forces `tmp` to escape. For `Histogram-50` that's ~150 allocs alone.

**Fix:** Two options. (a) Add a private `sumStore` field; on unmarshal set `m.sumStore = ...; m.Sum = &m.sumStore`. Costs `sizeof(T)` per optional field even when absent. (b) Use `sync.Pool` for the optional-value sidecar. Option (a) is simpler; option (b) is friendlier to long-lived pdata trees.

**Validate:** `UnmarshalHistogram` allocs/op should drop ~30%.

title: TEST-5: Expand fuzz corpus beyond OTel-only seeds
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `test/fuzz/fuzz_test.go:marshaledSeeds` and `test/fuzz/testdata/fuzz/*`.

**What:** `FuzzRoundTrip`/`FuzzMarshalSize`/`FuzzCrossLibrary`/`FuzzDifferentialBytewise` share **3 seeds** (Traces/Metrics/Logs). `proto/basic/*` (oneof, recursive, kitchen sink, maps) seeds are absent. `FuzzMarshalSize` and `FuzzDifferentialBytewise` have **zero** persisted corpus files.

**Fix:** Extend `marshaledSeeds()` with kitchen_sink, oneof, recursive, and the new edge-case fixtures (from TEST-7 below).
notes: 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): now depends on wiresmith-jgg (testutil.Populate) for breadth.
