Seed design: kitchen_sink/basic fixtures via Populate + hand-built adversarial byte seeds checked into test/fuzz/testdata/ — truncated length prefixes, max-tag varints (field 536870911), depth-limit shells (nested length-delimited at/over the recursion cap), full 10-byte varints with the 10th-byte overflow boundary.

title: CR-8: mapValueStart reference in map.go is fragile
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go:106` reads `mapValueStart` declared in `compiler/types/message.go:81`.

**What:** `mapValueStart` is defined inside `MessageType.EmitMapEntryUnmarshal` but read in `map.go`'s merge path. Today, only `MessageType` triggers the path, so compile-time it works. A future contributor adding a different message-shaped map value will hit a confusing "undeclared `mapValueStart`" compile error in *generated* code.

**Fix:** Hoist `mapValueStart` declaration into `MapField.EmitUnmarshal` so the symbol's scope is local to one file. Same generated bytes, more local reasoning.
notes: Closed as obsolete on 2026-05-29 by PR #99 (wiresmith-05d). The mapValueStart symbol and the entire merge-path machinery it backed are gone from compiler/types/map.go and compiler/types/message.go — there's no longer a cross-file symbol dependency to hoist. IMPROVEMENTS.md CR-8 entry is struck through with the same rationale.
close_reason: obsoleted by wiresmith-05d / PR #99: mapValueStart symbol is gone with the merge branch; nothing to hoist.

title: ARCH-4: Collapse Sint32/Sint64 into a shared sintBase
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/sint32.go`, `sint64.go`.

**What:** `varintBase` already parameterises on `unmarshalCast`. `int32`/`int64`/`uint32`/`uint64` are 7-line files. `sint32`/`sint64` could share a `sintBase` parameterised on `(width, shift, cast)` and shrink ~140 lines total.

**Fix:** Introduce `sintBase` matching the `varintBase` pattern. The "standalone due to zigzag encoding" justification in `compiler/types/AGENTS.md` is overstated — zigzag differs by shift amount only.

title: SEC-6: Bytes-map merge path retains sub-slice of caller's buffer
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go:106, 123-128`.

**What:** For message-valued maps with duplicate keys, the merge path stashes `mapValueBytes := dAtA[mapValueStart:iNdEx]` (a sub-slice of the input buffer) and feeds it back into `existing.unmarshal(mapValueBytes, depth+1)`. The merge is synchronous, so today this is safe — but the contract is implicit. Any future change introducing lazy decode or background processing would turn this into a use-after-recycle for callers who reuse buffers (sync.Pool patterns are common in OTel collector).

**Fix:** Either copy: `mapValueBytes := append([]byte(nil), dAtA[mapValueStart:iNdEx]...)` (one extra alloc per duplicate-key event, which is rare in well-formed wire), or add a code comment at `map.go:106` documenting the "must be consumed synchronously before `Unmarshal` returns" invariant.
notes: Closed as obsolete on 2026-05-29 by PR #99 (wiresmith-05d). The merge path that stashed mapValueBytes := dAtA[mapValueStart:iNdEx] no longer exists — cross-entry duplicate-key handling is REPLACE / last-write-wins now (per proto3 spec, matching protobuf-go). No sub-slice survives past the entry's iNdEx advance. IMPROVEMENTS.md SEC-6 entry is struck through with the same rationale.
close_reason: obsoleted by wiresmith-05d / PR #99: merge branch removed; no caller-buffer sub-slice survives entry decode.

title: PERF-5: Hoist wire-type-mismatch check into per-package helper
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:333-342` (`emitWireTypeCheck`); ~71 sites per `metrics.pb.go`.

**What:** Each case starts with a 7-line `if wireType != X { skipValue(...); continue }` block. `HistogramDataPoint.unmarshal` compiles to ~7.3 KB of machine code. icache density suffers; recursive walks evict the parent's hot code between nested calls.

**Fix:** Emit a per-package helper `skipMismatchedWire(dAtA, iNdEx, wireType, fieldNum) (newIdx int, err error)` and shrink each case to a 2-line call. The wire-mismatch path is cold; function-call overhead is negligible. Expected: ~30% shrink in unmarshal function size.

**Validate:** `go tool objdump` to confirm shrinkage; incidental UnmarshalTraces wins.

title: TEST-4: Add Marshal/Unmarshal/Equal consistency fuzz target
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** missing — should be `test/fuzz/marshal_equal_roundtrip_fuzz_test.go`.

**What:** Existing fuzz targets compare *bytes* across the wire boundary. A bug where `Equal()` returns false for two messages that round-trip through wire identically would not be caught.

**Fix:** Add `FuzzMarshalEqualRoundTrip`: unmarshal input → marshal to bytes → unmarshal again into `msg2` → assert `msg1.Equal(msg2) == true`. This exercises Equal and wire logic against each other.
notes:
Scope extended by 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): add two consistency legs beyond the original Equal roundtrip property:
1. Compare()==0 <=> Equal() — fuzz that the two methods agree on every roundtripped pair. This is the only real remaining Compare gap: test/basic/compare_test.go is already thorough (360 lines incl. nil-receiver); the initial audit claim of 'thin Compare coverage' was wrong.
2. Byte-equal re-marshal leg: marshal(unmarshal(bytes)) == marshal(unmarshal(marshal(unmarshal(bytes)))) — restrict to map-free constructors (map iteration order makes byte equality non-deterministic).

title: CR-7: Optional fields lack Has*() methods
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_has.go:30` (`fieldsForPresence` excludes optional/oneof/repeated/map).

**What:** `optional int32 foo` users must write `m.Foo != nil` while `int32 bar` users write `m.HasBar()`. CLAUDE.md says "Optional fields dereference the pointer" for `Get*`, implying Has should exist. Official protobuf-go emits Has for optional fields.

**Fix:** Extend `emitHasMethods` to also walk optional fields, emitting `Has<F>() bool { return m != nil && m.F != nil }`. No bitmap involvement; pure nil-check. Update CLAUDE.md description accordingly.
notes:
Fix: emit_has.go now walks optional fields after the bitmap loop and emits Has<F>() bool { return m != nil && m.F != nil }. Updated wiresmith-1c0 generator test (TestEmitHas_NoPresenceFields → TestEmitHas_NoBitmapFields) to pin the new contract: optionals get a nil-check Has accessor, repeated/map/oneof variants still don't. Also closes wiresmith-05d (already-shipped map REPLACE fix) as a bookkeeping closure in this PR's bd export.
PR opened: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/121
close_reason: implemented

title: ARCH-3: Lift private encoder interface onto Type for packed marshal
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/repeated.go:10-12, 98, 109`.

**What:** A private `encoder` interface is defined in `repeated.go`, then `r.Inner.(encoder).EmitEncode(...)` does an unchecked assertion in the packed-marshal emit. Adding a packable type that forgets `EmitEncode` compiles fine but panics at codegen time. Also: 7 files (`bool.go`, `bytes.go`, `fixed32.go`, `fixed64.go`, `sint32.go`, `sint64.go`, `string.go`, `varint.go`) duplicate the same 4-line `EmitValueMarshal { EmitEncode; ReverseTag }` boilerplate.

**Fix:** Lift `EmitEncode(Emitter, indent, access)` onto `Type` (or a `PackedEncoder` sub-interface implied by `IsPackable()`). `repeated.go` calls it statically. Pull the 4-line `EmitValueMarshal` into a free helper or default method to dedupe.

title: PERF-4: Add 1-byte fast-path for known-small length prefixes
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `gen/protohelpers/protohelpers.go:9-19`; called per nested-message length prefix.

**What:** Length prefixes for small nested messages are frequently < 128 bytes (1-byte varint). The current path still enters the `for v >= 1<<7` loop. Inlining helps but doesn't kill the branch.

**Fix:** Emit a specialized 1-byte fast-path at the call site for length prefixes: `if size <= 0x7F { i--; dAtA[i] = byte(size) } else { i = protohelpers.EncodeVarint(dAtA, i, uint64(size)) }`. Same pattern works for tag writes that are already single-byte literals — those are fine.

**Validate:** `MarshalSingleSpan` / `MarshalTraces` ns/op, 5-10% expected.

title: ARCH-2: Split Type interface to eliminate panic-only methods
description:
**Severity:** Medium

**Location:** `compiler/types/type.go:27-51`; `fixed32.go:36-38` (`VarintSizeExpr` panics); `fixed64.go:36-38` (same); `message.go:26-29,68-70` (`CastExpr` panics).

**What:** Classic interface-segregation violation. Call sites in `repeated.go` already guard via type checks (`switch r.Inner.FixedSize()`) so the panics are unreachable today — but the methods exist purely to satisfy `Type`, creating compile-time obligations that can never run.

**Fix:** Split `Type` into smaller interfaces:
  - `VarintSized` (subset implemented by `varintBase`/`Bool`/`Enum`/sint variants) carries `VarintSizeExpr`.
  - `ScalarDecoder` (implemented by everything except `MessageType`) carries `CastExpr`.
  - `PackedEncoder` (`EmitEncode`) — see ARCH-3.
  Then `repeated.go` and `emit_unmarshal.go` type-assert via the narrow interface rather than guarding panics.

title: enum_no_prefix: codegen-time validation for package-scope name collisions
description:
**Salvaged from wiresmith-1dq** (closed — enum prefixing itself shipped as `enum_no_prefix` / `enum_no_prefix_all`, gogo `goproto_enum_prefix=false` parity).

`enum_no_prefix` drops the `EnumName_` prefix from value constants. When two enums in the same Go package each declare a value with the same bare name (e.g. both have `RED` with prefix-off), the emitted constants collide and the package fails to compile. Today this is caught only by `go build` — documented as the author's responsibility (CLAUDE.md / docs/extensions.md).

Add a codegen-time validation pass that detects package-scope constant-name collisions among prefix-off enums and emits a clear error naming the colliding enums + value, instead of deferring to a downstream `go build` failure.

### Acceptance
- Two prefix-off enums in one package sharing a value name → wiresmith errors at codegen, naming both enums + the value.
- No false positives when prefixes differ or enums live in different packages.
- Table-driven test covering accept/reject.

title: presence option: docs (CLAUDE.md, extensions.md, design.md)
description: Document the presence option: the three values, scope precedence, validation table, deprecations, on-wire neutrality, and the unsafe-cast (FLAT/nullable=false) use case. Replace the pointer / no_presence / no_presence_all entries in CLAUDE.md and docs/extensions.md; add a design.md note.
acceptance_criteria: Docs updated; deprecation + re-annotation migration note included; no stale references to the removed options remain.
close_reason: Superseded: tri-state presence enum abandoned. 2026-06-18 audit showed zero consumer reliance on value getters and no BITMAP usage, so there is no presence/storage/getter axis to unify. Substantive change folded into wiresmith-der5 (uniform pointer-shape getters); consumer regen salvaged into wiresmith-h2ka.

title: presence option: interaction-matrix + golden tests
description: Fixtures exercising all three presence values at field/message/file scope across field kinds (message singular+repeated, scalar, proto3 optional, oneof, map). Assert round-trip, Equal/Compare, Has, and no XXX_fieldsPresent JSON leak under FLAT/POINTER. Extends the option-interaction matrix (cf wiresmith-xt5).
acceptance_criteria: Matrix covers 3 values x scopes x field kinds; golden files checked in; JSON-leak assertion present for FLAT/POINTER.
close_reason: Superseded: tri-state presence enum abandoned. 2026-06-18 audit showed zero consumer reliance on value getters and no BITMAP usage, so there is no presence/storage/getter axis to unify. Substantive change folded into wiresmith-der5 (uniform pointer-shape getters); consumer regen salvaged into wiresmith-h2ka.

title: DB-17: emit GoString() (gogoslick importers call it on wiresmith messages)
description: Mimir phase-2 blocker #5: each migrated proto with still-gogoslick importers needs a one-line GoString shim (gogoslick generates GoString() calls on imported message types). Consider emitting a GoString() into the _reflect or _compare companion (cold file) matching gogo's format closely enough for compilation (callers only need the method to exist). Collect exact gogo output shape first.

title: DB-15: minor gaps — no pointer option for map values; no automatic name-collision mangling (Size_)
description: Tempo blocker #7: (a) map<k, Msg> values are always value-typed; gogo emitted map[k]*Msg — tempo adapted call sites, an option could ease future migrations; (b) a field named size collides with generated Size() — gogo auto-mangles to Size_, wiresmith required explicit (wiresmith.options.customname) = "Size_". Auto-mangling matching protoc-gen-go/gogo rules would remove a migration footgun.

title: DB-14: smoother cross-runtime imports (gogo-annotated protos unparseable; leftover unused import)
description: Tempo blocker #6 + loki W2 overlap: referencing gogo-generated messages requires customtype envelope types + maintaining a gogo-stripped copy of the imported .proto (wiresmith/protocompile can't resolve gogoproto/gogo.proto options) + goimports cleanup for one leftover unused import. Ideas: ship a stub gogoproto/gogo.proto in the embedded resolver so gogo-annotated protos parse (options ignored); fix the unused-import emission for -M'd imports (similar to wiresmith-uol). Consolidate with wiresmith-56c (DB-2) when designing.

title: DB-8: customtype unmarshal context hook (parent-message dispatch)
description: Mimir blocker #4. Mimir's WriteRequest needs per-parent dispatch during unmarshal (RW2 symbol-table dispatch, exemplar skipping, pool-aware prealloc) — gogo also required hand-patching, parity today, but a hook (e.g. customtype Unmarshal receives parent context, or a generated-code extension point) would shrink Mimir's expdiff to just struct fields. Needs design; collect requirements from tempo/loki too.

title: DB-4: presence-bitmap semantics surprise consumers (Get<MsgField>/deep-equal observe fieldsPresent)
description: Found by Loki migration (W4). Get<MsgField>() consults fieldsPresent, which only Unmarshal/setters set; hand-constructed structs report Has=false and Get returns zero value; reflect.DeepEqual/cmp.Diff disagree on structurally-equal values with different bitmaps. Loki hit this in a frontend test. Action: document loudly in README/docs/design.md + migration guide (use generated Equal, construct via setters or set bitmap); consider emitting cmpopts helper or making Get<MsgField> return the field unconditionally when bitmap unset but field non-zero. Needs design discussion.

title: Parallel benchmarks (Traces only)
description:
All benchmarks are single-goroutine; real collectors marshal/unmarshal concurrently, and parallelism is where sync.Pool honestly pays off — the pooled vtproto variant (B1) deserves to be measured in its best light.

Design: bench/parallel_bench_test.go using b.RunParallel — Marshal+Unmarshal Traces x 4 libraries, plus _VTProtoPool and _OursReuse parallel variants (per-goroutine reuse). One shape only: a full parallel matrix is noise multiplication, not information.
acceptance_criteria: Parallel Marshal/Unmarshal Traces across 4 libs + pool/reuse variants; per-goroutine state correctly isolated (no shared message reuse across goroutines).
notes: Audit item B7 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e. Blocked on B1 (pool variant).

title: Adversarial/skewed payload benchmarks
description:
All current bench inputs are canonical mid-size OTel payloads. Competitors' best profiles (zero-copy strings) and wiresmith's stress points (slice growth, recursion) are unmeasured — better to measure before critics do.

Design: builders in the bench inputs + bench/skewed_bench_test.go, Marshal+Unmarshal x 4 libraries:
- TracesLarge: 2000 spans, ~2 MB — stresses pre-scan and slice-growth behavior.
- LogsStringHeavy: ~1 KB string bodies — the profile where unsafe/zero-copy competitors look best.
- DeepKVList: ~25-deep nested KVList — recursion overhead near the depth limit.

Standard benchmark naming so these ARE included in bench-compare intentionally — they are legitimate workloads, unlike the B1 advanced-mode variants.
acceptance_criteria: Three shapes benched Marshal+Unmarshal across all 4 libraries; included in bench-compare output; docs/comparison.md updated.
notes: Audit item B6 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e.

title: 32-bit (GOARCH=386) CI pass
description:
The generator emits dedicated 32-bit safety code (length-delimited varint decode into uint64 first, bounds-check before converting to int — see CLAUDE.md '32-bit length safety') but no CI job ever executes on a 32-bit int, so those guards are never exercised where they matter.

Fix: CI step running GOARCH=386 go vet ./... plus a 386 go test of the length-arithmetic packages: ./test/basic/ and ./protohelpers/.... No -race (unsupported on 386). Native execution of 386 binaries works on amd64 linux runners.
acceptance_criteria: CI has a 386 step covering test/basic and protohelpers; it fails if a 32-bit truncation guard is removed.
notes: Audit item T6 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-hgl.

title: testutil.Populate(m, rand): reflection-based message populator
description:
No way to randomly fill an arbitrary generated message. This blocks both broader fuzz seeding (wiresmith-a2t) and a cheap whole-corpus property test.

Fix: testutil.Populate(m, rand) that random-fills any constructor-registered message via reflection — depth-capped, covers scalars, maps, slices, oneofs, nested messages. Then:
1. Feed it into fuzz seed generation for wiresmith-a2t.
2. Add a populate -> marshal -> unmarshal -> Equal property test across all testutil constructors.

This is the useful 20% of gogoproto's Populate()/thetest.proto machinery (reference corpus at /Users/oleg-kozlyuk/Projects/gogoproto/test/) without the proto2/extensions 80%.
acceptance_criteria: Populate handles every field shape in the testutil corpus (verified by the property test passing over all constructors); deterministic given a seeded rand.
notes: Audit item T5 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-hgl. wiresmith-a2t (fuzz corpus) depends on this.

title: Option-interaction matrix tests
description:
Custom field option combinations (pointer, jsontag, customtype, customname, stdtime, stdduration, casttype) are tested individually but combos are mostly untested. The customtypeCompatiblePeers whitelist in compiler/generator/option_customtype.go encodes the compatibility policy but nothing enumerates it as a test matrix.

Fix, two levels:
1. Compiler-level: enumerate the whitelist as a matrix — every allowed pair gets a generation test, every rejected pair gets an error test (asserting the rejection message).
2. E2E: add whitelisted combos (customname+customtype, customname+jsontag, ...) to a proto/basic/ fixture plus a roundtrip test, so the combined generated code actually compiles and round-trips.
acceptance_criteria: Every pair in/out of customtypeCompatiblePeers has a test; at least the customname+customtype and customname+jsontag combos exist in a checked-in fixture with roundtrip coverage.
notes: Audit item T4 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-hgl.

title: Accept (gogoproto.*) field options as aliases for (wiresmith.options.*)
description:
**Source:** Deferred from [[wiresmith-06z]] notes (Clarified design, 2026-06-02): *"(3) gogoproto.customtype alias deferred (same call as jsontag — no precedent infra for embedding gogo.proto)."*

**What:** Accept `(gogoproto.customtype)`, `(gogoproto.casttype)`, `(gogoproto.nullable)`, `(gogoproto.stdtime)`, `(gogoproto.stdduration)`, `(gogoproto.jsontag)`, and `(gogoproto.goproto_enum_prefix)` as alias forms for the corresponding `(wiresmith.options.*)` extensions, so existing gogoproto-annotated `.proto` files compile under wiresmith without rewriting every annotation.

**Why:** Mimir has 29 `.proto` files using gogoproto annotations (see [[wiresmith-cqa]]). Without an alias, every file must be hand-edited to swap `(gogoproto.*)` → `(wiresmith.options.*)`. The annotation rewrite itself is mechanical but multiplies the size of the migration PR and makes back-porting bug fixes from the pre-migration branch painful.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Embed a minimal subset of `gogoproto/gogo.proto` (just the FieldOptions/EnumOptions extensions wiresmith implements) into the compiler binary, in parallel with the existing `wiresmith/options.proto` embedding.
- Serve it from the canonical import path `gogoproto/gogo.proto` so user proto trees can keep `import "gogoproto/gogo.proto";`.
- When the generator encounters a recognized gogoproto field option, translate it to the matching wiresmith option internally. Same validation rules apply (e.g. `customtype` rejected on map fields).
- Reject silently-unsupported gogoproto options with a clear error (`messagename_all`, `goproto_unkeyed_all`, etc. — see [[wiresmith-cqa]] for the no-op list).

**Out of scope:** matching every gogoproto option that wiresmith doesn't implement. The alias is for the options wiresmith has equivalents for; everything else stays an explicit error so consumers know to remove it.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A fixture proto using only `(gogoproto.*)` annotations compiles under wiresmith with the same output as the equivalent `(wiresmith.options.*)` version.
- Mixed-form file (some gogoproto, some wiresmith.options annotations on different fields in the same message) is rejected with a clear error: pick one form per file.
- Unsupported `(gogoproto.*)` annotations produce an actionable error citing the corresponding `(wiresmith.options.*)` if one exists, or "not supported" if not.
- Document the alias and the per-option mapping table in `docs/extensions.md`.

**Note:** lower priority than the underlying option beads (hnr, cr0, b34). The alias is migration ergonomics, not capability — the underlying options must ship first.

title: Investigate / harden CI fuzz flake: "context deadline exceeded" near 30s mark
description:
## Symptom

FuzzRoundTrip occasionally fails on CI with:

\`\`\`
fuzz: elapsed: 30s, execs: 260831 (3881/sec), new interesting: 20 (total: 25)
fuzz: elapsed: 31s, execs: 260831 (0/sec), new interesting: 20 (total: 25)
--- FAIL: FuzzRoundTrip (31.01s)
    context deadline exceeded
\`\`\`

No crasher artifact is written — Go's fuzz runner aborts on the per-worker context deadline before it can minimize and persist the offending input. Locally with -fuzztime=30s the same target shows the same pattern (a 3-second pause around elapsed=21-24s where execs stay flat) but exits 0 because >2 workers are available; CI has 2 workers, so a single stuck worker has more relative impact.

First observed on PR #105 (effb990) but the test code is unchanged from main — the diff that actually touches the unmarshal hot path is empty. Suggests this is a flake, not a regression.

## Hypothesis

A particular fuzzer-generated input lands on a slow path inside one of the message Unmarshal implementations (probably a recursion-heavy proto like Span or Profile) and a single iteration consumes >1 wall-clock second. When this happens near the end of the 30s fuzztime budget, Go's per-worker context-deadline cancellation fires, the worker reports "context deadline exceeded", and the test exits 1.

Two mitigations worth exploring:

1. **Bump CI fuzztime** to 60s (or pass -fuzztime via env var so locally we can use 30s). More budget makes the boundary case less likely.
2. **Per-constructor budget inside f.Fuzz**: wrap each constructor's roundtrip in a watchdog (time.AfterFunc + atomic flag, or context.WithTimeout) so a single slow message kind is skipped rather than blocking the whole worker.

Option 1 is cheap and probably enough. Option 2 is more robust if the flake reproduces frequently.

## Acceptance

- CI run-history dashboard shows zero "context deadline exceeded" Fuzz failures over the next ~20 PR merges.
- If flakes persist after option 1, the per-iteration watchdog from option 2 lands.

## Don't do if

- Re-runs of PR #105 (and any other affected PR) pass on first retry. Confirms it's a low-frequency flake, not worth chasing unless it grows.

title: Expand stdtime test coverage: out-of-spec nanos, signed cross-product, wire-format pins
description:
## Why

Competitive analysis against gogoproto / vtprotobuf / timestamppb (in PR #107 thread) flagged test gaps where a real-world peer could surface divergences from the official timestamppb contract:

- timestamppb accepts out-of-spec nanos on the wire and normalises via `time.Unix` (Go's `time.Unix` doc: 'valid to pass nsec outside [0, 999999999]'). Wiresmith inherits this through `time.Unix(seconds, int64(nanos)).UTC()` but it isn't pinned by a test, so a future 'validate on unmarshal' optimization would silently break interop.
- The existing wire-format pin only goes ours→official; the reverse direction (official-encoded bytes decoded by ours) isn't covered.
- Negative-seconds varint emission (10-byte sign-extended varint) and the inner nanos tag byte (`0x10`) are common regression sites with no direct byte-level assertion.

## Scope

Add to `test/basic/stdtime_test.go`:

1. `TestStdtime_NanosOutOfSpecDecodeNormalises` — hand-built wire bytes for `{seconds:-1, nanos:-1}` and `{seconds:0, nanos:1_000_000_000}` decode to `time.Unix(-1,-1).UTC()` and `time.Unix(1,0).UTC()`.
2. `TestStdtime_SignedNanosCrossProduct` — table over `seconds ∈ {-1,0,+1}` × `wire-nanos ∈ {-1,0,+1}`. Mirrors the official timestamppb test (timestamp_test.go:65-73).
3. `TestStdtime_Year1MinusOneNanosecondAndYear9999PlusOne` — `±1ns` past the canonical year-1 / year-9999 boundaries. My current TestStdtime_FarPastAndFuture only goes year-2 / year-9999-at-max.
4. `TestStdtime_NanosWireFormatIsInt32Varint` — assert the inner field-2 tag byte is `0x10` (varint), not zigzag or group. Catches sint32-regression.
5. `TestStdtime_OfficialEncodeRoundTripsToOurs` — complement to TestStdtime_CrossLibraryWireFormat going the other direction (official-encoded → ours-decoded). vtproto's wkt_test.go does this both ways.
6. `TestStdtime_NegativeSecondsTenByteVarint` — pin that pre-1970 `time.Time` produces a 10-byte sign-extended varint inside the envelope.

## Acceptance

- All six tests added under `test/basic/stdtime_test.go`.
- Run via `GOLANG_PROTOBUF_REGISTRATION_CONFLICT=warn go test ./test/basic -run TestStdtime`.
- No production code changes expected; if a test surfaces a real bug, file separately and fix in the same PR.

## References

- PR #107 (initial stdtime feature, wiresmith-gqx).
- Official timestamppb tests: https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf-go/blob/master/types/known/timestamppb/timestamp_test.go
- vtproto WKT byte-pin: https://github.com/planetscale/vtprotobuf/blob/main/testproto/wkt/wkt_test.go

title: Decide string UTF-8 semantics
description: Compare the behavior against competing compilers (gogoproto/vtproto/protoc) and let user decide if it needs to be fixed or documented. Review string field and string map-key decoding in compiler/types/string.go, which converts bytes directly to string without utf8.Valid. Determine whether wiresmith should validate invalid UTF-8 or document invalid-UTF-8 acceptance as deliberate non-conformance, then pin the chosen behavior in tests.

title: Investigate map-entry decode boundary handling
description: Compare the behavior against competing compilers (gogoproto/vtproto/protoc) and let user decide if it needs to be fixed or documented. Review compiler/types/map.go inner map-entry decoding: tag decode, length decode, and skipValue should be compared for whether malformed inner fields can consume bytes past the map entry boundary. Include malformed-map-entry probes for unknown fields, wrong wire type fields, string keys, bytes values, and message values.

title: Investigate unknown-field varint overflow in skipValue
description: Compare the behavior against competing compilers (gogoproto/vtproto/protoc) and let user decide if it needs to be fixed or documented. Review compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go unknown varint skipping: 10-byte varints currently enforce a byte count but do not reject a terminating 10th byte with payload bits greater than 1. Include a regression probe for unknown field 99 with a 10-byte varint ending in 0x7f.

title: Add (wiresmith.options.goproto_enum_prefix) — control enum value name prefixing
description:
**Source:** MIMIR.md "Enum prefixing".

**What gogo does:** by default, gogo emits enum value constants prefixed with the parent enum name: `enum Color { COLOR_RED = 0; }` → `Color_COLOR_RED`. Setting `(gogoproto.goproto_enum_prefix) = false` removes the prefix, emitting just `COLOR_RED`. Useful for cleaner enum value names where the prefix is already in the proto value.

**Wiresmith today:** matches `protoc-gen-go`'s prefixing rule (`Color_COLOR_RED` for top-level enums, parent-message chain for nested). See `docs/design.md:27`. This bead adds an opt-out.

**Proposed wiresmith shape:**
- Add `(wiresmith.options.goproto_enum_prefix) = false` as an enum-level option (not field-level).
- When set, emit constants without the type-name prefix: `COLOR_RED` instead of `Color_COLOR_RED`.
- The `Color_name` / `Color_value` maps still use the bare proto names as keys (unchanged — those already lack the prefix).
- The String() method on the enum type still works; only the constant name changes.

**Risk:** name collisions across enums in the same package. If two enums in the same file both have a `RED` value with prefix-off, Go won't compile. Validate at codegen time and error out.

**Priority justification (P3, lower than the other gogo-compat features):** Mimir can rename its enum values to avoid the prefix if needed (just edit the .proto). The other gogo features cannot be worked around as cheaply.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Test fixture with the option set on one enum and unset on another.
- Collision-detection test: a fixture with two enums sharing a value name + prefix-off should fail to generate.
- Document the constraint in `docs/extensions.md`.
notes: Mimir blocker context (per [[wiresmith-cqa]]): only pkg/mimirpb/mimir.proto uses goproto_enum_prefix=true (Histogram.ResetHint enum). Cheap Mimir-side workaround: rename the enum values from UNKNOWN/YES/NO/GAUGE to RESET_HINT_UNKNOWN/RESET_HINT_YES/etc. so the prefix is baked into the proto definition itself. This bead can be deferred indefinitely without blocking the migration.
close_reason: Superseded by shipped (wiresmith.options.enum_no_prefix)/enum_no_prefix_all (option_enum_no_prefix.go + fixture + docs) — gogo goproto_enum_prefix=false parity. Mimir's enum-prefix need (MIMIR.md) is met; cqa no longer blocked on this. Only delta — codegen-time collision validation that 1dq proposed (enum_no_prefix leaves collisions to go build) — split to wiresmith-lvb5 (P3).

title: JSON-4: Add MarshalText / UnmarshalText for text proto format
description:
**Parent:** wiresmith-4kx (JSON/text umbrella).

**What:** Emit `MarshalText() ([]byte, error)` and `UnmarshalText([]byte) error` producing/consuming proto text format (the `prototext` package's output shape).

**Priority justification (P3, lower than JSON-1..3):** Text proto is largely a debugging/CLI format. Tempo/Mimir need JSON, not text. Land last in the umbrella; can defer indefinitely without blocking JSON consumers.

**Key requirements:**
- Field names = proto names (NOT lowerCamelCase — text format keeps snake_case).
- Singular: `field_name: value` per field. Strings double-quoted; bytes `"\xNN"`-escaped.
- Repeated: one entry per line, OR `field_name: [v1, v2, ...]` shorthand for scalars.
- Maps: `field_name { key: K value: V }` per entry.
- Oneofs: only the active variant.
- Enums by name, fallback to number for unknown.
- Comments tolerated on parse, never emitted.

**Out of scope:** Parsing of compact JSON-like syntax `{ field: v }` that protoc accepts. Stick to the canonical multi-line form on output.

**Validation:** Round-trip `MarshalText → UnmarshalText → Equal`. Differential vs `google.golang.org/protobuf/encoding/prototext` for the OTel macros.

title: Experiment: inline EncodeVarint body into generated code
description:
Investigate whether emitting the body of protohelpers.EncodeVarint directly at each generated call site (or as a per-package unexported helper) measurably improves marshal performance vs. the current cross-package function call.

## Context

- 742 EncodeVarint call sites across gen/otlp, gen/basic, gen/test, etc.
- Function body has a for-loop, so Go's inliner cannot inline it across packages — every call is a real function call today.
- protohelpers package must remain regardless because the registry (RegisterType/RegisterEnum/MessageType/EnumValueMap) requires shared global state across all generated packages. This experiment is purely about the encoder, not eliminating the package.
- Prior investigation of //go:fix inline (documented in CLAUDE.md "Rejected approaches") established that the inliner refuses multi-statement bodies. This experiment goes a step further: have the wiresmith generator emit the body itself.

## Variants to measure

1. Baseline: current protohelpers.EncodeVarint call.
2. Emit body inline at every call site (full duplication, no helper).
3. Emit a per-package unexported helper (one definition per generated package, called from within the package).

## Method

- Add a benchmark that exercises marshal-heavy paths if existing bench/ coverage is insufficient.
- Use `make bench-compare` with a baseline of variant 1 vs each alternative.
- Report ns/op, B/op, allocs/op, and binary-size delta (`ls -la` on a representative built binary or `go build -o` + size).
- Keep an eye on compile time and generated-file size as secondary signals.

## Acceptance criteria

- Benchmarks run on at least one OTel-style proto (e.g. trace.proto) and one map-heavy proto.
- Decision recorded: adopt, reject, or "needs more data" — with numbers.
- If rejected, add a note to CLAUDE.md "Rejected approaches" section so future contributors don't re-litigate.

title: DOC-14: README/AGENTS framed as OTel-only despite generic proto3
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `README.md:3`; `AGENTS.md:3`.

**What:** Both top-line descriptions say "OpenTelemetry .proto files". The generator (`compiler/generator/generator.go`) handles any proto3. One OTel heuristic in `names.go` for `opentelemetry.proto.*` paths, but that's path-mapping, not a constraint.

**Fix:** Reword to "Custom protobuf compiler... originally designed for OpenTelemetry .proto files but supports any proto3 schema." Opens the door for non-OTel adopters.
notes: Two stale README claims to address in the rewording (not just the OTel framing): (1) line 3 'from OpenTelemetry .proto files' — broaden to 'originally designed for OpenTelemetry but supports any proto3 schema'; (2) line 71 'Not supported (not needed for OTel protos): services/RPCs, extensions, well-known types, proto2.' — services/RPCs ARE now supported via protoc-gen-go-grpc reuse ([[wiresmith-36b]] closed via [[wiresmith-dei]]); google.protobuf.Timestamp is supported via [[wiresmith-gqx]] stdtime. Reword to 'Not supported: extensions, proto2; partial WKT support — see [[wiresmith-7m6]]'.

title: DOC-13: No end-to-end 'your first .proto' example
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** missing.

**What:** Onboarding from "I have a .proto" to "I have Go code" is undocumented. No `examples/` directory.

**Fix:** Add `examples/hello/` with one .proto, the wiresmith command, and a snippet of the resulting `.pb.go`. Reference from README "Usage".

title: DOC-12: gen/protohelpers minimal docs and no stability marker
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `gen/protohelpers/protohelpers.go:1-3`; `registry.go` has none.

**What:** It's the ABI between wiresmith binary and every generated file, but unmarked re: stability. `MessageType`/`EnumValueMap` are exported but never used inside the repo — reflection escape hatches with no callers and no docs.

**Fix:** Add a `doc.go` covering: package purpose, ABI status (experimental until v0.1), and a note that `MessageType`/`EnumValueMap` are reflection escape hatches.
notes: Description references gen/protohelpers/ which no longer exists — [[wiresmith-ick]] (closed) relocated the package to top-level protohelpers/. Updated paths: protohelpers/protohelpers.go, protohelpers/message.go, protohelpers/stdtime.go. The intent (add a doc.go covering ABI stability + reflection escape hatches) is still valid.

title: TEST-12: Test mixed packed + unpacked elements in same field
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `test/peer/vtprotobuf_patterns_test.go` covers concatenated packed chunks; no test interleaves packed and individual unpacked elements.

**What:** Proto3 wire spec allows both encodings to appear in the same field concurrently.

**Fix:** Build a hand-crafted wire: `chunk1 (packed [1,2]) + single unpacked 3 + chunk2 (packed [4,5])`, assert all 5 values arrive in order.
notes:
Scope extended by 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): grow this into a full merge-semantics differential table, test/basic/merge_semantics_test.go — hand-built wire bytes unmarshaled by BOTH wiresmith and the official runtime, asserting identical resulting messages. Rows:
- duplicate scalar/string/bytes field: last-wins
- duplicate message field: MERGE not replace (the most likely silent divergence between implementations)
- duplicate oneof across different variants: last variant wins
- duplicate map key: REPLACE (see CLAUDE.md map-field caveats / wiresmith-05d)
- packed + unpacked interleavings (the original scope of this issue)
Reuse the packed-chunk wire builders from test/peer/vtprotobuf_patterns_test.go:362-456.

title: DOC-11: Conformance count duplicated across docs (fragility)
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `AGENTS.md:69`; `test/conformance/failure_list.txt` summary; (README is silent — good).

**What:** "695 passing, 5 expected failures" appears in both places. Today accurate; one will drift on next change.

**Fix:** Single source of truth — `failure_list.txt` summary comment. `AGENTS.md` should say "see `test/conformance/failure_list.txt`" instead of restating the count.

title: TEST-11: Regenerate or remove stale coverage profile
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `coverage.out` (dated 2026-05-07).

**What:** Profile shows many compiler-side functions as 0% that are clearly live (`compiler/generator/types.go:goType` etc.) — the profile cannot be trusted for triage.

**Fix:** Either re-run before each coverage audit, or wire `make coverage` into CI so the file is regenerated automatically.
notes: 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): while here, gitignore the stray local bench artifacts (bench.test, benchstat.txt) so they stop showing up as untracked noise.

title: ARCH-10: Add compiler/generator/AGENTS.md and refresh types/AGENTS.md
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** missing `compiler/generator/AGENTS.md`; `compiler/types/AGENTS.md:6-19` omits `PointerField`/`RepeatedPointer`.

**What:** A contributor adding a new emit phase has nothing to read. Type-side doc lists 4 composites; codebase has 6.

**Fix:** Add `compiler/generator/AGENTS.md` listing phases in order with invariants (e.g. "enums before structs"). Update `compiler/types/AGENTS.md` to enumerate all six composites with one-line predicates, and extend the "Adding a new type" checklist to mention `ZeroLiteral`, `EmitEqual`, `EmitEncode`, `EmitMapEntryUnmarshal`.

title: ARCH-9: Use errors.Join to accumulate validation errors
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/generator/option_pointer.go:85-114` accumulates `[]string` then joins; `generator.go:80-159` is fail-fast everywhere else.

**What:** Users who fix one validation error then rerun and discover the next have slow iteration loops. The string-concat-error pattern also loses structure — IDE integration / future tooling cannot read per-field details.

**Fix:** Use `errors.Join` (Go 1.20+) for the validation phases (`buildImportMapping`, `collectGoPackages`, `validateDestinations`, `validatePointerOptions`). Keep `Generate` fail-fast on the first compilation/IO error.

title: PERF-10: Remove dead group-wire-type code from skipValue
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:44-47`.

**What:** Proto3 forbids groups; every generated `skipValue` still emits a `case 3:` calling `protowire.ConsumeGroup`. Brings in protowire as a hard dependency just for the never-fired branch.

**Fix:** Emit `case 3, 4: return fmt.Errorf("groups not supported in proto3")`. Reduces icache pressure and binary size. Verify protowire is still needed elsewhere (it is, for `SizeVarint`).

title: ARCH-8: Move field_survival_test.go under test/basic/
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `test/field_survival_test.go`.

**What:** It's a roundtrip-survival test — same conceptual category as `test/basic/roundtrip_test.go`. Currently the only file at the top level of `test/`.

**Fix:** Move into `test/basic/`. Update any package-name references.

title: PERF-9: Add flag/option to skip presence-bitmap RMW
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:194-196`; ~38 RMW sites in `metrics.pb.go`.

**What:** `m.fieldsPresent[0] |= 1 << X` runs unconditionally. OTel hot-path consumers rarely call `Has*`.

**Fix:** CLI flag `--no-presence-bitmap` and/or per-message proto option to skip the bitmap. Getters then fall back to zero-value semantics (matching gogoproto).

**Validate:** Re-bench with flag enabled; expect 1-3% on UnmarshalTraces.

title: ARCH-7: Introduce MessageContext and document generateFile phase ordering
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/generator/generator.go:40-50, 271-309`.

**What:** `FileGenerator.body *bytes.Buffer` plus `imports` is fine, but `generateFile` invokes phases in a hardcoded order whose constraints are only readable by tracing every emit-site's `addImport` call. `presenceMap(md)` is recomputed in every phase (`emit_struct.go:54`, `emit_size.go:18`, `emit_marshal.go:46`, `emit_unmarshal.go:190`, `emit_getter.go:16`).

**Fix:** Introduce a `MessageContext` computed once per message holding presence map, sorted fields, pre-scan field list. Thread it through emit phases. Document phase-ordering invariants at the top of `generateFile` (one comment per edge: "Equal after structs so embedded MessageType.EmitEqual has type names").

title: PERF-8: Add unsafe.String opt-in for borrowed-buffer unmarshal
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/types/string.go:67`.

**What:** OTel collectors typically own input buffers for the duration of pipeline processing. A `string(dAtA[...])` copy is pure overhead in that scenario.

**Fix:** Gate an `unsafe.String(unsafe.SliceData(dAtA[iNdEx:]), n)` path behind a CLI flag (`--unsafe-borrow-input`) or proto option. Document the lifetime contract precisely (input buffer must outlive the parsed message).

**Validate:** `UnmarshalLogs` (string-heavy); expect 20-30% alloc reduction.

title: CR-10: UTF-8 not validated on string unmarshal
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/types/string.go:67` does `string(dAtA[iNdEx:postIndex])` without `utf8.Valid`.

**What:** Proto3 spec says string fields *must* be valid UTF-8; spec-compliant decoders reject invalid sequences. Conformance suite doesn't test it for proto3, and pdata also doesn't validate, so this matches the OTel ecosystem norm.

**Fix:** Document the divergence in `AGENTS.md`. If strict validation is ever desired, gate it behind a flag — never make it default (the perf cost is non-trivial for log streams full of strings).

title: SEC-8: gen/protohelpers/ registry has no synchronisation
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `gen/protohelpers/registry.go:7-42`.

**What:** Docs say "not safe for concurrent use" but make no statement about ordering. Reads after `init()`-time writes are safe under Go's memory model, but a caller who spawns `go register()` from inside an `init()` (legal, if odd) races undetected.

**Fix:** Either guard the maps with a `sync.RWMutex` or tighten the package doc: "Register* MUST be called from package init only; any goroutine-spawned registration is undefined behavior."
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: PERF-7: Calibrate or scale preScanMinBytes threshold
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/generator/emit_unmarshal.go:83`.

**What:** Below 256 bytes, no pre-scan; above it, full scan. The threshold is uncalibrated and a single message with 1 attribute at 257 B walks all bytes for no payoff.

**Fix:** Either tune empirically per benchmark, or scale the threshold by `len(fieldsForPreScan(md))` (more fields → benefit threshold is higher). Add a microbench at boundary sizes.

title: CR-9: Map marshal iteration order is non-deterministic
description:
**Severity:** Low

**Location:** `compiler/types/map.go:54` (`for k, v := range %s`).

**What:** Go map iteration order is randomized; two marshals of the same message produce different byte slices when maps are present. Matches official protobuf-go's default but breaks content-addressed storage / cryptographic signing use cases.

**Fix:** Either (a) sort keys before iteration (cost: per-marshal allocation + sort); (b) add an opt-in `--deterministic-maps` flag; or (c) just document the non-determinism in `AGENTS.md` "Design decisions". Pick based on whether OTel collector hot path needs determinism.
close_reason: Already fixed in code — verified during session audit (see commit message for per-bead evidence)

title: BUF-2.x: Support remaining WKTs (Empty, Struct, FieldMask)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md Layer 2 — general buf-managed proto goal. **Split from wiresmith-7m6**, which is narrowed to `google.protobuf.Any`.

Support the remaining well-known types: `google.protobuf.Empty`, `Struct` (+ `Value` / `ListValue`), and `FieldMask`.

Not on the migration critical path: a WKT-usage audit (2026-06-18, non-vendor `.proto` across tempo/loki/mimir-wiresmith) found **none** of the three consumers import these. Tracked for completeness of buf-managed proto support beyond the three current consumers. Sequence after `Any` (wiresmith-7m6).

title: Fix inaccurate 'single consumer' comment on hasNoPresence/fieldsForPresence
description:
Two comments claim hasNoPresence/fieldsForPresence is the single routing point for all bitmap consumers:
- option_no_presence.go:13-14: 'hasNoPresence is the single consumer (called from fieldsForPresence, which every bitmap-emitting site already routes through)'
- emit_has.go:30-34: 'Every bitmap consumer ... routes through this function ... the single switch that disables them all consistently'

But emit_getter.go:122 calls fg.hasNoPresence(md) DIRECTLY to choose the value-vs-pointer getter shape — it does not route through fieldsForPresence/presenceMap. The direct call is arguably justified (the getter needs the return-type decision, not just the presence bit), but the invariant comments are inaccurate as written and will mislead a future maintainer into thinking a single fieldsForPresence edit covers all no_presence behavior.
design: Either (a) reword both comments to acknowledge the getter's direct hasNoPresence call as a second, type-shape consumer, or (b) route the getter's decision through a shared helper so the 'single switch' claim becomes true.
notes: Found in code review of PR #125 (databases stack). Comment-accuracy finding (low).

title: Collapse triplicated bool-extension reader helpers into one generic helper
description:
boolMessageOption, boolFileOption (compiler/generator/option_no_presence.go:41-72) and boolEnumOption (compiler/generator/option_enum_no_prefix.go) are byte-for-byte identical except the *descriptorpb.{Message,File,Enum}Options type assertion. They each: nil-check ext, assert opts type, build extensionType, proto.HasExtension, proto.GetExtension(...).(bool).

Two costs:
1. Triplication — a fourth scope (e.g. ServiceOptions) needs a fourth copy; a divergence in one copy (e.g. wrong nil-return) silently changes behavior for that option only.
2. Discoverability — boolFileOption lives in option_no_presence.go but is called by hasEnumNoPrefix in option_enum_no_prefix.go; the shared primitives belong in option.go alongside extensionType/hasBoolOption.
design: Replace the three with one generic helper, e.g. func readBoolExt[O proto.Message](ext protoreflect.FieldDescriptor, opts O) (value, ok bool), or a non-generic form taking proto.Message. Move it to option.go next to the existing extension-lookup primitives.
notes: Found in code review of PR #125 (databases stack). Reuse/simplification finding.

title: Generalize 'type-replacing option' lists into a FieldOption predicate
description:
The concept 'this option swaps a MessageKind field for a value type that carries its own presence' is encoded as two parallel hand-maintained lists:
- suppressMessageType (compiler/generator/option.go:247-256): checks stdtimeOption/stddurationOption/customtypeOption
- fieldsForPresence (compiler/generator/emit_has.go:44-71): checks hasPointerOption/hasStdtimeOption/hasCustomtypeOption/hasStdDurationOption

PR #125 (wiresmith-uol) had to add customtype to BOTH. A future such option must be added in both or it silently desyncs: a suppressMessageType miss registers a dead import (non-compiling file); a fieldsForPresence miss makes a present-but-empty value round-trip wrong (bitmap says present, customtype emitter ignores the bit).
design: Add a predicate to the FieldOption interface (e.g. replacesMessageKind() bool or carriesOwnPresence() bool) so suppressMessageType and fieldsForPresence iterate fg.options generically. The hardcoded lists dissolve and new type-replacing options become correct by construction. Note pointer-option is MessageKind-gated in fieldsForPresence but not in suppressMessageType — reconcile when generalizing.
notes: Found in code review of PR #125 (databases stack). Altitude finding: prefer generalizing the mechanism over accreting special cases.

title: Generated-code + binary size report
description:
The icache-density story is load-bearing (the _compare.pb.go split, the rejected +70%-LOC unrolled varint) but code/binary size is never tracked as a number.

Design: make size-report target — LOC and bytes per gen/ tree, plus linked test-binary size per library import set. Manual run, results recorded as a docs/comparison.md section. Deliberately NOT in CI — it is a periodically refreshed report, not a gate.
acceptance_criteria: make size-report produces per-tree LOC/bytes + binary sizes; docs/comparison.md has the section with current numbers.
notes: Audit item B8 (2026-06-10). Part of epic wiresmith-d0e.

title: Support (wiresmith.options.stdtime) + (wiresmith.options.pointer) combo: emit *time.Time
description:
## Why

The current stdtime implementation (PR #107) rejects the combination with `(wiresmith.options.pointer) = true` outright (`stdtimeOptionRejection` in compiler/generator/option_stdtime.go: 'cannot combine, pick one'). That was overly conservative — the combination is semantically coherent and gives users the same explicit-nullability escape hatch `pointer` provides for every other message field.

Matrix the user gets if we allow it:

| options | Go field | 'unset' |
|---|---|---|
| `stdtime` alone | `time.Time` | `t.IsZero()` |
| `stdtime` + `pointer` | `*time.Time` | `nil` (matches gogo nullable+stdtime) |

The pointer form is what Mimir / Tempo users typically reach for when they need to distinguish 'truly unset' from 'set to the Unix epoch' or want to send `time.Time{}` (year 1) explicitly.

## Scope

- Drop the pointer-combo rejection in `stdtimeOptionRejection` (compiler/generator/option_stdtime.go).
- Add a `pointer=true` branch to `stdtimeFieldType` / `stdtimeGoFieldType` (compiler/generator/option_pointer.go) that returns `*time.Time` and routes Size/Marshal/Unmarshal/Equal/Compare through a nil-guarded wrapper.
- Likely shape: reuse the existing `PointerField` composite with `StdtimeType` as Inner. Will need StdtimeType to play nicely with PointerField's nil-pair semantics (or introduce StdtimePointerField if PointerField assumes Message semantics).
- Update emit_getter.go to emit `func (m *Holder) GetCreated() *time.Time` returning the stored pointer (nil-safe).
- Update docs/extensions.md matrix and emit_has.go (pointer-mode stdtime field exclusion from bitmap stays, presence is the nil check).

## Acceptance

- `proto/basic/stdtime.proto` (or a new sibling fixture) gains a pointer+stdtime field alongside the existing value-shape field.
- New tests in `test/basic/stdtime_test.go` covering: nil = absent on marshal; non-nil zero `time.Time{}` round-trips as year-1 envelope; the value-shape and pointer-shape fields coexist in one message.
- Rejection test removed for the now-allowed combo; the 6 remaining rejection cases still pass.
- Docs updated.

## References

- PR #107 (initial stdtime feature, wiresmith-gqx).
- Discussion: https://github.com/grafana/wiresmith/pull/107 (user feedback flagged the rejection as overly conservative).

title: Emit gRPC service descriptors so wiresmith files keep services in their FileDescriptor
description:
## Why

Today serializeFileDescriptor strips fdp.Service before marshaling and emits NumServices: 0 (compiler/generator/emit_registration.go::serializeFileDescriptor) because protoimpl.filetype.Builder.initDecls cross-checks the descriptor's service array length against TypeBuilder.NumServices and panics on mismatch.

The trade-off is documented in the comment above the strip: wiresmith-owned protoreflect.FileDescriptor reports Services().Len() == 0 even when the source .proto declares services. Runtime gRPC works fine (reflection.Register serves grpc.Server.GetServiceInfo, which reads the live grpc.ServiceDesc from protoc-gen-go-grpc), but descriptor-walking consumers (gRPC reflection's FileContainingSymbol / FileByFilename, anything that does protoregistry.GlobalFiles.RangeFiles → fd.Services()) won't surface wiresmith-owned services.

The official protoc-gen-go matches the cardinality the other way: it KEEPS services in the descriptor and sets NumServices: len(f.Services), and emits the per-service ServiceInfo entries that initDecls walks (cmd/protoc-gen-go/internal_gengo/reflect.go; see wiresmith-dei competitor analysis in PR #105 thread).

gogoproto sidesteps the problem by using the legacy proto.RegisterFile(gzippedBytes) path which has no cardinality check at all — not viable for wiresmith because we want the modern protoreflect path for messages.

## What

Match protoc-gen-go's approach so wiresmith-owned files keep services in their FileDescriptor and protoreflect-based reflection can find them.

Concrete pieces:
1. Stop stripping fdp.Service in serializeFileDescriptor.
2. Emit NumServices: N (N = len(fd.Services)) in the TypeBuilder.
3. Emit a file-level []protoimpl.ServiceInfo array (one entry per service) — the format initDecls expects, mirroring what protoc-gen-go emits next to the existing file_*_msgTypes / file_*_enumTypes arrays.
4. Populate per-service depIdxs — method input/output type indices into the file's combined message-table — so initDecls can wire each ServiceInfo's Methods to the right protoreflect.MessageDescriptor.
5. New basic fixture under proto/basic/ (or extend proto/basic/service.proto if appropriate) that asserts protoreflect.FileDescriptor.Services().Len() > 0 for a wiresmith-owned .proto with a service.

Out of scope: emitting service stubs / server interfaces / client interfaces. protoc-gen-go-grpc still owns that. This bead is purely descriptor-side scaffolding so reflection can find the service.

## Acceptance

- gen/basic/service/v1/service.pb.go (or equivalent) regenerates with the embedded raw descriptor including the Echo service and with NumServices: 1.
- A new test in test/basic/ (or test/peer/) walks protoregistry.GlobalFiles for the wiresmith-owned service file and asserts Services().Get(0).FullName() == "basic.service.v1.Echo".
- Existing test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go continues to pass (the runtime grpc path is unaffected).
- Conformance count unchanged.

## References

- compiler/generator/emit_registration.go::serializeFileDescriptor (the strip site)
- google.golang.org/protobuf cmd/protoc-gen-go/internal_gengo/reflect.go (reference impl)
- google.golang.org/protobuf internal/filetype/build.go (the cardinality check)
- PR #105 (wiresmith-dei) — competitor analysis lives in the thread there
notes: 2026-06-18: no current consumer (tempo/loki/mimir-wiresmith) registers gRPC server reflection, so none need descriptor-side service entries. Removed as a blocker of bg7 (Loki migration); kept open at P4 as reflection-completeness polish. Runtime gRPC + stubs are done (df6/dei closed).

title: Add CLI integration tests
description: Add temp-dir integration tests for cmd/wiresmith process-level behavior. Cover core flows such as -M mapping, --version, positional proto files, and errors for files outside the proto path.

title: Cover documented reflection limitations with tests
description: Add explicit tests for unsupported reflection/runtime APIs documented in gen/protohelpers/message.go. Cover field-level reflection panics or errors, protojson/prototext behavior, Clone/Merge behavior, and deterministic marshal slow paths so future runtime changes fail loudly.
notes: 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-hgl): cross-link with wiresmith-66m (nil-receiver sweep) — the sweep's explicit skip-list for documented ProtoReflect panics should reference the tests added here, so the two stay one pinned contract rather than drifting apart.

title: Broaden differential and panic fuzzing coverage
description: Extend fuzz coverage beyond TracesData, MetricsData, and LogsData. Add profile differential coverage where a suitable reference exists, add map-bearing/basic/pointer/compare/crossmap constructors to panic and size fuzzing where safe, and seed malformed map-entry plus unknown-varint cases.

title: Run compiler unit tests in CI
description: Update the correctness test wiring so compiler unit tests run in CI. make test currently runs only go test ./test/..., and the GitHub Actions workflow invokes make build plus make test, so compiler/generator and compiler/types unit tests are not covered. Include ./compiler/... in make test or add an explicit CI step.

title: gRPC perf: comparative bench wiresmith+grpc vs protoc-gen-go+grpc
description:
Build a comparative per-RPC latency benchmark that puts (wiresmith + protoc-gen-go-grpc) next to (protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc) on the same .proto.

Setup challenge: both generators would register the same proto package globally, so the comparison harness needs separate Go packages (or a parallel process model) to keep the registries non-conflicting. One approach: copy proto/basic/service.proto into a sibling proto under a different go_package, generate it with protoc-gen-go to a parallel gen/bench/grpc_official/ tree, and run the bench against both endpoints inside one test binary.

Why now? The hot-path Methods table dispatch is already empirically verified by test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go (a slow-path fall-through would panic through wiresmith's MessageReflect.panicReflect). The remaining open question is the numeric magnitude — should match the standalone Marshal/Unmarshal wins minus gRPC framing overhead. Worth measuring once to record numbers, not blocking.

Why P4: empirical pass already proves the fast path is engaged; this is a confidence-boost numeric exercise, not a correctness gate. Pair with wiresmith-dei for context.
notes: 2026-06-10 audit (epic wiresmith-d0e): keep P4. wiresmith-5fx (Size benchmarks on all shapes) captures the gRPC-relevant codec cost — the gRPC codec path is Size+Marshal — and an e2e RPC bench will be dominated by HTTP/2 framing + syscalls, so the standalone benches are the better signal-to-noise instrument. This issue remains a one-off confidence-boost measurement.

title: gRPC docs: 'Using wiresmith with gRPC' how-to section
description:
Add a docs section explaining how to use wiresmith with protoc-gen-go-grpc: same proto file declares messages + service, both generators target the same go_package, wiresmith strips services from its registration (compiler/generator/emit_registration.go::serializeFileDescriptor), protoc-gen-go-grpc emits the stubs. Should reference the proven recipe (proto/basic/service.proto + Makefile generate-ours) and the test pattern (test/peer/grpc_protoc_compat_test.go) so adopters can copy it. Place in docs/ — e.g. docs/grpc.md — and link from README.md.

Why P4: enabling feature is verified and shipped (wiresmith-dei); docs are a nice-to-have for adopters and not blocking any consumer.

title: Drop ;<name> in Makefile wiresmith_mflags; rename gen/opentelemetry/* package clauses to v1
description: Follow-up to wiresmith-gz4. The Makefile's wiresmith_mflags helper currently appends ';commonv1' (etc.) to each -M override so the generated OTel packages keep their historical synthetic name (commonv1, logsv1, …). All three reference compilers (protoc-gen-go, vtproto, gogoproto) emit 'package v1' for the same files (path.Base of the override), and the wiresmith-internal gen/vtpb and gen/gogopb packages already do so — every consumer in test/ and bench/ uses explicit import aliases like 'commonv1 "…/common/v1"', so the natural-name dependency is purely cosmetic. Action: drop the ';name' suffix from wiresmith_mflags, regenerate, and confirm the test/bench imports still compile (they should — they're already aliased). This is API-facing only for downstream wiresmith consumers who imported the OTel packages unaliased; nothing in this repo does that.
notes: Originally bundled into wiresmith-gz4 but split out to keep that PR focused on the resolver change + escape-hatch flag. The ;name suffix in the Makefile preserves the pre-gz4 API.

title: BUF-3.3: Stable versioning (SemVer tags)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 3

### Stable versioning
SemVer tags so `buf generate` can pin a version.

title: BUF-3.2: Reproducible build (Dockerfile or pinned toolchain)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 3

### Reproducible build
Dockerfile or pinned Go toolchain version that buf.build's CI can reproduce.

title: BUF-3.1: buf.plugin.yaml metadata
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 3

### `buf.plugin.yaml`
Plugin metadata: name, version, runtime (Go binary or Docker), dependencies.

title: BUF-2.3: Sample buf.gen.yaml in README
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 2

### Sample `buf.gen.yaml`
One snippet in the README:

```yaml
plugins:
  - local: protoc-gen-wiresmith
    out: gen
    opt:
      - module=wiresmith
```

title: BUF-2.1: Honor paths=source_relative
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 2

### Honor `paths=source_relative`
buf's default places files at `<source_relative>/<basename>.pb.go`. wiresmith currently derives paths from `option go_package` + `--module` (see `outputPathFor` at `compiler/generator/generator.go:121`). Either support both modes or document a wiresmith-only convention and accept the divergence.

title: BUF-1.7: Don't emit empty files
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Don't emit empty files
If a generated file would be empty (e.g., proto contains only services that wiresmith doesn't emit), suppress it. vtproto exposes `--allow-empty` because protoc rejects empties — wiresmith should default to suppression.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.6: Return errors via the response
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Return errors via the response
The validation errors at `compiler/generator/generator.go:101-108` (output collisions), `generator.go:155-159` (inconsistent `go_package`), and `generator.go:177` (`..` in `go_package`) must go into `CodeGeneratorResponse.Error` instead of bubbling up. stderr is fine for warnings; fatal results belong in the response.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.5: Plugin parameters (opt: from buf.gen.yaml)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Plugin parameters
Parse `opt:` strings from `buf.gen.yaml` — at minimum `module=` (matching buf's strip-prefix semantic). Bind via `flag.FlagSet` and `protogen.Options.ParamFunc`.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.4: Filter to FilesToGenerate
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Filter to `FilesToGenerate`
`CodeGeneratorRequest` ships every transitive import, but the plugin must only emit for files listed in `FilesToGenerate`. Current code generates for every result from `protocompile.Compile`.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.3: Stop writing to disk
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Stop writing to disk
Replace `os.MkdirAll` + `os.WriteFile` at `compiler/generator/generator.go:238-242` with appending `*pluginpb.CodeGeneratorResponse_File` entries. buf places the files; the plugin doesn't.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.2: Generator must accept descriptors, not a directory
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### Generator must accept descriptors, not a directory
`Generator.Generate()` at `compiler/generator/generator.go:68` is hardwired to `protocompile.Compile(importPaths...)`. For plugin mode, descriptors arrive pre-linked from buf — the generator needs a second entry path that takes `linker.Files` (or a `protoreflect.FileDescriptor` slice) directly. The downstream emit code is already descriptor-based, so this is a parser-detach rather than a rewrite.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-1.1: New entry point (cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith/main.go)
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, Layer 1

### New entry point
A `cmd/protoc-gen-wiresmith/main.go` using `google.golang.org/protobuf/compiler/protogen`:

```
protogen.Options{ParamFunc: f.Set}.Run(func(plugin *protogen.Plugin) error { ... })
```

Reads `CodeGeneratorRequest` from stdin, writes `CodeGeneratorResponse` to stdout. Reference shape: vtproto's `cmd/protoc-gen-go-vtproto/main.go`.
close_reason: Closed

title: BUF-EPIC: Add buf-generate plugin support
description:
**Source:** BUF.md, umbrella tracker

What it would take to make wiresmith usable from `buf generate` and (optionally) publishable to the buf.build plugin registry. Today wiresmith is invoked as a standalone CLI (`cmd/wiresmith/main.go`) that parses `.proto` files directly via `bufbuild/protocompile` — buf integration is a separate entry path, not a rewrite.

Layers are concentric: the plugin protocol is the minimum, `buf generate` integration makes it useful, and the registry layer is only needed for remote publishing.

## Suggested order

1. Items 1.1–1.6 plus the doc snippet from 2.3 — minimum viable plugin.
2. WKT support (2.2) — biggest real-world unblocker; without it most buf modules won't compile their generated output.
3. `paths=source_relative` (2.1) — quality of life for buf-default users.
4. Registry layer (3.x) — only if/when remote publishing becomes a goal.

The disk-writing refactor in 1.3 is also a natural win for testing: `TestGenerateMatchesCheckedIn` could verify response contents in memory instead of round-tripping through `gen/`.

**Note:** Sub-items are tracked separately as BUF-1.1 through BUF-3.3.
